03_TSK_Valera_Favor
Guid
1589572e-87ff-49e9-891e-b29e8f12ef03
A Bigger Fish
is a
quest
in
Pillars of Eternity II: Deadfire
.
Synopsis
Martino wants a group of Royal Deadfire Company sailors ousted from the Wild Mare in Queen's Berth. He seemed to favor a permanent solution.
Walkthrough
Head to the Valera estate and talk to
Atello Valera
.
Martino Valera
will require a token of your loyalty: Get rid of the RDC sailors at
The Wild Mare
, who are boasting of their achievements in hunting pirates across the Deadfire.
Head to the Wild Mare, dropping by
Zili Valera
if you want a peaceful solution. The soldiers are drinking on the upper floor balcony. Talk to
Shrimp
, who will recognize the Watcher from
Defiance Bay
. Then you can politely ask them to clear out. You can use
Bluff
4 to make the crew leave, tell them to swing by Zili Valera, or intimidate/attack them. Martino prefers bloodshed, but the RDC would rather you didn't kill their sailors.
If
A Sinking Feeling
is active and you speak to Zili about Persa first, dialog will be locked and Zili will not be available to distract the sailors until A Sinking Feeling is complete.
If
Maia
is in your party, the sailors will recognize her, and you can ask her to scare them off to resolve the situation without bloodshed.
Return to Martino. Once you hand the quest in, you have a few days before the heist happens, giving you the ability to finish
Family Pride
.
Shrimp
carries a
Ring of the Marksman
, which can be looted if you kill the sailors or pick-pocketed with a Sleight of Hand skill of 7 or above.
Journal
Quests/03_Neketaka_Vailian_District/03_TSK_Valera_Favor.quest

A Cure for Rage
is a
quest
in
Avowed
.
Synopsis
An Aedyran caravan entered Galawain's Tusks bound for Solace Keep, but never reached its destination.
Walkthrough
This article or section is a
stub
. You can help the
Pillars of Eternity Wiki
by

A Cure for Rage

An Aedyran caravan entered Galawain's Tusks bound for Solace Keep, but never reached its destination.

4
Locate the Missing Aedyran Caravan
10004
Lerae mentioned an Aedyran caravan traveled north to Solace a while ago and has yet to return. She fears the worst and asks that you find evidence of its whereabouts.
20005
Lerae also mentioned a dwarven merchant in the caravan that she seems to have taken a liking to. Her name is Gilyn. Be on the lookout for her.
53
Acquire Anti-Mycotic Moss
10053
This moss is toxic to spores and other fungi. It grows among the roots of trees with red leaves, which grow on the tops of large rocks.
20008
This green moss is said to grow in the southern Sallow Steppe area. It prefers higher altitudes and grows near the large red trees that inhabit that area.
54
Acquire a Calcified Beetle Nest Nodule
10054
Older parts of fire beetle nests can harden by absorbing some of the minerals in the rocks around them. You will need a nodule of such material to make the cure.
20009
A large fire beetle nest can be found southwest of the Warden Tower. Look for reddish brown nodules at the base of the sickly green nest walls.
55
Acquire a Jar of Gunpowder Snuff
10055
Inhaled through the nose, this potent narcotic produces an intense surge of focus and euphoria.

A Dance with Death
Details
Type
Faction quest
Level
15
Quest region
Deadfire - Northwest
Quest giver
Captain Aeldys
Captain Furrante
Companion
Serafen
Faction
Príncipi sen Patrena
Experience gained
Total XP
6,075
XP type
Normal
XP level
15
Outcomes and rewards
Claim the Floating Hangman
Related quests
Previous:
Honor Among Thieves
Next:
-
Technical
Quest ID
FCT_QST_Principi_Ghost_Ship
Guid
70525acb-34f6-4a38-97f6-792591118dfe
A Dance with Death
is a
faction quest
in
Pillars of Eternity II: Deadfire
.
Synopsis
The only way to Ukaizo for the Príncipi starts by gaining command of
Lucia Rivan
's Floating Hangman. With the
Engoliero do Espirs
the Watcher is supposed to sail to sea and draw the undead Darcozzi paladin.
Lucia Rivan became a Death Guard because of her undying devotion to an oath she took to protect the Engoliero do Espirs. Aeldys discovered that speaking the dying oath of the Darcozzi while wielding the sword activates an aura which acts as a beacon for Rivan.
Walkthrough
Talk to whoever won the power struggle to become pirate leader in
Honor Among Thieves
. Your job is now to steal a ghost ship - the
Floating Hangman
, more properly named the
Fonferrus
- in order to be able to sail it to
Ukaizo
.
The only way to draw Rivan out into the open is by speaking the Darcozzi oath before her old sword,
Engoliero do Espirs
. The sword is currently guarded by
Yseyr the Berathian
in
The Hanging Sepulchers
beneath the Sacred Stair in Neketaka. To take it peacefully, you need to be a death godlike, have Perception 20, Religion 8 or you can simply pick pocket it from him, even if he is encountered earlier/before the quest (it will be a Quest item only at that point).
Sail away from the city, stand on your ship's deck, right-click on the
Engoliero do Espirs
quest item, and select "Speak the Oath". You are promptly boarded by
Lucia Rivan
and her undead horde. Unless you are able to pass a
Diplomacy
+17,
Bluff
+16 or
Metaphysics
+15 check, you'll have to smash her and her crew to bits to seize her ship. (If you just hand over the sword, the quest fails unceremoniously.)
Note:
If you plan to do things peacefully with Lucia Rivan, change your ship with the Dyrwoodan Sloop before you do the oath. She'll be pissed with you, but she'll take the sloop anyway for the sake of getting back her sword. You will get the Floating Hangman in the end, and you'll be satisfied in doing so.
Return to Dunnage to speak with the pirate leader to complete the quest and seal your alliance with the pirates. This will cause all other faction alliance quests to fail.
Alternatively, you can also just keep your spooky new ship (it's a
galleon
in terms of mechanics) and sail off, either to look for other allies, or to Ukaizo on your own. This will not align you with the Príncipi for the final confrontation at Ukaizo.
You can also kill whomever you left in charge of the council instead of turning in the quest, if you wish to keep the Hangman but aren't keen on having these particular pirates roaming the Deadfire. This will, of course, result in A Dance with Death failing, but you're left with a new ship and free to seek out other allies.
(Attack via tool bar.)
Announcing your recently acquired captaincy of the
Fonferrus
will update the quest, having the Príncipi awaiting your signal to take their fleet into Ondra's Mortar.
The Coming Storm
will be completed, and you will start
The City Lost to Time
.
Note: unlike other faction quests, you wont get achievement for completion of principi quest line untill after reaching ukaizo
Consequences
Completing this quest will align you with the Príncipi for the final confrontation at
Ukaizo
.
After you meet with the Príncipi leader the last time,
Pallegina
will leave via a
letter
.
Speaking with
Maia Rua
after completing this quest will result in her leaving your company.
There is no way of permanently retaining Maia, however, if you are in a relationship with her, you can convince her to stay (temporarily) by telling her "Stay with me.
The Defiant
needs you and I do as well."
If you are successful and convince her to stay, Maia will leave the navy in her end-game slides.
Journal

End states

A Distant Light

Strand of Favor

Effigy's Resentment
*
Related quests
Previous:
The Veins of Eora

He Waits in Fire
From the Wreckage
Technical
Quest ID
11_QST_Castles_In_The_Sand
Guid
8ce05a89-5396-4132-a147-7a5aa4df55c2
A Distant Light
is a
main quest
in
Pillars of Eternity II: Deadfire
.
Synopsis
Eothas has been spotted near Hasongo, an island with a Rauataian outpost and an adra lighthouse. Hasongo can be found to the west of Neketaka.
Walkthrough
To complete this quest, you need to head to
Hasongo
and make your way through the fort to the lighthouse, in order to commune with Eothas again. The adra vein in the lighthouse has gone dark - a sure sign that Eothas drained it, however a nāga named
Sugaan
bars the way.
Sugaan is a treasure trove of information on the past, describing the extinction event that
Ondra
perpetrated on the archipelago. Unlike most kith, the naga have preserved knowledge of the past to a far greater degree than the
Huana
, and Sugaan believes that history will repeat itself.
To convince him to let you approach the pillar to restore it, either:
Say
"Eothas came to reach the adra, not to punish anyone."
or
"I'm a Watcher. I can restore the adra if you'll just let me near it."
then use
Metaphysics
6.
Find
Yanass
in the
cannon rampart
and bring her with you. She will convince him on your behalf. If you haven't found Yanass yet, you can still leave and come back later. Sugaan won't attack you if you leave.
Ask with steel.
Upon interacting with the pillar, you will commune with Eothas, who will commend you on your efforts and shed a little more light on his plans (if you reach
Ashen Maw
before interacting with the pillar, the conversation with Eothas will be skipped). He will also explain the Watcher's unique position, in knowing the truth about the gods, despite the interference of the Leaden Key and the Hand Occult. With
Metaphysics
4 and
Intellect
16, you can identify the nature of the soul link, and Eothas will explain that by severing the tether at Maje Island - due to you contacting him - he had to replenish his soul reserves at Hasongo, rather than continuing on to his destination. Before breaking off, he will also return a portion of your soul to you.
If you sacrificed one of your companions to Skaen's
Blood Pool
during the events of
Pillars of Eternity
, the part of your soul that housed this memory will be returned to you, and you will be given the
same
permanent boon, granting you a unique bonus depending on the companion sacrificed. Some of these effects differ to account for new mechanics in Deadfire, see
Permanent effects
for more information.
If you absorbed the souls in the quest
Undying Heritage
during the events of
Pillars of Eternity
, you will be re-granted
Gift from the Machine
, giving you +1
Might
and +5% Max Health.
If you did neither of these things, you will not be granted a bonus.
If Sugaan is alive, you can speak to him and can suggest a diplomatic solution.
Maia
will insist on not letting him walk away, not after the number of dead Rauataians left behind by the nāga. You can pacify her, and she will
dutifully
comply. Sugaan will also thank you for showing him two new things: Restoring the adra and mercy on part of kith. He gives you the unique amulet
Strand of Favor
.
Next stop,
Ashen Maw
. As you leave Hasongo, you will be greeted by an RDC fleet with Grand Secretary Atsura, who will congratulate you and recommend heading to Neketaka.
God conversation
Shortly after completion of
A Distant Light
on Hasongo, the Watcher will commune with
Berath
and the other gods regarding
Eothas
' intentions. This interaction occurs as a random event while sailing, sometimes immediately after leaving the port.
The gods comment on a number of your actions in
Pillars of Eternity
, including:
Berath
: The outcome of
A Servant of Death
, whether you killed
Rehstin
and
Erona
to end their avoiding The Wheel (see
Histories
).
Wael
: The outcome of
The Parable of Wael
, whether you returned the scroll to
Grimda
or lost it for Wael (see
Histories
). If you returned the scroll, Wael will
destroy all scrolls
in your stash as punishment.
Your choices of dialogue during this conversation will determine which upgrade to
Visage of Death's Herald
(or its upgrades) the Watcher receives. It is also dependent on the last upgrade to this ability you received during the god interaction following
Stranded
on
Port Maje Island
. The final upgrade occurs after
He Waits in Fire
.
A score is tallied representing the amount of times the Watcher made a response of a particular Disposition. Each disposition is associated with an upgrade to
Visage of Death's Herald
(and its upgrades). You must choose at least 2 of the dialogue options for the associated ability, and your final "score" must be greater than that of the other two scores, with exceptions - see below.
Each upgrade adds an
Inspiration
which is granted to the Watcher when using the ability. Diplomatic responses give a Perception Inspiration, Sassy gives Intellect, and Aggressive gives Might. If the ability already grants that Inspiration type, the Inspiration is upgraded to the next tier. For example,
Insightful
(Tier 1) would become
Aware
(Tier 2).

If you have 2 or more Diplomatic responses, and have more Diplomatic responses than (or equal to) the amount of Aggressive or Sassy responses:
If you don't have
Mien
,
Wit
or
Ire
, you gain
Mien of Death's Herald
(upgraded from
Visage of Death's Herald
).
If you already have
Mien of Death's Herald
, it is upgraded to grant Aware (Tier 2 Perception Inspiration)
If you already have
Wit of Death's Herald
, this is upgraded to
Raiment of Death's Herald
If you already have
Ire of Death's Herald
, this is upgraded to
Awe of Death's Herald
If you have 2 or more Sassy responses, have more Sassy than Diplomatic responses, and have more (or equal to) Sassy than Aggressive responses.
If you don't have
Mien
,
Wit
or
Ire
, you gain
Wit of Death's Herald
(upgraded from
Visage of Death's Herald
).
If you already have
Mien of Death's Herald
, this is upgraded to
Raiment of Death's Herald
.
If you already have
Wit of Death's Herald
,  it is upgraded to grant Acute (Tier 2 Intellect Inspiration)
If you already have
Ire of Death's Herald
, this is upgraded to
Cruelty of Death's Herald
.
If you have 2 or more Aggressive responses, and have more Aggressive responses than Diplomatic and Sassy responses:
If you don't have
Mien
,
Wit
or
Ire
, you gain
Ire of Death's Herald
(upgraded from
Visage of Death's Herald
).
If you already have
Mien of Death's Herald
, this is upgraded to
Awe of Death's Herald
.
If you already have
Wit of Death's Herald
, this is upgraded to
Cruelty of Death's Herald
.
If you already have
Ire of Death's Herald
,  it is upgraded to grant Tenacious (Tier 2 Might Inspiration)

Technical
Quest ID
prot2_rumbald
A Farmer's Plight
is a
side quest
in
Pillars of Eternity
.
Synopsis
Rumbald
in
Dyrford Village
has recently lost his herd of pigs to an
ogre
. He asks you to kill it.
Walkthrough
Go to the cave in the east part of
Dyrford Crossing
. The
ogre
Korgrak
is very temperamental, and many dialogue options will result in him attacking you. In order to resolve the conflict peacefully, you must ask him why he's living in the cave, then tell him it's dangerous, and that even if he kills you, more attention will be brought on the ogre. You can convince him to leave (
Edér
will mention
Twin Elms
), advise that he lie low for a while, suggest killing
Rumbald
, or invite him back to your
stronghold
. This will unlock him as a
hireling
.
Rumbald
will not be happy if you deal with the ogre peacefully, only giving you a
Piglet
for your trouble. If you reimburse him for the cost of his herd (400
), he will be much happier, and give you his unique
blunderbuss
Lead Spitter
. On the other other hand, if you sent
Korgrak
after the farmer, his wife takes his place in front of their home, and gives you a piglet instead.
If you do get in a fight with
Korgrak
, be very careful about who goes into melee; the ogre does massive amounts of damage. Try taking advantage of his low reflex and will defenses to incapacitate him, or charm/dominate some of his
bears
to take the blows for you. It is also possible to use pull and run strategy, killing bears at first and then
Korgrak
.
Interaction with
Korgrak
:
He is extremely easy to aggravate.
You may ask about lost elf noblewoman.
You may ask about the pigs.
You may ask about the piles of bones.
Ask him what he's doing in the cave:
Perception
16 - Notice he's favoring one leg - he becomes hostile
Survival
9 - Mention that he already ate whole herd of pigs, and won't be able to hide for long.
Intellect
18 - Say that he'd only hole up in a spider infested cave if he feared something.
Being
Rational
- allows you to parley and guide him or recruit him.
Otherwise there will be blood...
Bugs
Korgrak
does not disappear from the Cave after recruiting him.
It is possible to kill friendly
Korgrak
and still possible to have his "double" at
Caed Nua
.
You may therefore still gain the best reward from
Rumbald
(for killing the ogre) despite recruiting
Korgrak

A Glimpse Beyond

Complete the quest

Skipping Ahead
Technical
Quest ID
07_TSK_Glimpse_Beyond
Guid
a1d760f7-59d9-493d-806c-45b8a5dbbb1b
A Glimpse Beyond
is a
faction quest
in
Pillars of Eternity II: Deadfire
.
Synopsis
The animancers at the Sacred Stair have been using luminous adra in a variety of strange experiments. They're in need of volunteers. Flaune claims that her group of researchers have devised a way to channel my ability to navigate the In-Between to... well, launch people from one adra pillar to another. They'd like the Watcher to participate in the experiments.
Walkthrough
This is a straightforward quest that can be started either by talking to
Flaune Elette
or being sent to her by
Director Castol
. Talk to her and volunteer (you get a special reaction with a Scientist background). You can also confirm her theory with
Intellect
12. Flaune will also mention that it's possible for your party to come along by using the In-Between - something you will use later too.
Use the machine and focus on the connection point. Instead of landing at the designated farm, however, you'll land in
Rymrgand
's realm:
Realm of the Beast of Winter
.
The road is linear and you will run into a variety of
Scourging Blights
on the way. The route ends when you come across the
Soul Collector
, a high level monster. He's slow, so deal with the blights first and then attack it. It responds well to surrounding and debuffs.
After you deal with it, approach the giant mask and meet the master of this realm, Rymrgand. He will threaten you with annihilation and demand that you inform your animancer friends that they meddle in things beyond their ken - and demand that you inform them to stay away.
Do note that there's no bonus points for being clever here - if you invite Rymyrgand to attempt to destroy you, he heartily will, without hesitation - so you're best off being agreeable and avoiding the slog of redoing the entire bossfight due to an entirely scripted death event.
Once you return, it will turn out that your body actually remained back at the Spire, while your
mind
ventured forward. Faune will apologize, pay you
400
, and ask you to report to the VTC HQ while they recalibrate their gear.
Journal

Quest ID
FCT_QST_RDC02_Smugglers
Guid
8a1c2a0e-d242-4b6d-89be-6267e0d1c9d3
A Matter of Import
is a
faction quest
in
Pillars of Eternity II: Deadfire
.
Synopsis
Hazanui Karū asks the Watcher to pick up an important shipment and deliver it to the island of Sayuka.
Walkthrough
Head over to
Imperial Command
and talk to the hazanui. As it turns out, the RDC is shorthanded due to the loss of
Hasongo
and storms at home delaying the fleet. She will muse on the weather engineering uncovered at Poko Kohara, then ask you to meet
Widla
, a smuggler, at sea to deliver a special cargo to one of the greatest minds of Rauatai at
Sayuka
. Hazanui Karū hands you
1,000
, as the already negotiated payment for the cargo, and sends you on your way.
Head west of Neketaka.
Widla
and her dhow,
The Goshawk
, will automatically seek you out and make contact. Widla will demand a higher price than agreed for the crates, unless you have at least Good 2 reputation with the Príncipi.
Without the necessary reputation, starting dialogue with the Insight option, mentioning that the "Royal Deadfire Company has a long memory", then exhausting all non-hostile dialogue will open dialogue with
Perin
; a Bluff 6 check will cause Widla to send Perin below deck, removing him if combat breaks out, or a Diplomacy 8 Bluff 6 check will cause Perin to mutiny, sending Widla below deck. Perin will offer the cargo for 1000, otherwise combat is easier as Widla is no longer present.
If you chose to attack the crew, interact with the crates on deck to grab them and sail away.
Head for Sayuka. Once you make way, you will discover the crates contain a very special kind of cargo:
Iverra
, a renowned Vailian scientist. You can talk to her to learn that she deliberately fled the Republics in a crate to test her air-recycling spell and to reach
Sayuka
, in order to help with the excavations there.
Upon safe delivery of the "cargo" to Sayuka, head to
Fleet Master Okaya
in her quarters to complete the quest, netting you a measly
350
and Moderate Positive reputation with the RDC.
If you attacked or were attacked by the smugglers earlier, you can mention this to the Fleet Master at this point, and she will compensate you in the form of an extra
800
on top of the usual
350
.
Gallery
Loading the crates

The Gilded Vale
Technical
Quest ID
cp_qst_getting_water
A Moment's Respite
is a
main quest
in
Pillars of Eternity
. It starts after talking to
Odema
at the
encampment
.
Synopsis
Your character fell sick and there is an ominous feeling in the air. Odema, the caravan leader, will tell you that he fears a soulstorm is on the way, and that it is best to find a cure for your sickness. He sends you off to harvest some local herbs that will stop your fever. You are accompanied by
Calisca
, a female
fighter
.
Exposition
The following is shown after starting a new game, providing some exposition along with a narration and a graphic of the caravans travelling through the Dyrwood:
https://pillarsofeternity.fandom.com/wiki/File:07_cv_prologue.ogg
Narration by Dave B. Mitchell
Five wagons grope blindly for the path on a starless night, their master glancing ever upward to the skies for assurance that he is on the right course, a dim lantern his only protection against the encroaching darkness.
But the skies bring no comfort, shining no light, betraying no hint of what they know.
The caravan carries travelers bound for the frontier hamlet of Gilded Vale, you among them, where a local lord has offered land and wealth to settlers from abroad looking for a fresh start.
You have taken suddenly ill, sweating and shivering, and one of the other travelers signals for the caravan master to stop on your behalf. He pulls up just in time to avoid plowing into the trunk of a fallen tree that bars the way ahead. You will go no further tonight.
Walkthrough
Talk to
Odema
to get your bearings and learn about a remedy for the "Rumbling Rot". He will ask
Calisca
to help you find the springberries. These are located to the northwest of the camp, defended by a young wolf.
Calisca will talk to you about your past, giving you the opportunity to continue with a little bit of character development and exploration. Then she will suggest finding
Sparfel
and giving him a kick in the rear, to get him to fetch water to the camp and concoct you that springberry tea.
You can find him - or rather, the
waterskin
he left behind - on the other side of the river and the stone bridge. Collecting it will trigger a fight sequence with a few
Glanfathan
tribesmen, who just shot Sparfel. After you defeated them, return to the camp.
Glanfathans attacked the encampment and slaughtered all but one:
Heodan
. He is alive and was taken captive. The Glanfathan leader will then  initiate dialogue and demand that you drop your weapons and threaten to kill Heodan. At this point:
If you select 1
Lore
option (asking if the Glanfathans worship Galawain) or have 2
Athletics
and choose the Athletics option, you will begin combat with Heodan uninjured.
Any other option (including dropping your weapons, which will remove the weapon from Calisca and your current weapon slot) will result in Heodan receiving an injury prior to the fight (-20
Fortitude
-2
Constitution
), rather than killing him.
After the fight, a scripted encounter will begin where Heodan is grabbed by an attacker while trying to escape. The first option is to throw or fire your weapon at the attacker; if you had a melee weapon equipped, it will be dropped. The second option, only available with
Dexterity
15, is to throw a rock at the attacker. The third option is to do nothing. The first two options will free Heodan and allow him to escape. The third will result in his death.
Killing the caravan
Instead of completing the quest objectives given by Odema and Calisca, you also have the option of slaughtering the caravan. This can be very difficult for some classes, especially on
Hard
or above. Make sure you have picked up all the items from the chests in the area, including if possible
The Disappointer
, which you can sell to Heodan and use to buy potions and equipment. Collecting the berries first will cause
Sparfel
to disappear and remove one of your tougher enemies, as well as allowing you to flesh out your biography in the discussion with Calisca.
When you're ready, put some distance between you and Calisca and order her to attack one of the caravan members. She will turn hostile to you as soon as the fight begins, but as long as she is closer to the caravan than to you she will be tied down with fighting the other caravan members. Leave her to kill as many of them as she can before she dies while you pick off enemies at the rear. Most of the caravan will go down in a hit or two, but
Heodan
and especially
Odema
are more difficult—be prepared to heal a lot.
As soon as everyone's dead, a cutscene will begin and the quest will end.
Gallery
The caravans travelling the road at night

Quest ID
07_qst_mothers_plea
A Mother's Plea
is a
side quest
in
Pillars of Eternity
.
Synopsis
Aufra
is worried about her unborn baby. She asks you to venture to
Anslög's Compass
to find someone who can prevent her baby from becoming a
Hollowborn
.
Walkthrough
Once you arrive in
Gilded Vale
, go to the first house,
Aufra's House
, on the left-hand side and enter it.
Speak to the woman,
Aufra
, and offer to help her and she'll give you some gold.
Leave the house and travel south to
Magran's Fork
and south again to
Anslög's Compass
.
Once in
Anslög's Compass
head south-west eliminating any
Xaurip
you see.
When you reach the ocean go east and speak with
Ranga
and ask for the potion.
She'll ask you to kill all the
Xaurip
in
Anslög's Compass
. Aside from the
Xaurip
you would've encountered by walking towards
Ranga
, there is also a
Xaurip
encampment in the north-east.
After eliminating the
Xaurip
return to
Ranga
and she'll send you on another errand to the cave slightly above her.
Enter the cave and kill at least one
Sporeling
, and pick up the spores it drops.
Return to
Ranga
and get the potion. She'll tell you the potion doesn't actually do anything, but thinks that it can help Aufra via the
placebo effect
.
Return to
Aufra
and give her the potion. You have the option to tell her the truth or not about the potion, and then to offer further advice.
Reward
Depending on your answers, you will get different levels of
reputation
and
disposition
:
Choice
Result
If you
don't
warn Aufra about the potion and leave immediately:
Major Good reputation (Gilded Vale)
+1 Stoic disposition.
If you
don't
warn Aufra about the potion and say
"Glad to be of assistance"
:
Major Good reputation (Gilded Vale)
+1 Deceptive disposition
If you warn Aufra about the potion, and then change your mind telling her that the potion will make her tired:
Major Good reputation (Gilded Vale)
+1 Deceptive disposition
If you warn Aufra about the potion, tell her the truth + she will pray to the gods:
Moderate Good reputation (Gilded Vale)
+1 Honest disposition
If you warn Aufra about the potion, tell her the truth + demand your money:
Minor Good reputation (Gilded Vale)
+1 Honest disposition
If you warn Aufra about the potion, tell her the truth + any other answer:
Moderate Good reputation (Gilded Vale)
+1 Honest disposition
If you tell Aufra the truth
Receive
10
and a
Minor Cloak of Protection
, regardless of what follow up dialogue you select
If you are deceptive, she will only give you the
10
.
Notes
Completing the quest will result in a
Letter from Aufra
being delivered to
The Defiant
in
Pillars of Eternity II: Deadfire
, revealing that she had a son and the little boy is healthy and fine, thanks to you. The tone of the letter is slightly different, depending whether you told her the truth or not.

A Paradise of the Mind
Details
Type
Side quest
Level
15

Copperhead
NPCs involved

Quartermaster Harka
Menzzago

Splintered Reef
The Pickled Eel
Boathouse
Sea-Lashed Crypt
Glowing Outcrop
Experience gained
Total XP
3,375
XP type
Normal
XP level
15
Outcomes and rewards
Kill Menzzago
Amaliorra
Spider Silk Robe
Copperhead's Helm
Copperhead
as crew member
"Paradise of the Mind"
achievement
Make a deal with Menzzago
"Paradise of the Mind"
achievement
Promise of
Menzzago
's aid
Technical
Quest ID
20_qst_Splintered_Reef_Main
Guid
6d0c7bf7-fe19-4a04-ba51-a6948572e2f8
A Paradise of the Mind
is a
side quest
in
Pillars of Eternity II: Deadfire
.
Synopsis
While exploring Splintered Reef, I came across a tavern filled with vessels, going about their lives as if nothing were amiss. Inside I met a Dargul by the name of Copperhead. He claims there's something strange going on. Judging by the fact that no one seems to have noticed that they are rotting corpses consuming the flesh of living kith, he's probably right.
Overview
Journey to
Splintered Reef
and talk to
Copperhead
in
The Pickled Eel
.
Leave the tavern by killing
Zulka
and crew
OR
avoid her and sneak out.
Talk to Copperhead in the
Boathouse
.
Head to the
Sea-Lashed Crypt
, and unlock the door to the
Glowing Outcrop
by obtaining two
Vial of Fampyr Blood
.
Confront
Menzzago
and either
Kill him and shatter the orb to break the illusion and free the townspeople from their twisted fantasy.
Come to a mutual agreement by offering him luminous adra potions to sustain his need for life essence, in exchange for a promise of the aid of his fleet - should you require it. This causes the attacks on kith to stop, but the undead remain enslaved and under his control.
Walkthrough
Head to
The Pickled Eel
and talk to
Copperhead
, a copper helmet-wearing dargul. He's thankful that someone has turned up to assist with the strange happenings in the reef, and will ask you to meet him at the
boathouse
to discuss the situation.
Before he leaves, Copperhead warns you about the guls with the staves, known as "protectors". Just as he says that,
Zulka
and her gang of unfriendly dargul's enter the tavern with a pair of captive kith. You'll have to either fight through them, or sneak past in order to leave.
If you choose to fight, Zulka will command all of the tavern patrons to attack you - but luckily a few well-placed explosive barrels make the job easier.
Note:
Note that should you kill the
Tavernkeeper
, you will no longer be able to purchase the
Grave Hound Pup
from him, so it is recommended that you purchase it
before
talking to Copperhead.
Once they are dead, the captives in the back room can be freed. You can examine the iron collar on them using
Arcana
15 to determine that it possess a similar incantation to the marks on the foreheads of the undead, which makes them appear as livestock instead of captive kith. Doing this, or talking to
Copperhead
in the boatshed allows you to tell them what is going on. One of the captives mentions that they were sailing under
Captain Teryc Lofell
of the
Antelope
(a
Príncipi
ship), and that they were took captive by the crew of
The Floating Hangman
. Freeing them is the objective of the quest
The Final Voyage
.
Alternatively, sneaking by is relatively easy, none of the townsfolk are hostile and Zulka wanders back and forth, creating space for a swift and stealthy exit. She will remain at the tavern should you return during the quest, and will become hostile as soon as you enter.
Head to the boathouse on the other side of the reef. Copperhead will reveal that the entire town is living under some sort of illusion, generated from the keep at the heart of the island, which used to belong to
Lucia Rivan
. He wants to free the townsfolk from their twisted fantasy, and asks you to investigate.
You can ask him to let you look at his helmet, which is supposedly shielding him from the effects of the illusion. Copperhead is very reluctant, as removing it will put him back "into the dream". With
Arcana
18, you confirm that the helmet has a repulsing effect on magic just by looking at it. Though if you force it from him with
Might
15, he is reduced to a slack-jawed puppet as soon as it leaves his head. You gain
Copperhead's Helm
, and are able to more closely examine it. Returning the helmet will restore his consciousness, but due to a seemingly broken dialogue tree, you are unable to actually tell him about the helmet: "I think your helmet dampens magical effects. It's shielding you from whatever spell is being cast on everyone else."
He'll also offer to create a distraction, as the guards rarely leave their posts for anything but fresh meat.
The crypt
You'll need to reach
Menzzago
on the
Glowing Outcrop
. Head to the
Sea-Lashed Crypt
and deal with the guards. If you took Copperhead up on his offer of a distraction, most of the
Risen Armsman
will be lured away from the exterior section of the crypt. Even so, the fight is managable as long as you don't aggro all the enemies at once. Again, you can use the explosive barrels and amphora that are dotted around to your advantage. The wooden gangplank serves as an excellent choke point to funnel incoming enemies.
Enter the crypt proper via the entrance to the north, then proceed through to the great doors in the center of the area which lead to the
Glowing Outcrop
. In order to open them you'll need to pour a
Vial of Fampyr Blood
into each font. These are dropped by
Runemaster Kastor
in the lab to the east, and
Quartermaster Harka
to the northwest.
Confronting Menzzago
Once you've obtained the vials and unlocked the door to the Glowing Outcrop, Menzzago awaits on the other side. He explains that the glowing sphere of energy serves as a channeling point for the powerful spell cast upon the reef and the townsfolk, a spell which - as expected - provides a means to make them unaware of their own fate. You can kill him outright or try to negotiate a peaceful conclusion.
To get the only peaceful option, you must tell him that you could use an army of undead. Making a deal involves offering him a supply of luminous adra potions to replace their regular meals of live kith (as a source of living essence). In return, Menzzago will become your ally, and his fleet of ships will be at your disposal. This will put a stop to the fampyr's need to consume live kith, but will not break the illusion. To start, you'll need a party-assisted
Diplomacy
or
Survival
8 in order to make your case (proclaiming yourself as Berath's Herald, passing the
Insight
8 check, or offering your services as a
Goldpact Knights
paladin has no effect). Next, you'll need one of the following:
If you have discovered Ukaizo, you can leverage your current alliance with a faction (Huana, VTC, RDC, Príncipi), to convince him that the spoils of adra there will be shared with him, much to the chagrin of the respective factions' companions. Failing to pass one of the checks below will cause him to become hostile. If you suggest you'll ally with
him
to take Uzaizo, this is a free pass. If you instead say you'll take Ukaizo yourself, he becomes hostile. If you attempt to persuade him that your allied faction will share, this requires one of the following:
"I'll talk them into it"
, requires a party-assisted 12
Diplomacy
.
"They wouldn't dare refuse."
, requires
Resolve
18.
You can also offer to negotiate a supply of luminous adra potions from the
Vailian Trading Company
. This requires ALL of the following:
Allied with the
Vailian Trading Company
for
The Coming Storm
or have at least 3 reputation with them, and have a party-assisted 16
Diplomacy
.
Restored the adra in
Hasongo
OR
Poko Kohara
.
Have not yet discovered
Ukaizo
.
He will eventually demand a token of good faith, in the form of a
Luminous Adra Potion
. Giving him a sample will complete the quest, and not providing it will cause him to attack. You can also choose to poison him instead (see below). This requirement is exclusive to the VTC option, choosing either the Ukaizo option (above) or Crookspur option (below) do not require that you provide a sample of luminous adra.
If you have allied with
Crookspur Slavers
, you can say you'll demand they send regular shipments of luminous adra. This requires that you've completed
Tip of the Spear
, that
Master Kua
is still alive, and that Crookspur is still owned by the slavers.
Otherwise, you can choose to attack and break the orb in order to free the townsfolk.
If the you possess a
Scorching Venom
and
Bluff
8, you can poison Menzzago instead of a giving him a Luminous Adra Potion (you can pick this option alongside the Diplomacy/Survival check above, or after picking the VTC dialogue). This will start combat, and Menzzago will begin the fight with the
Sickened
affliction.
For those experiencing difficulty with the fight, since it has many difficult opponents, one can enter stealth with their party (even with low skill) and approach from the east, staying tight against the wall. When the conversation begins, the party can feasibly be directly beside Menzzago. This gives an opportunity to face him directly, divides the Fampyr's to each side of him, and avoids having to fight the Algul's that come up the south wall. As long as you keep the fight above the steps, the algul's shouldn't join the fight.
Once Menzzago is dead, make sure to loot his body for
Amaliorra
,
Spider Silk Robe
, and
Menzzago's Grimoire
. Shatter the orb in order to stop the illusion, turning the undead of Splintered Reef into mindless husks again.
Quest completion
Either way, return to Copperhead after smashing the orb, as he asked. Or bask in the glory of having made a pact with the devil. Completing the quest grants you the
achievement
, "Paradise of the Mind".
If you smashed the orb:
Copperhead is thankful. He's able to remove the helmet without fear of losing his self-control. While he's had a long time to come to peace with what he is, Copperhead wonders what it would be like if the other townsfolk would've had the same chance. As a token of gratitude, he gives you his helmet
Copperhead's Helm
. You can choose to accept it or not, and ask him to join your crew.
Returning to the tavern,
Zulka
is no longer present, but the townspeople have all reverted to their prior state - and are hostile towards you like any other guls. You can also free the captives in the back room if you haven't already, as they aren't a part of the quest itself - though the dialogue is centered around information that has already been established.
If you made a deal with Menzzago:
Copperhead is frustrated that the illusion still stands, and that the "people" of Splintered Reef are still under control. You can try to bring him some consolation by telling him that he no longer needs to eat kith to survive, but his spirits and his hope of bringing freedom to the area are dashed.
You gain Menzzago's promise of the aid of his fleet, should you require it, and Lifeless ships will accompany you to Ukaizo. You'll have the chance to deploy them during the fleet engagement, potentially eliminating a key enemy ship, and the undead may come aboard to assist you during deck battles.
Zulka
is no longer present at The Pickled Eel, and you may come and go freely.
If you killed Menzzago but didn't smash the orb:
Copperhead is puzzled that you chose not to dispel the illusion. He reminds you that kith are still being fed to the undead, and worries about what will happen if Luca Rivan returns. He gives you a
Corroded Bronze Horn Figurine
.
Zulka
is no longer present at The Pickled Eel, and you may come and go freely. You can opt to free the captive kith held there, if you haven't already.

A Path to the Garden
Details
Type
Main quest
Difficulty
10
Experience gained
XP type
Normal
XP level
10
XP weight
4
Related quests
Previous:
Ancient Soil
Next:
Face Your Fears
Technical
Quest ID
0401_cq_critpath1
Guid
0E304432-29A34F43-83DA2B8B-B48DEA45
A Path to the Garden
is a
quest
in
Avowed
.
Synopsis
During the attack on Fior, Inquisitor Lödwyn stole Yatzli's research on the Godless civilization. Yatzli has promised to share what she knows about about the Garden - and Sapadal - if you help her beat the Inquisitor to her destination: a Godless ruin named Naku Kubel in Shatterscarp.
Walkthrough
Head toward the gate to continue on to
Shatterscarp
where you will be waylaid by
Captain Aelfyr
. She will admit the Steel Garrote's plans:
Look, I'm only telling you this because you deserve to know Lödwyn's intentions for the Living Lands. If Hylgard cannot maintain a grip on the Living Lands, then the Steel Garrote will simply take it from him. You see, the Steel Garrote answer to the Burned Queen and not your floundering empire. And we will break from Aedyr if that's what it takes.
You can choose to attack her (or not) before continuing to Shatterscrap. You will be forced to camp and confer with your companions before entering the area.
Optional: Upon entering Shatterscrap, speak with
Captain Wymgar
to learn that
Eagel's Reach
has been recently attacked by Dreamthralls.
Continue on toward
Thirdborn
, where you will find the gates being attacked by dreamthralls. Speak to
Yatzli
and
Temerti
, who will inform you that the way to the godless ruins of
Naku Kubel
are inaccessible due being under the Archmage
Ryngrim
's control. At this point, you can take Yatzli as a companion.
Journal
quests/al04/0401_cq_critpath1

With the events of Fior behind you, you now seek an audience with Yatzli, the orlan wizard and Godless scholar. She asked to meet you in Thirdborn, a settlement in the southern end of Shatterscarp. The two of you intend to explore Naku Kubel, a Godless ruin that might hold the secret to the Garden's location.
20003
You spoke with Yatzli and Temerti, the leader of Thirdborn. Temerti said the notorious archmage Ryngrim has created a magical barrier around the ruins of Naku Kubel, for reasons known only to her. In order to enter the ruins, you and Yatzli will need to speak with Ryngrim, who has taken up residence in the Shark's Teeth area of Shatterscarp.
20000
Captain Aelfyr of the Steel Garrote also gave you a letter to deliver to Temerti, the leader of Thirdborn.
3
Speak with Yatzli
10003
You found your way into Thirdborn. Look for Yatzli and speak with her to plan the next leg of the journey to Naku Kubel.
20000
Captain Aelfyr of the Steel Garrote also gave you a letter to deliver to Temerti, the leader of Thirdborn.
16
[Optional] Learn What Happened in Eagle's Reach
10016
There are many dead and injured soldiers in Eagle's Reach. Investigate the area to learn what happened to them.
20001
Captain Wymgar said the outpost was attacked by Dreamthralls shortly before your arrival. Several Aedyran soldiers are dead, as well as members of the Thirdborn militia who came to their aid.
24
Eliminate the Dreamthralls
10024
Upon reaching the entrance to Thirdborn, you found the settlement's guards under attack by Dreamthralls. Help the guards eliminate the threat.
20002
All the Dreamthralls are dead. It should now be safe to enter Thirdborn.
28
Talk to Aelfyr
10028
Aelfyr has blocked your way to Shatterscarp. You won't be able to proceed until you've spoken with her.
End states
30000
Captain Aelfyr wants to speak to you before you leave Emerald Stair.

Quest ID
15_qst_yenwood_2
A Return to Court
is a
side quest
in
Pillars of Eternity
, added as of patch 3.0.
Synopsis
Another messenger from the ducal palace has arrived in
Caed Nua
.
Chancellor Warrin
said he'd send another messenger if anything new regarding the disputed ownership of Caed Nua came up. The Watcher is invited to attend a meeting at
The Charred Barrel
.
Walkthrough
Some time after you completed
A Call to Court
, you will be notified of another messenger in your
stronghold
. Talk to him in the
Great Hall
. The messenger seems nervous (Perception check), as he tells you to meet
Chancellor Warrin
again, this time in a less official venue.
Go to The
Charred Barrel
in
Brackenbury
and talk to Chancellor Warrin, who is standing by the stage. If asked why he summoned you he will act surprised, saying that he received a missive
from you
asking for a meeting at the Charred Barrel. Suddenly, the entire tavern goes quiet and the patrons disappear through the front door. Moments later, Captain
Emery
enters, flanked by several goons. Turns out the meeting is an ambush set by
Lord Gathbin
. The Chancellor wisely takes cover behind a table.
You can either fight them, or talk your way out of it. If you have  Cruel or Benevolent of 3 or higher, you can sway her to your cause. She will then switch sides and betray Gathbin in
The Battle of Yenwood Field
. A
2,500
bribe can also get her to walk away.
It is worth noting that if you cannot sway Emery, you will have to fight her in battle later anyway, in which case it is easier to deal with her now.
Chancellor Warrin will explain that he has heard of strange reports concerning Lord Gathbin's activities in
The Grasp
, such as hiring a large number of men for an unknown purpose. He recommends caution and keeping a close eye on the keep.
Return to
Caed Nua
where you will find a mob gathered in front of the castle, who claim that your men have been harassing them. Once the matter is cleared up, you can pay them some compensation in return for a stronghold Prestige bonus
Pay them
Prestige bonus
1,000
[Benevolent]
+3
500
[Benevolent]
+2
100
[Benevolent]
+1
Nothing
None
Proceed into the Great Hall and speak to
Marshal Forwyn
. Once the conversation is over, the quest
The Battle of Yenwood Field
starts.

1202a6bd-252e-432d-a1a2-efdb6f70f55d
A Rising Star
is a
task
in
The Forgotten Sanctum
.
Synopsis
Bekarna
might have regained her mind, but she still needs her grimoire returned. Problem is, it was lost somewhere in the Archives section of the facility.
Walkthrough
Bekarna's grimoire
is located in the
Quarantined Section
, on the left side of the southwestern room. A
clue
to its whereabouts is found in
The Central Stacks
, but is not required to complete the quest.
With the depleted grimoire in hand, you'll need to expose it to starlight in order to restore its power. To do so, you can either:
Place the grimoire on the pedestal in
The Oratory of Wael
during nighttime.
Walk outside to the world map during the night, a dialogue will pop up and the grimoire will transform.
Return the completed
Bekarna's Celestial Grimoire
to Bekarna. She will ask for a piece of starmetal next, triggering the final piece of her questline,
Out of Eternal Horizons
.

Technical
Quest ID
13_qst_servant_of_death
A Servant of Death
is a
side quest
in
Pillars of Eternity
.
It is connected to the
Council of Stars
and
Court of the Penitents
main quests.
Synopsis
Berath tires of mortals evading the natural cycle of death and rebirth. They showed the Watcher two individuals in particular - a dwarven man and an elven woman - who have extended their lives for centuries. The message is simple: These two individuals have cheated death for too long. Ending their lives would no doubt please Berath.
Walkthrough
You get this quest from praying to
Berath
during
Council of Stars
. She asks you to assassinate two specific individuals:
Archdruid Rehstin
In the north part of
Elms' Reach
, there's a cave called the
Blood Sands
.  Inside, there's a bunch of dwarves dressed in red calling themselves the
Ethik Nôl
.  Their leader is
Archdruid Rehstin
.
Pull what information you want from him and then kill him (before or after the sacrifice – your choice). Everyone in Blood Sands (except for Keeper Wrdha) will turn hostile if you do this, so be prepared. You will earn Extraordinary
reputation
decrease with
Ethik Nôl
, Major decrease with
The Fangs
and Extraordinary increase with
Ovates
for killing the archdruid. Killing other cult members give major decrease with Ethik Nôl.
High Ovate Erona
Optionally talk to
Iswld
at the
Golden Grove
headquarters in
Elms' Reach
, who will tell you that the High Ovate is on a retreat in Elmshore.
Travel to
Elmshore
, and find
High Ovate Erona
with some of her followers on an island on the far right, middle of the map. This little island can only be reached from its southwest corner. You have a couple of options:
Attack and kill her, resulting in a Major
reputation
decrease with Ovates of the Golden Groves.
If you pass the Resolve check (15
Resolve
) she will accept her errors and she will kill herself without a fight by turning to stone and will earn you a Major
reputation
increase with Ovates of the Golden Groves.
Either will earn a Major
reputation
increase with
Ethik Nôl.
Finally return to Berath at
Teir Evron
. You can basically say whatever you want and the quest will conclude.
Notes
Completing the quest unlocks the ability to hire a
Fampyr
to your stronghold in
The Champion of Berath
quest.
Journal

I think this is the god's way of telling me that these two individuals have cheated death for too long. Ending their lives would no doubt please Berath.

[FS]

Technical
Quest ID
lax03_qst_02_tayn_01
Guid
cdef2d34-03bf-4a3d-bc18-16465994cc57
A Shout in the Dark
is a
quest
in
The Forgotten Sanctum
.
Synopsis
Tayn
wants the Watcher to liberate the captive kith in the Collections wing of the Halls Obscured with a scroll that he created. The scroll will reinsert memories stripped from the kith into their minds. The catch? They will be inserted at random. Tayn dismisses the problem as entertaining, but
Llengrath
is horrified by the idea and would rather the Watcher put the captives out of their misery.
Walkthrough
Important note on Llengrath's scroll:
If you plan on using
Llengrath's scroll
to euthanize the prisoners, be aware that this includes
Bekarna
. If you want her to live and complete her quests, you must first restore her mind by completing
Wasting Minds
, i.e. bringing her the book
Advanced Wistful Stargazing
.
This quest requires the player to reach
The Assembly of Echoes
in the Collections wing first and defeat the
Memory Hoarder
. After it's gone, the player can then use the aperture and choose either Tayn's or Llengrath's resolution:
Reading Tayn's spell will cause the souls to be provided with unpredictable minds and memories. Upon returning to Tayn, he will also reveal that he packed the spell with a little something that will cause the prisoners to be extra talkative, hopefully spilling the Hand Occult's hidden secrets.
Reading Llengrath's spell will euthanize the souls and return them to the Wheel.
It's also possible to collect a special egg from the machine by reaching down into the mouth of the instrument. The egg will hatch into a pet,
Retina
, upon returning to the Temple of Revelation.
Completing the quest increments trust with the chosen archmage and access to the Godseed quest.
Journal

Technical
Quest ID
FCT_QST_Principi_Slavers
Guid
ca999a50-e900-4968-a695-f30c8df8599b
A Shrewd Proposition
is a
faction quest
in
Pillars of Eternity II: Deadfire
.
Synopsis
Captain Furrante has a wealthy contact who has business dealings with the slavers of
Crookspur
. However, recent troubles with the locals have affected their trade routes.
This contact is willing to owe Furrante a political favor for assisting the slavers. However, as slaving is a crime within the Príncipi, Furrante has asked the Watcher to discreetly smooth this problem in his stead.
Walkthrough
Furrante will ask you to travel to
Crookspur
and meet
Master Kua
, the leader of the slavers. As you leave Dunnage, you will receive an alternate missive from Aeldys, asking you to come meet her. She, in turn, wants you to destroy the slaver operation.
After leaving Dunnage, you will recieve a
letter
from
Captain Aeldys
asking you to meet with her at
Fort Deadlight
. You can inform her of Furrante's little side-gig. She knew as much, but if you also figure out that he's working with the VTC (by way of
Director Castol
), you can inform her of that and get 2500 cp. Bringing her the
Candied Nuts
she asked for will net you Minor Positive Reputation gained with Príncipi sen Patrena.
Ultimately, this quest has two separate paths that you can take, the outcomes of which will influence the other concurrent quests. You can either choose to oblige the Crookspur slavers, and wipe out the Wahiki tribe, or you can bring a stop to their operation. Make sure to bring along
Serafen
for some unique dialog.
For Crookspur
This option is the goal of
Captain Furrante
's side of the quest, and the VTC quest
Of Like Minds
. It starts
Tip of the Spear
.
Note:
Completing Tip of the Spear will cause you to fail the RDC quest
Clearing Out Crookspur
upon returning to
Atsura
in the
Imperial Command
(though not if you have killed Kua, see
notes
below). This means you will no longer be able to ally with the
Royal Deadfire Company
later during
The Coming Storm
!
Head to
Crookspur
, to the far north-west of
Neketaka
.
Enter the fortress by speaking to the ogre at the gate, he'll think you're just another buyer here for the slave auction, and will let you straight in. The auction will be coming to a close, with the last slave,
Handsome Eliam
- a death godlike, up for offer. You can either bid for the slave (win straightaway with
2,000
, or
1,200
if you bid sparingly), continue to spectate the auction, or - by passing a skill check below - question the legality of his sale. This will have you ejected from the fort, putting it on high alert (not recommended if you want to assist the slavers):
With
Streetwise
10, you can point out their need for so much firepower if what they're doing is perfectly legal.
With
Bluff
10, you can say that their operation is under investigation by the crowned royals of Neketaka.
If you don't make a scene
Auctioneer Marcca
will wish to speak with you. Do so to be granted an audience with
Master Kua
, assuming you:
Had the winning bid for Eliam during the auction.
Pay her
2,000
to show you are worthy clientele.
Lie to her with an assisted
Bluff
10 check, telling her that your lord has sent you to inspect their stock.
Enter the fort to the north west and speak to him in his office. Kua will commend your "refined tastes" in purchasing their most valuable offering, and will ask that you pay what is owed. You can pay him (if you want to waste your money) or offer your services in exchange. He will discuss his prior agreement with Furrante, and ask that you eliminate the leaders of the Wahaki tribe, who have recently been commanding attacks on the slavers' ships. You can agree to take care of it, and again you have the opportunity to question the legality with
Bluff
10 (with no effect). If you decline Kua's offer, he and his guards will attack you.
On your way out, Master Kua will remind you to pick up your slave. You can either have him join your crew as a slave, or free him. You can then offer him a job on your ship or suggest that he head to the Temple of Berath in Neketaka.
Otherwise this will start
Tip of the Spear
, a sub-quest involving taking out the leaders of the Wahaki tribe on
Ori o Koīki
. See the associated page for more information.
Once the quest is complete, return to Kua (for some experience and a pat on the back).
Return to
Captain Furrante
to complete the quest, netting you the unique crossbow
Fleetbreaker
,
2,500
, and Major Positive reputation with the Príncipi.
Against Crookspur
This option involves taking a stand against the slavers and optionally removing them from the island. Note that after freeing the slaves, or upon putting the fort on alert, you will no longer be able to assist the slavers, as this will fail
Tip of the Spear
- see
notes
below. This method will cause the bad outcome for Furrante's side of this quest, but the good outcome for Aeldys' side.
Free the slaves
Freeing the slaves is the goal of
Captain Aeldys
' side of the quest, and you are able to do so independently of wiping out Crookspur. Wiping out the slavers will not net you any more of a reward from Aeldys. However, as mentioned above, you won't be left with other options with the slavers after freeing the slaves.
You can talk to Aeldys' agent
Seafol
, on the beach at Crookspur to get more information about this route. She has a skiff ready and waiting for any slaves you send out.
Firstly, enter the sea cave via the entrance on the beach to the east, swimming down the lighter tunnel to get to the
Sea Cave
. Here you'll have to deal with a few spiders, but nothing too difficult - you can easily sneak past too. The tunnel to the south leads to a barred gate, that you can open using either a
prybar
,
Might
18, a Corrode ability, an orlan in the party to reach through the bars, or a Wizard with the spell
Form of the Helpless Beast
, turning one of your party members into a piglet to open the way.
The fort dungeons themselves are swarming with ogres and slavers, with exits to the fort exterior to the north east, and the interior to the north west.
The slaves that you need to free are held in a cage to the east. You can open it with Mechanics 11, or by retrieving the
Large Iron Key
from
Taskmaster Grom
to the north. Freeing these slaves is the minimum required to complete this quest. Although the slaves are concerned about the safety of their escape, you do not need to clear the spiders from the caverns, the slavers from the dungeons, or even unlock the barred gate in order for them to escape. Hilariously, they'll run the wrong way anyway, but will make it out without issue. Further along the corridor you can loot a
Blackwood Log
.
The Wahaki slaves are held in the southwest side of the area, you can free them with Mechanics 12, or with the same key as above, looted form Taskmaster Grom. Freeing them isn't necessarily required for this quest, but they can be used to assault the fort.
Once you've freed the slaves, continue with wiping out Crookspur or return to Aeldys.
Wiping out Crookspur
Wiping out the Crookspur slavers is the goal of the quests
Clearing Out Crookspur
,
Fruitful Alliance
and
Cruel Cargo
, so make sure to pick those up first.
The most straightforward method is invading the fortress under the pretense of participating in the auction (purchasing
Handsome Eliam
in the process) and then killing Master Kua once you're granted an audience with him. See the above for information regarding this route.
Alternatively you can liberate the Wahaki slaves led by
Bauha
. Open the cage using Mechanics 12, or after you've acquired the key from Taskmaster Grom. You can continue to assault the fort with the tribesman, or convince them to escape with Intimidate 10 or Survival 8, rather than try and fight the slavers (though this just means you will have to take them out yourself).
If you incited a revolt with the Wahaki slaves, this will clear some of the slavers in the dungeons and the fort, making your job a bit easier. Doing so will also free the slaves for Aeldys' side of the quest if you haven't already done so. The warriors will exit out the way of the ladder to the north east, then out the front of the fort, leaving the corpses of slavers in their wake.
Return to Aeldys
Once you have either freed the slaves and/or wiped out the slavers, return to
Captain Aeldys
to complete the quest, netting you the unique hood
Blackblade's Hood
,
2,500
, and Major Positive reputation with the Príncipi. If you also return to Furrante, he will be displeased that you failed to uphold his business arrangement.
Notes
Note that you cannot do both sides of this quest, and will have to choose between the either siding with Furrante and the slavers and wipe out the Wahaki, or side with Aeldys and save the slaves/clear out Crookspur. There may be some crossover where the player would be able to do quests on both sides, but typically doing so will result in more bad outcomes than good.
Wiping out Crookspur is technically optional for Aeldys' side of the quest, but rescuing the captive slaves - be it peacefully or violently - will cause Crookspur to become hostile, eliminating any opportunity to further assist the slavers and the extended quests.
If you kill the Wahaki tribe leaders for the slavers, this will cause
Cruel Cargo
to fail (if you already picked it up), and will result in a bad outcome for
Fruitful Alliance
. After this you will be unable to do Aeldys' side of the quest, since the slaves will have been moved by the time you return to Crookspur. This happens immediately upon killing the Wahaki tribe leaders, regardless of what quests you have or haven't picked up.
Killing the slavers at this (or any) point will cause a bad outcome for Furrante's side (even if you do it after killing the Wahaki and completing
Tip of the Spear
). If you do so and return to Aeldys, she will be displeased that you failed to save the slaves, but will appreciate that their operation is no longer standing. If you do so and return to Furrante, he will be upset that you failed to uphold his business arrangement. This is the worst possible outcome for the quest.
If you kill the slavers for the Wahaki tribe, or free the slaves for Aeldys, this will cause
Tip of the Spear
to fail. In addition this will result in a bad outcome for Furrante's side of the quest and
Of Like Minds
- though neither of which will lose you reputation, only the associated quest rewards.
Killing the Wahaki tribe leaders has no bearing on the outcome of this side of the quest.
Clearing Out Crookspur
will be failed if you return to Atsura after completing
Tip of the Spear
, but will not fail if you have killed Master Kua. In this sense it is technically separate to the Crookspur quest chain. This way, you can wipe out the Wahaki, return to Kua to complete
Tip of the Spear
, return to Furrante to complete A Shrewd Proposition, return to Castol to complete
Of Like Minds
, then kill Master Kua and the slavers, then return to Atsura to complete
Clearing Out Crookspur
.
Outcomes
These tables cover the two main paths "Anti-Crookspur" (kill the slavers + aid the Wahaki + free the slaves) and "Pro-Crookspur" (aid the slavers + kill the Wahaki). It does not cover potential edge cases or mixing of the two paths, since most of the time attempting to do one side of the quest will cause the other side to fail or get a bad outcome. Workarounds or alternate paths should be mentioned in the notes section above.

Quest ID
03_TSK_Bardatto_Favor
Guid
2e2aa3a5-dbd1-4075-b13c-4c2c66d188e3
A Sinking Feeling
is a
quest
in
Pillars of Eternity II: Deadfire
.
Synopsis
Ezzali Bardatto asked the Watcher  to seek out information regarding a Valera plot against her. She suggested the Watcher start with Zili, a more easygoing member of the Valera family.
Walkthrough
After
Salt in the Wound
, head to the Bardatto estate and talk to
Ezzali Bardatto
. Talk to her and she will reveal that there are rumors of a Valera plot against her family. She will ask that you go and interrogate
Zili Valera
by the watchtowers to learn of the plans. Talk to him and he will tell you to find
Persa
in
The Hole
.
Head to the Hole, in the Gullet district of Neketaka. Persa is standing in the store room with
Baer
. You can eavesdrop on them in stealth mode. If they notice you, Persa will immediately question your presence. You can use Bluff 2 and Might to get out (but this will not advance the quest). Use Perception 16/Insight 3 to identify Baer as a Bardatto guard and you'll have to fight the whole group.
Once you have the information, return to Ezzali.
Bugs
If you have been caught by Persa once already but talked your way out of a fight, the trigger for being caught the second time doesn't disappear even after the quest has concluded and the conspirators are no longer at the Hole. This causes forced dialogue with the now-absent Baer, but doesn't appear to cause any other issues.

Outcomes and rewards
Give Thieves' Letter to Lord Reymont
2000
Ring of Unshackling
House Doemenel
: Moderate Negative reputation
500
with Intellect 16
Give Thieves' Letter to Abrecan Doemenel and steal the Heart of the White March
3000
House Doemenel
: Major Positive reputation
Related quests
Previous:
-
Next:
The Changing of the Guard
Technical
Quest ID
01_qst_two_story
A Two Story Job
is a
side quest
in
Pillars of Eternity
.
Synopsis
Lord Reymont
is the wealthiest man in
Brackenbury
, who recently procured an unique jewel the
Heart of the White March
.
Abrecan Doemenel
plans to acquire it, to ensure that neither the
Crucible Knights
nor the
Dozens
can buy it and return it to the original dwarven owners in the White March, gaining a powerful ally and thus upsetting the delicate balance of power in the Bay.
Walkthrough
There are two ways to receive this quest. The first is by entering an
unmarked house
in the northeast of the
Copperlane
where thieves will attack you. One of them has a
Thieves' Letter
which allows you to start the quest. You can give it to either
Lord Reymont
or to
Abrecan Doemenel
to proceed.
Alternatively, you can receive the quest directly from
Abrecan Doemenel
himself. This requires you to have been referred to him by his sister
Danna
during
At All Costs
after helping her kill
Verzano
. If you received the quest this way,
Abrecan
sends you to get him the letter from the thieves, preferably without violence. Again you can decide whether to return it to your employer or to show it to
Lord Reymont
.
If you return the note to the
Doemenels
, you are then tasked with stealing the
Heart of the White March
from the Reymonts. The easiest way to do this is to enter
Reymont Manor
through a second floor window, left ajar by an insider contact. Use a
Grappling Hook
or climb up the wall (
Athletics
3 for all
party
members required) to get inside. Move along carefully through the adjacent corridor in
Scouting Mode
, and you should discover a hidden switch opening a false wall. Inside the secret room, a
trapped
(level 8) chest has the
gem
. Be careful in the corridor, as guards patrol the area. Once you have the "Heart", exit through the window and report back to
Abrecan Doemenel
. You will get 3000
along with a Major Positive reputation increase with the
Doemenels
.
If you deliver the letter to
Lord Reymont
he will give you 2000
and a
Ring of Unshackling
. If you note that the "Heart" costs more than 2000
, you will be given an additional 500
. You will also receive Moderate Negative reputation with the
Doemenels
.
Notes
Killing the patrolling guard doesn't appear to alter the outcome of the quest. Even if the guard was killed,
Abrecan
will still compliment you on not alerting anyone. You will lose slight Defiance Bay reputation for each guard, though.
If you are having trouble with the patrol, use your highest
stealth
character to bait one of the moving guards but then move out of range or sight. This will cause the guard to stand still for a moment, changing the patrol cycle. If you get fully detected too easily, there are ways to boost
movement speed
or stealth that can help.
Journal entries in the data files show quest complete stages where the Heart is given to the
Crucible Knights
or
the Dozens
. However, it does not appear to actually be possible to turn the Heart in to either of the factions.
For example, when talking to
Clyver
in
Crucible Keep
:
Watcher:
"I understand you're looking for the Heart of White March. Interested in buying it from me?"
Show him the fake jewel
Clyver:
He looks at the jewel in your hands and scoffs. "A fake. And hardly a good one."
He turns away from you. "Show yourself out."
Show him the real jewel
Clyver:
He gasps as looks at the glowing jewel in his hand. He closes his gauntlet around it, yet the soft light still seeps between his fingers. "Fine work indeed. I don't know how you managed this, but make sure you keep this between us."
(Remove item
Heart of White March
, give player
3,500
, trigger end state 4 of
A Two Story Job
)
Or when talking to
Wenan
in
Admeth's Den Expedition Hall
:
Watcher:
"Word has it you're looking for the Heart of White March. I'll sell it to you for a fraction of what Reymont was asking."
Show him the fake jewel
Wenan:
Wenan looks at you incredulously. As he examines the gem, his surprise melts into fury. "You think I can't recognize a fake? You'd better head for the door. Fast."
Show him the real jewel
Wenan:
"There's no way you-" He falls silent as you show him the jewel. "By the flame, how did you get this? Wait, no." He holds up a hand. "Best you don't tell me. I'll just accept this as a... fortunate gift. And in return, here's a gift for you."
(Remove item
Heart of White March
, give player
3,000
, trigger end state 5 of
A Two Story Job
)
Bugs
(v3.7.0.1318) If you acquired the
Thieves' Letter
, stole the
Heart of the White March
and progressed far enough in the main quest where you can ask out
Abrecan Doemenel
about House Doemenels interests in the Animancy Hearings, you can get stuck when speaking the first time to
Abrecan Doemenel
and directly starting the topic "Invitation to the hearing in Duc's palace" without scratching any other topics concerning the
Thieves' Letter
- You end up being unable to mention the (now successful) acquirement of the letter nor handing over the diamond. Dialog window will simply get closed if you try to do so. You still can end the quest by talking to
Lord Reymont
. Needs to be verified further

4
Steal the Heart of White March.
10004
I returned the letter detailing the heist to Abrecan Doemenel. Now, he's tasked me with stealing the Heart of White March. Abrecan believes the gem will be somewhere on the second floor; there's an open window that I can sneak into from the street. He wants to avoid any bloodshed.
The Heart of White March is expected to sell to either the Crucible Knights or the Dozens. Both groups want to use the gem to buy the friendship of the dwarves of White March. House Doemenel does not wish either faction to gain such powerful allies.
5
Steal the Heart of White March.

Abrecan Doemenel, heir to the Doemenel crime empire, was none too happy that I'd killed his men and thwarted his heist. He offered me an ultimatum: complete the heist and steal the Heart of White March or face the wrath of House Doemenel.
I agreed to steal the gem. Abrecan believes it will be somewhere on the second floor; there's an open window that I can sneak into from the street.
The Heart of White March is expected to sell to either the Crucible Knights or the Dozens. Both groups want to use the gem to buy the friendship of the dwarves of White March. House Doemenel does not wish either faction to gain such powerful allies.

Technical
Quest ID
01_qst_voice_past
A Voice from the Past
is a
side quest
in
Pillars of Eternity
.
Synopsis
Dalton
, a retired adventurer in
Copperlane
, has been hearing the voice of
Rowyna
, his former lover and comrade who died at his side almost 60 years ago. In his dreams, she wanders the
catacombs
beneath the city. He's asked
the Watcher
to venture down there to search for her.
Walkthrough
Dalton
(who is north and just west over the bridge of the
catacombs
entrance) is an old retired adventurer who lives in the
Copperlane
district of
Defiance Bay
. He has been plagued by dreams of a woman he used to adventure with named
Rowyna
, whom he loved. She died fighting 60 years ago, but Dalton feels the dreams are different lately and that she is somehow still here. He asks you to investigate the catacombs.
Head to the catacombs in the southwestern corner of
Copperlane
. In the catacombs, in a room in the north west you will find the undead animancer
Helig
. The path to Helig is filled with various skeletons, so it may be a good idea to have crushing weapons to bypass their DR. The two corridors leading to him also contain traps, so make sure to keep an eye out in sneak mode. Helig will tell you that Rowyna died in battle with him. He claims to remember her but will not tell you anything unless you get
his grimoire
back from his former rival
Moedred
who - since no one knows that he murdered Helig - still works at the
Sanitarium
. Helig says that Moedred keeps "all that is precious to him" in a trunk in his lab, and hands you the
key
used to open it. If you want, you can also just attack and kill Helig at this point. He will drop the
Soulward Amulet
which you can return to Dalton.
Moedred is in the south end of the basement of the Sanitarium. He will admit to murdering Helig but claims he did it because the man was pure evil. He says the chest Helig asked you to search contains a dangerous monster and that he does not have any grimoire. Examining the chest (Perception check) shows evidence that supports that a monster is in it. Now that he knows Helig is "alive" he asks you to go back and end him.
Should you choose to open the chest (with the key that Helig gave you earlier), a
Wicht
pops out and kills Moedred before you can intervene.
Helig will get angry and attack you when you return and (indirectly) tell him that you did not open the chest (you have no option for lying). When he dies you find that he had his grimoire on him, as well as the Soulward Amulet, which contains some of Rowyna's soul.
If you killed Moedred by opening the chest, you can instead let Helig know that Moedred is dead and he will reward you with the amulet.
You can now finish the quest in 3 ways: Keep the amulet, give it to Dalton, or destroy it to release her soul.
Destroying the amulet yourself results in a moderate negative reputation with
Defiance Bay
.
If you give it to Dalton, you can convince him to destroy the amulet with 15
Resolve
or 13
Intellect
resulting in a moderate positive reputation with
Defiance Bay
and
Benevolent
disposition.
Notes
Once you attended the hearings in the course of
The Hermit of Hadret House
, there's no possibility anymore to visit or speak to the animancer
Moedred
, leaving you no other choice as to kill
Helig
for the
Soulward Amulet
.
Role-playing tips
If you ask
Eorn
whether he has seen
Rowyna
, he states that he heard voices coming from the northwest.
In a crate near
Helig
you'll find a
Tarnished Locket
. You can sell it or give it to the
Revenant
close to the crate for some little flavor text, as the poor creature seems to be somehow fixated on that thing. It's most likely nothing but a loose end and has no significant effect gameplay wise. However, some players have interpreted this to mean that the revenant is actually Rowyna who retains a semblance of awareness to care for the memory of Dalton even in her sorry state. This view is supported by the fact that the tarnished locket shows an image of a noble, albeit with the face part being too tarnished to recognize the painted. Dalton, when giving the player his quest, mentions that he was a noble and that he and Rowyna were lovers. The revenant's fixation on the locket implies that the person painted in the locket had a close relationship with the revenant, and when the fact that Helig is a necromancer who killed Rowyna is combined with this information,  players are lead to believe that the revenant could a reanimated corpse of Rowyna. Nevertheless, this interpretation remains a conjecture without a more solid evidence.
Behind the scenes
Bobby Null
was the designer for
A Voice from the Past
, and the first draft of the quest was implemented by
Matthew Perez
.

A Vote of No Confidence

Quest ID
FCT_QST_VTC06_No_Confidence
Guid
9551e62b-fd51-4e1b-bdfa-bc54806a6cad
A Vote of No Confidence
is a
faction quest
in
Pillars of Eternity II: Deadfire
.
Synopsis
Lueva Alvari has called together a meeting of the Vailian Trading Company's key investors, demanding Director Castol's removal from his post. The Canta Nicese will want to hear from those involved in Director Castol's recent operations. That means the Watcher.
Alvari asks the Watcher to join the council meeting in the director's chambers on the upper floor of the
Vailian Trading Company headquarters
.
Walkthrough
Speak to
Lueva Alvari
, who wants to enlist you to help her replace
Director Castol
in a corporate coup.
Go to the upper floor to start the meeting of the
Songretta mea Compresa
, the company's board of shareholders, which includes
Nirro, Canta Nicese
,
Duc Remasi Storanzo me Selona
,
Osgara Rafendr, Erla of Norwaech
,
Príncipo Tarvisi Vadremino III me Revua
, Albina Carnetto, and an archmage.
Your testimony will decide whether Castol or Alvari comes out on top. The numbers don't work out in the Director's favor yet and Lueva is pouncing on that fact as hard as possible. You can contradict her:
When she mentions Poko Kohara and you restored the pillar, you can point out that the investment of men and resources did produce a usable vein.
She will malign Castol's investment in animancy research. You can offer a counterpoint, that the research is promising and could give the VTC tremendous power in time, help reach Ukaizo, or that the experiments simply worked. Tarvisi will express great interest in the research and you can use Bluff/Insight/History 6 to point out the practical applications.
Finally, you can ask
Pallegina
to intercede on behalf of the preferred company head.
Whoever prevails then enlists you to blow up the
Royal Deadfire Company
Powderhouse
. If
Maia
is in your party, you must decide whether to continue to go along with the Vailian Trading Company's plans, in which case Maia leaves your party, or to decline the quest, ruling out the Vailian Trading Company as an ally (and Major Negative reputation). If you decline the quest and
Pallegina
is in your party, she'll permanently and automatically leave it as soon as you sail again (you'll receive
Pallegina's letter
) with no interaction and regardless of your global reputation with the VTC.
Notes
If you have both Pallegina and Maia in your party and you don't want to lose any of them, leave Maia out of the party before starting the meeting or before talking to whoever has won the director position after the testimony (
needs confirmation).
note from a player: talking to maia after this quest triggers a dialogue in which she leaves. I had romance and max relationship with her and "ship needs you and so do I" didn't make her stay. What worked for me is: (1) refuse RDC quest and kill all the brass citadel prior to this quest. BUT DON'T ENGAGE THE POWDER STORAGE FACILITY (this seems to trigger the "maia glares at you", but killing the hazanui doesn't). (2) immediately go to ukaizo after this quest is completed, giving her no chance to make up her mind to leave.
Note from another player: what worked for me was to not commit to blowing up the powder house at all. I didn't have Maia in my active party and I sided with Castol. I then chose "You'd kill all those people?" and "This is monstrous" during the conversation--similar to how you can receive the VTC quest to work with Furrante without committing to actually doing it--and Castol just said he trusted me to do the right thing. I never spoke to Flaune after that, and Maia (at least so far) never left.
