======================================================================

Goods and Services
Details
Type
Faction quest
Level
7
Quest region
Neketaka - The Gullet
Quest giver
Captain Mad Morena
Captain Aeldys
Companion
Serafen
Faction
Príncipi sen Patrena
NPCs involved
Cortina
Locations
Undercroft
Fort Deadlight
Luminous Adra Mill
Experience gained
Total XP
2,025
XP type
Normal
XP level
7
Outcomes and rewards
Complete the quest
Tarn's Respite
Major Positive reputation with the Príncipi
Related quests
Concurrent:
Food for Thought
Technical
Quest ID
06_QST_LuminousAdra_Hunt
Guid
c4d6ac5e-ef99-441f-8e2c-e2c99d3fcf30
Goods and Services
is a
quest
in
Pillars of Eternity II: Deadfire
.
Synopsis
Mad Morena, the Príncipi captain who runs the Undercroft in the Gullet, has a buyer seeking crude luminous adra at black market rates, but she has no stock to sell. The Watcher needs to obtain some crates for her if I want to earn her favor.
Walkthrough
This quest can be easy or hard, depending on how rich you are. To skip it, you'll need to have
30,000
in your pocket, otherwise you'll have to find the
crates
.
Start the quest by either talking to
Captain Aeldys
or
Captain Mad Morena
.
You receive a
Wine and Gunpowder-Scented Letter
from
Captain Aeldys
, inviting you to a parley at
Fort Deadlight
. The letter arrives when you:
Received the quest
Symbols of Death
and left
Dunnage
.
Or completed the quest
Blow the Man Down
, waited to receive the
Letter of Fine Penmanship
from
Captain Furrante
when returning to the world map about 2 days later, then similarly waited for another day.
Talk to Aeldys at Fort Deadlight, she will then send you to Mad Morena.
According to Morena, the Vailian Trading Company has a stockpile of crude luminous adra at their mill in
Queen's Berth
, or you can ambush the Company's boats at sea as they ship the adra out of Neketaka.
The amount of crates you need depends on Morena's mood:
Three crates for being sent by Aeldys.
Four crates normally.
Six crates for annoying her.
At the
Luminous Adra Mill
, you can either buy the crates directly from
Cortina
, or steal them from a number of containers scattered throughout the mill.  Stealing the crates requires a bit of luck, and will be easier using a character with higher
Stealth
.
Alternatively you can pirate the crates from the
VTC
ships leaving
Neketaka
. 2 crates are guaranteed to drop from all VTC ships while the quest is active.
If stealing isn't your thing, Cortina will be willing to sell you the adra at a varying price if you can convince her. You will need to buy the full number of required crates, even if you have already obtained some by other means.
2,000
per crate - At least 3 Reputation with the Vailian Trading Company, asking for the company to return the favour.
1,500
per crate - At least 3 Reputation with the Vailian Trading Company, and
Diplomacy
6.
1,500
per crate -
Bluff
9, convince her that the purchase may be the first of many.
Return to the Captain and hand them in.
Notes
If you are sent by
Aeldys
you can get the sabre
Tarn's Respite
three times. This is one of the best early
sabres
you can find, and is equal to the
Superb Sabre
in
Penetration
.
While the Goods and Services quest is active, take the first bounty from
Barati
, a low-level VTC ship, and sink one more VTC ship. You will get two crates of luminous adra from each. Take these to Morena for your first sabre.
The Luminous Adra Mill has six more crates, but steal just three at a time, making two more trips to Morena to get two more sabres.
Each time you bring luminous adra to Morena and say, "I've got the luminous adra," you get the same dialogue and a sabre. Although the reward each time includes Major Positive: Príncipi sen Patrena and Moderate Positive: Delver's Row Criminals, the second and third rewards don't seem to add anything to your reputation. Experience points are only given for the first delivery.
If you were not given the quest by Aeldys you can only get two sabres at four crates each.
When attempting to buy crates from Cortina, if you have
Pallegina
in your party and relationship 3 with VTC, you can farm reputation with her infinitely by selecting the VTC 3 option "I've done the Vailian Trading Company my share of favors. It's time you do one for me.", and then backing out of purchasing the crates (
verified working
in 5.0.0.)

Harsh Medicine

Halgot's Warmth

200

Major Positive reputation gained with The Gullet.
Technical
Quest ID
06_QST_Cold_Hard_Hope
Guid
0c97f630-fe71-4c02-8810-5ec5351ab608
Harsh Medicine
is a
side quest
in
Pillars of Eternity II: Deadfire
.
Synopsis
Several Roparu in the Gullet have fallen ill with drowner's lung, a deadly and contagious disease. Pitli, a priestess from the Children of the Dawnstars, is caring for them in secret and needs medicine.
Walkthrough
Pitli
believes that Delver's Row can provide the necessary medicine. She doesn't know how to get there, but thinks that people who hang out at the local tavern might.
You can talk to Overseer Hitengo about this and the sick will be taken away and likely die.
Speak to
Fyrna
at
The Hole
regarding the location of
Delver's Row
(or see
The Narrows
for details), and then make your way there.
Once in Delver's Row, talk to
Ernezzo
in his shop to the northwest of the area. You can either pay
800
for the medicine, or do a little favor for him - kill
The Spindle Man
.
Talking to
The Spindle Man
will reveal a hidden entrance to the storeroom if you reveal Ernezzo's plans towards him. He will ask that you kill Ernezzo instead. In exchange, The Spindle Man promises to give you better prices (a 10% discount) at his store.
You can also hire
Rust
to assassinate either Ernezzo (for
300
) or The Spindle Man (for
600
- due to the additional guards). Rust will take a day to get the job done. You can also ask him about the cure to drowner's lung, he'll think you're diseased and point you towards
Ernezzo
.
Alternatively, you can sneak into the side room and steal the medicine yourself.
Another option is just to kill Enezzo for the medicine. His gang leave the shop at night to drink, meaning less enemies to deal with.
Either way, once you've retrieved the
Drowner's Lung Medicine
, return to
Pitli
. You can demand payment or just give it to her for a warm fuzzy feeling and
Halgot's Warmth
, a unique ring.
Once you leave Pitli's Sanctuary, you will be contacted by a
Cloaked Messenger
who will invite you to meet
Dereo the Lean
at
his lair
in
The Narrows
. This is one of
multiple ways
you can gain access to the lair.
Notes
After accepting the offer to kill
the Spindle Man
and informing him of the request of
Ernezzo
to have him killed. He will ask you to kill
Ernezzo
instead though most likely  turning the whole
Delver's Row
against you, using a
Delayed Fireball
then closing the door can accomplish the task without angering anyone but his guards who can't see you.

He Waits in Fire
Details
Type
Main quest
Level
12
Quest region
Deadfire - Northeast
NPCs involved
Eothas
Jadaferlas the Ancient
Bathor Erkon
Locations
Magran's Teeth
Ashen Maw
Lair of the Ancient
The Jagged Keep
Foundations of the Ashen Maw
Kahanga Palace
Experience gained
Total XP
6,750
XP type
Normal
XP level
12
Outcomes and rewards
Complete the quest
Upgrade one of the following Watcher abilities:
Sympathy for the Lost
to
Mercy for the Lost
No Pity for the Lost
to
No Time for the Lost
Chorus of the Lost
to
Commandment of the Lost
Related quests
Previous:
A Distant Light
Next:
The Coming Storm
Technical
Quest ID
CP_QST_10_Magran_Teeth
Guid
501e4c0d-590b-421e-bae2-e8e281ab32d8
He Waits in Fire
is a
quest
in
Pillars of Eternity II: Deadfire
.
Synopsis
The Watcher spoke with Eothas in the ruins of Hasongo but was unable to divert him from his machinations. He bade the Watcher to meet him at a luminous adra pillar in a chain of volcanic islands called Magran's Teeth that rises from the northernmost seas of the Deadfire.
Consequences
This section outlines the consequences and outcomes that may arise by completing this quest
Completing this quest
ends all faction quest lines
if you have not yet completed them up through
Fruitful Alliance
(Huana),
A Shrewd Proposition
(Príncipi),
Clearing Out Crookspur
(Royal Deadfire Company), or
A Glimpse Beyond
(Vailian Trading Company). The quests will still be in your journal but it is likely that they will not update or you will have trouble turning them in. The faction quests that follow these will not become available until you do complete He Waits in Fire. They run concurrently with
The Coming Storm
.
Completing this quest without having completed
A Distant Light
on
Hasongo
forfeits the "soul" bonuses from PoE1
that have not yet been acquired via that quest (including
Effigy's Resentment
,
Gift from the Machine
). You may still journey to Hasongo to start/complete
A Distant Light
, but the connection to Eothas will already be severed and you will not be able to commune with him to re-learn these abilities, nor will the god conversation following Hasongo be triggered. As a result, one of the three upgrades to the Watcher-only "Death's Herald" abilities will also be forfeited (though you will still gain one upgrade during the
god conversation
following He Waits in Fire).
If it has not yet been started or completed, the quest
The Veins of Eora
will be completed upon speaking with Eothas in
Foundations of the Ashen Maw
.
Shortly after the quest, the
Steward
will want to speak with you, starting
The Proving
, the first quest of
Seeker, Slayer, Survivor
.
Walkthrough
This quest does not have any prerequisites, and can be completed at any time by simply sailing to Ashen Maw (the quest will start once walking over the bridge in the first area).
Once you arrive at the Ashen Maw, much of the garrison has been already wiped out by Eothas who is siphoning essence from a luminous adra pillar in the center of a volcano named the Ashen Maw. Travel deeper into the volcano to meet with him. Enter The Sentinel Wall from the Maw, then talk to the Rathun survivors. You can try to talk them down - say you do not follow Eothas, then use Metaphysics 5 to note your soul wasn't eaten; Religion 5 to state that while Eothas and Magran have had hostile dealings in the past, you have nothing to do with it; or Insight 5 to point out their anger is misdirected. Head down to the Ashen Bridge.
To reach Eothas, you need to activate the shrine of Magran in
the Jagged Keep
. To activate the shrine, you need the
Torc of Bithákten
guarded by
Jadaferlas the Ancient
in the
Lair of the Ancient
. Head there. Jadaferlas is a challenging opponent for wits or brawn. You can find out the nature of her deal with Resolve 11, then use Bluff 8 to to pique her curiosity, or Diplomacy 8 to play on her vanity. Then you need to have Resolve 16, Survival 14, or Religion 14 + Priest (Magran) class. If that fails, being honest and having Honest 2 reputation will. She will fly away, allowing you to plunder her hoard and snatch the torc from the essence of Brenthís, the dead Rathun-Priestress of Magran.
In the Jagged Keep, the Torc buys you free passage all the way to
Bathor Erkon
. In lieu of killing him, you can talk him down if you have Diplomacy 5 or Intellect 15. Then you need to establish that your interests don't align with Eothas’s (eg. by stating he snatched a part of your soul). Then you can reveal the truth of Jadaferlas' betrayal.
Fire up the shrine with the Torc. Magran will contact you and explain a few things. Most notably, the fact that she formed the Rathun to ward against kith in order to safeguard Ukaizo - and they are entirely expendable. You can now leave the conversation and talk to the Bathor again. If your skills are high enough, you can convince the Rathun to leave the place before it is destroyed. Place the torc on the shrine, then descend to meet Eothas in the
foundations of the Ashen Maw
.
Eothas will be quite personable and nice - for a massive statue of adra whose single tooth is about your size. He will ask you about the origins of your greatness: Do you think it's innate? Or a result of the experiences that shaped you? After this little nature vs. nurture quandary is behind you, he will give you the opportunity to ask for answers (he will stall you, asking who really wants to know - you or the gods - etc.). Finally, when Magran thoughtfully reminds all of the
impending volcano eruption and giant tsunami that's coming
, he will reveal his goal:
“
I want to return the gods to our original purpose. And to allow mortals to worship us - or ignore us - for what we are, not what we pretend to be. When I entered Waidwen, I did so with the intention of illuminating the history of Engwith. I wanted to show all the nations of the Eastern Reach the machines we had used to create ourselves, how we had hidden our true nature from mortals for millennia.
But even if I had succeeded, my words would have been easy to deny. Belief creates the foundation upon which a mind's reality is built.
Why is it so important that mortals know that the gods came from the Engwithans?
We began with a dream that if we provided guidance to mortals, each generation of souls would grow stronger. And if the souls grew stronger, societies would become better, moving ever closer to achieving the potential we believed all mortals have within them. Over two thousand years have passed. Hundreds of generations have been born, lived, and died. If the world has improved under our guidance, mortals should be prepared to understand the truth. If they have not improved after all of this time, then we have utterly failed in our aims and there should be a reckoning of our faults.
The time has come for a new covenant between gods and mortals, one forged in the light of truth and understanding between our kind.
I will leave this place and go to the lost city the Huana call Ukaizo. It is there that all souls pass through the machines of the gods, where all souls pass into the Beyond before beginning their next life. When I reach that place, I will find our great machines and tear them to pieces. I will smash the Great Wheel until the lights of Hel all gutter and die.
And when my work is done, I will leave this world forever.
[...]
Both gods and mortals alike rely on the Wheel. We depend on it for the souls that give us power. Mortals depend on it for the lives of future generations. Breaking it will force all of us to face the truth. We will fail together or move forward together. Either ending is preferable to the cycle we still find ourselves in.
”
Then Eothas will promptly save you. The adra giant will take your entire party in his palms to protect them from Magran's wrath. Of course, as your soul is partially embedded inside Eothas, you're in for a very, very bad trip. You will find the part of your soul that was split and get the option to rejoin, just as Eothas carries you away in his palm through the ruins of the Ashen Maw and the fire and brimstone Magran unsuccessfully threw at him.
Then Ondra sends her gigantic tsunami to smash Eothas and you. As the water smashes into the titan, you go back to tripping souls. You get the option of reaffirming yourself, with the narration depending on the choice you made at
Sun in Shadow
. Then Eothas will set your ship and you down on the water, with
krakens
clinging to him (in abject futility). Then he will gently nudge the ship with you southwards, which effectively turns it into a speedboat - and asks that you deliver message of his plans to the rest of the gods.
This completes the quest. After being saved by Eothas, you get an upgrade to one of the following Watcher-specific abilities (originally granted at the end of
Stranded
):
Upgrade
Sympathy for the Lost
to
Mercy for the Lost
Upgrade
No Pity for the Lost
to
No Time for the Lost
Upgrade
Chorus of the Lost
to
Commandment of the Lost
The council
You come to in the audience hall of
Kahanga Palace
, in the middle of a debate between
Director Castol
,
Hazanui Karū
, and
Queen Onekaza II
, regarding the information you brought from Eothas. In your sleep-induced mutterings, you reveal Eothas's plan. The kith are understandably concerned about him breaking the mechanism of reincarnation they took for granted - and potentially killing off the pantheon. Once you come to, you are immediately invited to join the talks (just who cleaned you up is not explained, unless you arrived overnight and stand there smelling of ash and fire) as to how address this new development.
The discussion is interrupted by the arrival of the Príncipi, who lay siege to
Queen's Berth
and invite themselves to the proceedings under the banner of
Captain Aeldys
. Once she makes her entrance, you reveal and clarify Eothas' plans. Although your knowledge is limited, you can speculate that breaking the cycle can extinguish all life on Eora in time - or just admit you have no idea. All faction leaders agree that something needs to be done about this and propose passing through Ondra's Mortar to reach Ukaizo and defend it. Fortunately, your soul is still bound to Eothas, allowing you to sense and locate the god within the storms.
Queen Onekaza II
declares that she will not abide outsiders in Ukaizo, the ancestral home of her people.
God conversation
Shortly after completion of He Waits in Fire, the Watcher will commune with
Berath
and the other gods regarding the events of the quest. This interaction occurs as a random event while sailing, sometimes immediately after leaving the port.
Your choices of dialogue during this conversation will determine which upgrade to
Visage of Death's Herald
(or its upgrades) the Watcher receives. Also see
Stranded
and
A Distant Light
for the last two god conversations.
A score is tallied representing the amount of times the Watcher made a response of a particular Disposition. Each disposition is associated with an upgrade to
Visage of Death's Herald
(and its upgrades). You must choose at least 4 of the dialogue options for the associated ability, and your final "score" must be greater than that of the other two scores, with exceptions - see below.
Upgrades
The conditions in-game are very extensive, and are summarized below (with some repeat logic omitted). In cases where you have equal dispositions, the order of presedence is Aggressive > Sassy > Diplomatic (e.g. 4 Aggressive beats 4 Sassy).
Each upgrade adds an
Inspiration
which is granted to the Watcher when using the ability. Diplomatic responses give a Perception Inspiration, Sassy gives Intellect, and Aggressive gives Might. If the ability already grants that Inspiration type, the Inspiration is upgraded to the next tier. For example,
Insightful
(Tier 1) would become
Aware
(Tier 2).
In order to get
Majesty of Death's Herald
, which grants all three types of Tier 1 Inspirations, you must have already previously gotten
Raiment
,
Awe
, or
Cruelty
after the previous two god interactions (in
Stranded
and
A Distant Light
), and then select the remaining disposition responses according to the table below (For example, if you had
Raiment of Death's Herald
, you would need to choose dialog options that resulted in an Aggressive outcome in order to upgrade to
Majesty of Death's Herald
).
In order to get a Tier 3 Inspiration, you must select the same disposition in all three god conversations. For example, to get the sought-after effect
Brilliant
(Tier 3 Intellect Inspiration), you must choose Sassy responses in all three conversations.
Death's Herald upgrade matrix

Visage of Death's Herald

Raiment of Death's Herald
Awe of Death's Herald

, remove

.

is a

in

.
Synopsis
Chiko asked you to meet their elderly uncle, Keipo, a Shatterscarp legend who slew a fearsome leviathan. Chiko believes Keipo has been feeling his age. Talking to a fellow adventurer could be just the thing he needs.

Chiko asked you to meet their elderly uncle, Keipo, a Shatterscarp legend who slew a fearsome leviathan. Chiko believes Keipo has been feeling his age. Talking to a fellow adventurer could be just the thing he needs.

Helping Hands
Details
Type
Side quest
Level
1
Quest region
Port Maje

NPCs involved
Irrena
Chitupec
Eld Engrim
Beodul
Vela

Total XP
742
XP type
Minor
XP level
1
Outcomes and rewards
Complete the quest
Three or four crew members to start
Technical
Quest ID
09_QST_Crew_Members
Guid
a4ee8842-51bd-45a1-9b31-ec85a6377a85
Helping Hands
is a
side quest
in
Pillars of Eternity II: Deadfire
.
Synopsis
The Defiant has run aground, and its crew is dead or scattered. A crew will be of use in maneuvering the ship. Perhaps there are survivors somewhere on the beach. There may be more survivors to be found.
Walkthrough
The goal is to collect the remnants of your crew. There are several to be had:
Irrena
is up on the deck and needs
Survival
2 to heal her or
Athletics
2 /
Might
15 to help her down from the ship. If you have no Survival, Edér's party assist should help.
Chitupec
is near the center of the beach if you elected to save him during the prologue and will point you towards Beodul in the cave.
Eld Engrim
is the easiest to get, as he's waiting just up the road with
Vela
(if she was rescued during
Sacrificial Bloodlines
in
Pillars of Eternity
).
Beodul
is located inside the cave, near the upper center, surrounded by a ton of bear traps. You can disable the traps (
Mechanics
1), guide him through (
Perception
11), call him a coward and make him pass through them (
Bluff
2), or set the traps off with stones (
Intellect
13 or
Sleight of Hand
2). Alternatively, you can initiate combat with the enemy construct to the East, use it to activate one of the traps, and if Beodul survives combat, he will be freed.
Gathering them up completes the quest. Once you return to
Vilario's Rest
(after completing
To Hunt a God
, and finding help with your ship during
Stranded
), they will leave the beach and join your
crew
. However, if you decided to kill
Governor Clario
during
Stranded
, they will not join your crew and will remain on the beach forever.
It is possible to make this quest incomplete, by not talking to
Irrena
, by not talking to her before getting
The Defiant
sea worthy.
Journal

Temple of Revelation
Related quests
Previous:
-
Next:
Annotated History
Technical
Quest ID
lax03_cp_qst_01
Guid
bdb55b4a-8bb7-4cef-9452-c9ebac669de8
This article is about the quest. For the enchantment, see
Hidden Depths (enchantment)
.
Hidden Depths
is a
quest
in
The Forgotten Sanctum
.
Synopsis
The archmage
Llengrath
paid me a visit on my ship and warned me of possible impending doom in the Black Isles. She requested my aid in her search for the archmage Maura, who has been seeking a way to stop Eothas.
Walkthrough
This is the introductory quest to
The Forgotten Sanctum
, leading you to the
Temple of Revelation
. In order to start it, you must first be able to sail to
Ukaizo
, meaning you need to meet one of the requirements:
Upgrade your ship with both
Blackwood Hull
and
Dragonwing Sails
, then sail around the map a little until
Llengrath
contacts you on the ship
Complete one of the factions final quest which is tied to
The Coming Storm
First, head to
The Black Isles
, a large volcanic island south of
Tikawara
. Disembark at the southwestern-most point, and walk to the center of the island to reveal the entrance to the
Hall of the Unseen
.
The Hall of the Unseen is a large maze of switches and doors. Thankfully starting this quest means that all the doors will open upon entering, preventing the need to complete the puzzle that would otherwise be required to reach the center.
Once you reach the center,
Llengrath
will greet you and open the staircase down (located in the western wall of the central area, and to the north of the unlocked chest). This completes the quest
Bugs
One situation may be encountered which causes
Llengrath
to not visit on your ship under unknown reason. My solution is that you can sail to
Ukaizo
and finish the scripted interaction. The game will save the progress that is the state before scripted interaction. Then you load this save and move your ship,
Llengrath
will visit.
A bug may be encountered which causes Llengrath to not spawn upon reaching the center. This means you can't talk to her, which will prevent the staircase from appearing.
To show Llengrath and re-trigger the cutscene, use the following
console
commands (these require cheats to be enabled). This will also complete the quest:
ActivateObject
8ba5594c-b894-40b0-b9c6-22a439538431 True
StartCutscene
8ba5594c-0a8d-4ac7-befa-ff1644aa8320
The staircase may be manually activated using the command:
ActivateObject
8ba5594c-5dec-46b4-976e-893a52b47b8b True
.
Journal
Quests/LAX03_Forgotten_Sanctum/lax03_cp_qst_01.quest

540
1.5
(XP = Experience points, ST = Stronghold turns)
The experience listed is per party member, and does NOT include other
bonuses
, such as party size.
Outcomes and rewards
Return to Kaenra
Unwavering Resolve
Side with Nyrid by killing Purnisc
50% discount on
Svef
(from
Nyrid
)
Keep Purnisc's secret, or intimidate him
50% discount on
Svef
(from
Purnisc
)
Technical
Quest ID
01_qst_intimate_stranger
His Old Self
is a
side quest
in
Pillars of Eternity
.
Synopsis
Kaenra
is a woman recovering from a rather bad experience with her fiancé,
Purnisc
. He started bringing
svef
addicts into their home and pushing drugs. When she confronted him about it, she received a fist to the nose and an order to get out. She asks the Watcher to return the betrothal ring Purnisc gave to her, as it once belonged to his grandmother.
Overview
Talk to
Kaenra
in
The Goose and Fox
in
Copperlane
.
Talk to "Purnisc" (
Nyrid
) on the first floor of
Purnisc's House
in Copperlane.
Talk to the real
Purnisc
on the second floor.
[optional]
Expose Nyrid.
Deal with Purnisc.
Report back to Kaenra.
Walkthrough
Find
Kaenra
at a table in the back of the
Goose and Fox
tavern in the
Copperlane
district of
Defiance Bay
. She will tell you about her fiancee
Purnisc
who has started to act strangely and cut off contact with her, and ask you to return his grandmother's ring to him, a token of engagement.
Exit the tavern and find
Purnisc's House
to the north-west in the same district. Enter, and speak with "Purnisc" in the first room to the right. If you give him the ring, he won't seem to realize who Kaenra is. Something is clearly not right, so you must investigate further. You have two options:
Provoke a fight:
If you select the
passionate
response after giving him the ring, or you simply attack him, a fight breaks out and "Purnisc" reveals himself as a
wizard
called
Nyrid
! When he's almost dead, he will ask for mercy and you can choose to take him prisoner (provided you have already restored your stronghold
dungeons
) or finish him off. You can then either proceed to the second floor to find the real Purnisc [→ 3], or end the quest prematurely by returning to Kaenra and telling her the ring is delivered. (She will give you
Unwavering Resolve
and Minor Positive reputation with
Defiance Bay
.)
Proceed to the second floor without bloodshed:
If you don't want to fight (just yet), talk instead to the Mercenary in the kitchen who is guarding the stairs to the second floor. To get through, you must bribe him (
100
) or intimidate him (
Resolve
14+).
Note:
If you try to get past him a second time he will attack you. If there are other mercenaries on sight they will join the fight.
In any case, once upstairs, make your way to the room on the right either by fighting
(you can loot the door key from the mercenaries' bodies)
, or by sneaking
(you'll need a character with
Mechanics
5 for the door)
.
Inside you'll see the real
Purnisc
lying on the floor. He will ask you to kill
Nyrid
, the mage who is impersonating him downstairs.
Unless you have already fought Nyrid earlier, go down and confront him. This conversation leads to two options:
Make a deal with Nyrid:
He will lower the price of
Svef
to
68
if you kill Purnisc for him. If you do this, the quest ends, and Kaenra vanishes from the tavern, making you unable to get a reward from her.
Fight Nyrid:
When he's almost dead, he will ask for mercy and you can choose to take him prisoner (provided you have already restored your stronghold
dungeons
) or finish him off.
Once Nyrid is gone, talk to Purnisc again. He will ask you to talk Kaenra into returning home and also to keep his svef dealing a secret. If you agree to this (or force him to give you a discount with
Might
14), he will then sell Svef to you at
68
next time you enter his house. You could also kill him. Ultimately you can even refuse to do him another favor, which ends the quest prematurely and results in him immediately trading with Svef again, even if he is not yet back in full form.
Finally return to Kaenra. You can choose to...
Tell her the full truth
, that the real Purnisc was being imprisoned and impersonated, and is a svef dealer and was lying to her about it. Her ring will automatically be returned to her.
Suggest she stay away
(Rational) or that there are
other men in Defiance Bay
(Stoic), or
say sorry it turned out like this
. This results in her leaving Purnisc, and he remains a svef dealer. (0)
Say he needs help and forgiveness
(Benevolent) or that
he still loves her
(Passionate), which results in her returning to Purnisc to give him a second chance and wean him off his svef habits. (7)
Tell her the partial truth
, that Purnisc was being impersonated and imprisoned upstairs, and that you released him. She's glad to be able to return to Purnisc, although is unaware of his dealings with svef. With this option, Kaenra asks for you to return her
ring
, assuming you didn't give it to Nyrid. You can do so graciously (Benevolent), lie and say you lost it (Deceptive), or choose to keep it as payment (Cruel). (1)
Don't tell her the truth
about Purnisc's svef dealing or the impostor, and convince her that her involvement with him is done, and to just move on with her life. (6)
If you gave the ring to Nyrid, the impostor, but didn't find the real Purnisc locked up, and either killed or imprisoned Nyrid after learning he was an impostor, you can simply say that "it's done".
If you made a deal with Nyrid for a discount, lie and say that Purnisc is finished with her.
Tell her that you killed Purnisc
, either in honesty, for his crimes, or that you put him out of his misery. In tears and shock, she tells you to go away. (2)
Lie and tell her that the impostor Nyrid killed Purnisc
after killing Purnisc yourself. She's distraught and saddened by the loss. (8)
Whatever dialog option you pick with her, she gives you the amulet
Unwavering Resolve
- except if you killed Purnisc after killing Nyrid and admit to it, in which case she will be shocked and appalled.
Notes
Going back to talk to
Kaenra
after finding
Purnisc
upstairs, without dealing with
Nyrid
, does not give you any option to tell her about
Purnisc
's predicament, but you can tell her that strange things are going on in that house.
Some NPC's in
The Gullet
in Deadfire will talk about a svef dealer in
Defiance Bay
if
Nyrid
was killed during the quest and
Purnisc
is still dealing svef, or if
Nyrid
is alive and he keeps dealing svef. The quest has its own section in
histories
. Any outcome that results in Kaenra returning to Purnisc results in him eventually ceasing his drug dealing:
"I killed Nyrid and Purnisc stopped dealing svef.
" in the history creator.

There is also a triggering bug or a weird behavior, one player mentioned in the talk section.

Honor Among Thieves

Hel Beckoning
: Looted by killing Aeldys in Fort Deadlight
Fleetbreaker
: From Furrante for killing Aeldys in either location

A Dance with Death
Technical
Quest ID
FCT_QST_Principi_Betrayal
Guid
d540ebd1-abbe-4314-a0e3-5ffacb24af42
Honor Among Thieves
is a
faction quest
in
Pillars of Eternity II: Deadfire
.
Synopsis
Furrante has charged Aeldys with having committed crimes of defiance, negligence, excessive cruelty, and sedition against the Príncipi. The Council of Captains has voted to execute Aeldys, however, she is the only one who knows the means for which to summon the Floating Hangman - information Furrante needs before Aeldys can hang.
Walkthrough
Captain Furrante
wants to eliminate
Captain Aeldys
and use her knowledge to seize the
Floating Hangman
. He sends you to carry out the execution. He has already secured the consent of the Consuaglo by accusing her of sacrificing a Príncipi crew to lure out the Floating Hangman.
You can choose three different outcomes for this quest:
Furrante stays leader, Aeldys is dead or exiled.
Aeldys becomes leader, Furrante is dead.
Both leaders are dead,
Two-Eyed Pim
(or
One-Eyed Pim
) becomes leader.
The steps to get these endings depend on how you dealt with
Benweth
during
Blow the Man Down
.
If Benweth is alive
Ending 1: Furrante stays leader.
Go up to the tower and kill Aeldys. Read her soul and report to Furrante. Note that
Hel Beckoning
will not be lootable from Aeldys' corpse when killing her on the roof.
OR: Speak with Aeldys, return to Furrante and tell him to hang her, then read her soul.
OR: Convince Aeldys and Furrante to let Aeldys go in exchange for the information. Through the dialogue you can have her offer to take Clussa's Voyage in return for the Hangman's secret. You can convince Furrante make the offer too, before talking to her, using Insight 12 or Intellect 16.
Ending 2: Aeldys becomes leader.
Kill Furrante. However, the other Príncipi will become hostile and attack, resulting in some reputation loss. Go up to the tower and release Aeldys. You can lie to the guard you can avoid a fight if they question you.
OR: Loot
A Letter from Director Castol
in Furrante's office (the southern room in Balefire Beacon). You can easily unlock the chest with a total
Mechanics
skill of 13 or higher, or you can attempt to pickpocket Furrante for his
key
. Confront him about working with the Vailian Trading Company to sell slaves. Furrante will be hanged and Aeldys freed.
OR: Go up to the tower and free Aeldys. All nearby Príncipi will become hostile. Go back downstairs and kill Furrante and the other hostile Príncipi. You can loot
Furrante's Breastplate
from Furrante's corpse, and will receive
Scordeo's Edge
from Aeldys. You'll also loot Benweth's suolenet.
Ending 3: Two-Eyed Pim becomes leader.
Go up to the tower and kill Aeldys. Return to Furrante and kill him.
OR: Attack Furrante and kill him. Go up to the tower and free Aeldys. Attack/kill Aeldys as soon as you're able to. Pim will stay friendly and won't get maimed.
Note that the quest will fail if you take this route, you'll be unable to do
A Dance with Death
, but Pim will be leader.
Alternative Ending: One-Eyed Pim becomes leader.
Complete this quest, but take a path where Aeldys becomes leader.
Accept the following quest,
A Dance with Death
. You can work through that quest until obtaining the ghost ship, the
Fonferrus
.
Return to Aeldys. Attack and kill her. The other Príncipi will become hostile and also attack. Two-Eyed Pim will be among the bodies. However, Pim survives the attack (having lost an eye), becomes
One-Eyed Pim
, and is leader of the Príncipi.
If Benweth was killed
Ending 1: Furrante stays leader.
Confront Aeldys in the
Fort Deadlight
command room. Kill her and take the information you need from her corpse. This yields you the unique sword
Hel Beckoning
as loot, but you'll have to fight your way out of the fort. Return to Furrante.
Ending 2: Aeldys becomes leader.
Kill Furrante outright. However, the other Príncipi will become hostile and attack, resulting in some reputation loss.
OR: Loot
A Letter from Director Castol
in Furrante's office (the southern room in Balefire Beacon). You can easily unlock the chest with a total
Mechanics
skill of 13 or higher, or you can attempt to pickpocket Furrante for his
key
. Confront him about working with the Vailian Trading Company to sell slaves. Furrante will be hanged. Return to Aeldys with the news.
By way of reward, you receive
Scordeo's Edge
and
Blackblade's Hood
from Aeldys.
Ending 3: Two-Eyed Pim becomes leader.
First confront and kill Aeldys in Fort Deadlight. Kill Furrante. You'll have to use the attack button to engage him. The other Príncipi will become hostile and attack, resulting in some reputation loss.
Note that the quest will fail if you take this route, you'll be unable to do
A Dance with Death
, but Pim will be leader.
Alternative Ending: One-Eyed Pim becomes leader.
Loot
A Letter from Director Castol
in Furrante's office and confront him then let him be hanged, or kill him outright.
Return to Aeldys and share the news. Finish this quest.
Accept the following quest,
A Dance with Death
. You can work through that quest until obtaining the ghost ship, the
Fonferrus
.
Return to Aeldys. Attack and kill her. The other Príncipi will become hostile and also attack. Two-Eyed Pim will be among the bodies. However, Pim survives the attack (having lost an eye), becomes
One-Eyed Pim
, and is leader of the Príncipi.
Choosing the leader
Relevant dialogue to help you decide which leader you'd rather support.
Furrante's vision for Ukaizo and the Príncipi, respectively:
"We will keep it from the hands of others for one. We will settle it, plunder its endless resources, and enjoy having a place to call home.
"Riches and wealth, incomparable to what we have had before. When we rule Ukaizo, we will thrive. None shall hold the means to threaten us."
Aeldys' vision for Ukaizo and the Príncipi, respectively:
"We sack Ukaizo. Really run it into the ground. The way I see it, we'll either survive Eora's end to sail off with the riches, Or, well, we won't. Regardless, we'll have a Hel of a last go. If we can find and pilfer whatever your rogue god's after, who knows. Maybe us pirates will be heroes."
"We survive at any cost. We expand, conquer the seas. We live free, and we die as we choose. What better life could there be? If we've only got the one life to live - no future for Eora, no more chances for our souls - then we may as well relish what time we've got."
Two-Eyed Pim:
Sadly, he only receives an end-game slide in the Huana control Ukaizo, but his focus is solely on trade.
One-Eyed Pim:
Maimed Pim won't be a very effective leader and seemingly won't become trade focused if the Huana control Ukaizo (no slide will play).
Notes
If Aeldys or Furrante is hanged, their loot (
Hel Beckoning
and
Furrante's Breastplate
respectively) will be found on the table to the right or left of Furrante's seat in
Balefire Beacon
.
Clussa's Voyage is a Principi punishment given to pirates that commit serious crimes. A pirates suolenet is dropped into Ondra's Mortar and they are then forced to take a rowboat into the storm.
One player has encountered a bug or a wiered triggering see:
Talk:Honor Among Thieves – Pillars of Eternity Wiki

. Only

a lighter punishment - exile instead of death - if she gives up the information she has about the
Floating Hangman
.
15

Furrante
from power.
10015

quests\11_twin_elms_hearthsong\11_qst_hunter_brother
ID
Objectives
0
Hunter, Brother
10000
Rîdaî, a Glanfathan from a prestigious tribe, wants to know what happened to his brother Fîorm, a renowned hunter, during the blood-hunt that took him with another hunter, Arthwn, to the forests of the Northweald.
Arthwn claims that Fiorm exiled himself in shame after failing to kill a giant stelgaer in their blood-hunt. Rîdaî believes that Arthwn, a member of a lower tribe, is lying for his own gain.
1
Travel to the Northweald Wilderness.
10001
Journeying beyond the mountain pass of the Oldsong district, Fîorm and Arthwn set together on a blood-hunt at the Northweald. Rîdaî suspects that clues to his brother's fate wait to be discovered in this forest.
20004
Rîdaî mentioned that Arthwn returned to Twin Elms without Fîorm. Perhaps I can find Arthwn near the Hall of Warriors of Elm's Reach and find some answers.
20007
I spoke with Arthwn in Elms' Reach. The hunter insisted that he was the one to kill the giant stelgaer at the Northweald, near a ridge atop a waterfall. According to him, Fîorm couldn't bear the thought of losing to a hunter from a lower tribe, the Twice-Split Arrows, and chose exile rather than return to Twin Elms in shame.
2
Search the Northweald for clues to Fîorm's fate.
10002
I've arrived to the Northweald. It is here where Fîorm and Arthwn journeyed to hunt a fabled giant stelgaer, and where I may find clues to the events that transpired during their hunt.
3
Search the Sentinel Ridge for clues to the giant stelgaer's fate.
10003
I found Fîorm's corpse. His lingering essence revealed part of his last memories. In trying to kill the giant stelgaer by himself, Fîorm tricked Arthwn and fought the creature alone, dying in the attempt.
20000
Although I now have enough evidence about Fîorm's fate, perhaps there's more I can learn about the events that surround his death.
4
Search near the Sentinel's Waterfall for clues to Fîorm's fate.
10004
I've found the corpse of the giant stelgaer. It stalked atop the waterfall, and spotted Arthwn below. Then, another elf, almost assuredly Fîorm, gravely wounded it with a spear. The stelgaer fought to save its life, but died in the resulting battle.
20001
Although I now have enough evidence about the stelgaer's fate, perhaps there's more I can learn about the events that surround its death.
5
Speak with Rîdaî in the Passage of the Six.
10005
I've found enough information about Fîorm's death. His brother, Rîdaî, will want to know about it. I can find him in the Passage of the Six, in the Hearthsong district of Twin Elms.
20002
I found Fîorm's corpse. His lingering essence revealed part of his last memories. In trying to kill the giant stelgaer by himself, Fîorm tricked Arthwn and fought the creature alone, dying in the attempt.
20003
I've found the corpse of the giant stelgaer. It stalked atop the waterfall, and spotted Arthwn below. Then, another elf, almost assuredly Fîorm, gravely wounded it with a spear. The stelgaer fought to save its life, but died in the resulting battle.
20006
I confronted Arthwn about his lies. The hunter, desperate to help his tribe, asked me to favor him during the trial.
6
Share your findings with the anamenfath.
10006
I've decided to speak with the anamenfath, in the Passage of the Six of Twin Elms, to explain the events surrounding the disappearance of Fîorm during his blood-hunt with Arthwn.
20005
I confronted Arthwn about his lies. The hunter, desperate to help his tribe, asked me to favor him during the trial.
7
Speak with the anamenfath in the Passage of the Six.
10007
I've spoken before the anamenfath about what happened in the blood-hunt between Fîorm and Arthwn. Following the outcome of my investigation, the anamenfath asked me to speak again with her.
8
Speak with Rîdaî in the Passage of the Six.
10008
I've spoken before the anamenfath about what happened in the blood-hunt between Fîorm and Arthwn. Following the outcome of my investigation, Rîdaî asked me to speak with him.
9
Speak with Arthwn in the Passage of the Six.
10009
I've spoken before the anamenfath about what happened in the blood-hunt between Fîorm and Arthwn. Following the outcome of my investigation, Arthwn asked me to speak with him.
End states
Arthwn Side

I've concluded my investigation of Fîorm's disappearance and presented my position to the anamenfath. Arthwn's story was deemed true, casting him as a great hero.
Ridai Side

I've concluded my investigation of Fîorm's disappearance and presented my position to the anamenfath. Rîdaî's story was deemed true, casting his brother, Fîorm, as a great hero and shaming Arthwn.
True Side
30002
I've concluded my investigation of Fîorm's disappearance and presented my position to the anamenfath. Rîdaî and Arthwn were both wrong. Fîorm's death and treachery shames his tribe, and Arthwn's lies have now sunk his own kin even lower.

Hunting Season

1,485

Normal

10
Outcomes and rewards
Kill the druids
Major Positive reputation with RDC
Xefa's Empirical Explication
, a Concussion Bomb, 2 Grenades, and 3 Stun Bombs (from Fleet Master Okaya)
Saint Omaku's Mercy
(on Druid of Spring's corpse)
Kill Okaya
Saint Omaku's Mercy
RDC permanently hostile
Technical
Quest ID
15_QST_Hunting_Season
Guid
22f7cfd1-6756-4e7d-973d-6c772a7d778c
Hunting Season
is a
side quest
in
Pillars of Eternity II: Deadfire
.
Synopsis
The port of Sayuka (north of Nekataka) has a conflict with druids native to the island. You can try to end the conflict by helping one of the sides.
Walkthrough
This quest starts automatically after visiting the
Sayuka workshop
. Upon exiting, one of the engineers comes running your way, warning you of an attack on the settlement by a group of Bog Druids. Help defend it by killing all the enemies; Bog Druids leading a pack of
Bears
,
Elder Boars
,
Tigers
and
Ice Blights
.
If you choose, you can also flee Sayuka. This will advance the quest, and the RDC soldiers will take care of the attacking creatures.
Once they're taken care of, speak with
Fleet Master Okaya
. She will thank you, and give you
500
- yet another underwhelming sum of money for risking your life. She explains that the druids have attacked the settlement once before, after which a small force was sent to quell them, but they have not returned and are suspected to have been killed. She will ask you to travel to
Bentbranch Bog
and kill the Druid Leaders yourself.
Bentbranch Bog
Travel to Bentbranch Bog, on the island of Sayuka.
Once there, a short cutscene will play during which a delemgan will summon a cluster of Assassin Vines, thus blocking the short path to the Druid Leaders. Take the long road around by venturing north, or try to clear the Vines (take care, as Vines are very strong range attackers and will burst down squishy group members in seconds). Keep in mind that you will most likely have to fight the delemgan as well as the Vines regardless of the path you choose.
Reach the Druids in the northeastern corner of the location. There will be four of them: the
Druid of Spring
, the
Druid of Autumn
, the
Druid of Summer
, and the
Druid of Winter
.
Talk to the Druids. You can either attack them outright, or attempt to resolve the situation peacefully.
The latter is only available if you are a
Druid
,
Ranger
, have at least 13
Diplomacy
, or if you bear the Nature's Mark (received by un-corrupting the tree on
Motare o Kōzi
) and avoid dialogue options mentioning the RDC. The Diplomacy option then also requires that you are a Druid, Ranger or
Nature godlike
, otherwise they will simply tell you to leave.
With
Tekēhu
in your party, you can ask him for assistance too, though he sides with the druids (
Worldly
disposition).
With
Maia
in your party, she will question your choices should you choose to help the druids.
Regardless, they do not want to negotiate peace, and instead demand that you kill Fleet Master Okaya.
Kill the druids
You can simply refuse their offer or attack them outright. The fight is relatively tough, and the pair of sigils make it even more so. Once they're dead you can loot them for a substantial amount of Exceptional quality gear, as well as the unique bow
Saint Omaku's Mercy
.
Alternatively, you can agree to help the druids (which will grant you the bow) then kill the druids so that you can claim the reward from Okaya as well. As a side note, this choice also gives you better field positioning against the druids, which can be a nasty battle if you are ill-prepared since there are so many casters.
Return to Fleet Master Okaya for Major Positive reputation with the Royal Deadfire Company, the unique Blunderbuss
Xefa's Empirical Explication
, a Concussion Bomb, 2 Grenades, and 3 Stun Bombs.
Agree to help the druids
If you instead agree to kill Fleet Master Okaya, the
Druid of Spring
will grant you a unique War Bow,
Saint Omaku's Mercy
. If
Maia
is in the party, you will lose major relationship (8) for choosing this option. You will, of course, lose Major Reputation with the Royal Deadfire Company if you follow through with the Druids' plan.
Return to the
Fleet Master's quarters
in Sayuka.
If Maia was present during the deal with the druids and is still in your party as you walk into the Fleet Master's office, she will betray you, instantly warning Fleet Master Okaya of your plans and becoming an enemy.
You can choose to follow through and kill the fleet master. This will please the druids, but the RDC will become permanently hostile against you. Most NPC's in the city will attack you when you leave the building, so be prepared!
Otherwise, you can inform Okaya about the Druids' offer for a Reasonable or Honest reputation bonus. She'll still want you to kill them though.
Notes
In an earlier patch, gaining both the bow and the reward from the RDC was only possible if you agreed to help the druids. This has since been changed, as the Druid of Spring will now always drop the bow, making this "trick" redundant.
The Druids will attack you if you pick any dialogue choice besides upper three ones: Druid check, Ranger check, or 13 Diplomacy. If you can pick any of these, they will make their offer, but you can still refuse and attack them.

Related quests
Concurrent:
Prison of Ice
Council of Stars
Court of the Penitents
Technical
Quest ID
12_qst_the_streamstilled
Into the White Void
is a
side quest
in
Pillars of Eternity
.
It is connected to the
Council of Stars
and
Court of the Penitents
main quests.
Synopsis
Rymrgand
showed the Watcher a vision of a gate - the Frost-Hewn Breach - and the pale elves trying to open it. In the vision, the elves crumbled into snow, and the Watcher sealed the gate with an ice crystal that they still had when the waking dream ended.
Walkthrough
You need to make your way to
Noonfrost
in
Oldsong
. Inside is a clan of
Glamfellen
(pale elves) who are trying to escape this world by entering the Frost-Hewn Breach. You need to make your way to the Frost-Hewn Breach either by sneaking or killing your way in and seal it with the
shard
you received from Rymrgand.
Once you have reached the Frost-Hewn Breach, you'll meet
Glasvahl
. He will explain that he and his tribe are seeking oblivion by passing into the White Void,
Rymrgand
's domain. They seek it because they learned from a Watcher who passed through the White that Wends that every one of them had been reborn whole into the clan over centuries. Generations spanning back to the first days the Glamfellen set foot on the White that Wends. They desire to die, scattering their souls into the ether and attaining the mercy that Rymrgand is supposed to grant. That night, they all began Awakening. Experiencing fragments of our past lives - old hatreds, dead passions, tangled memories. It illuminated nothing but the realization that they are doomed to live a thousand pointless lives, never closer to Rymrgand's salvation. The pain is too much for them and they simply wish to die.
You have a few options:
Allow them to leave this world by giving them the
Glowing Ice Shard
. In this case, you will not be able to seal the breach. Yields moderate negative reputation with
The Fangs
and major negative reputation with
Twin Elms
. When you return to
Rymrgand
, you will need
Intellect
19, or have the
Explorer
,
Merchant
or
Mystic
background (mystic causes second option to succeed, not sure about the others) to get full experience for the quest (as well as his favour for
Court of the Penitents
). Otherwise, the quest fails and you will not receive the god's favor.
Kill them. Seal the breach by placing the ice shard in the pedestal (not the door). Yields extraordinary positive reputation with
The Fangs
and major positive reputation with
Twin Elms
- the loot contains a bunch of exceptional equipment. When you return to
Rymrgand
, as long as you justify your actions, you receive full experience.
A player with
Rational
or
Honest
disposition rank 3 will have unique responses should they say
"This was what your vision directed."
A player with
Stoic
disposition rank 3 will have unique responses should they say
""Their souls will eventually break down."
Persuade them to return to their lands and seal the breach by stating that the will of the gods is unknowable - and perhaps they have a specific purpose in the grand scheme of things. To do this you will need to pass a
Resolve
17 check. All the pale elves will leave the temple making it easier to loot. Yields major positive reputation with
The Fangs
and extraordinary positive reputation with
Twin Elms
.
Notes
Appeasing
Rymrgand
and gaining his favor by closing the breach (or convincing him that it was okay afterwards) has the knock-on-effect of opening up the
Skaen
/
Woedica
ending during
Memories of the Ancients
(it is one of several options to do so). Skaen will confront you in The Court section of
Sun in Shadow
after defeating the antagonist and approaching the soul machine, allowing you to select the Skaen
ending
.
If you let Glasvahl and the glamfellen through the Breach, in
Pillars of Eternity II: Deadfire
you can encounter him in the
Realm of the Beast of Winter
during
A Glimpse Beyond
. Rymrgand comments on your choice to go against his wishes:
"You will remember Glasvahl, I trust. He remains here, lingering in the In-Between, refusing rebirth and dissolution both."
"A most irritating spirit. I will not thank you for sending him to me."
"But I will not concern myself with that for now."
Which is slightly altered if you vowed to do Rymrgand's bidding in
Council of Stars
:
"You will remember Glasvahl, I trust. He remains here, lingering in the In-Between, refusing rebirth and dissolution both."
"His company is certainly... amusing. Few embrace oblivion with the fervor of this mad elf."
"I might keep him here a while yet. Every king needs his dancing bear."
The god chuckles, a sound like boulders crashing down a mountainside.
"But I will not concern myself with that for now."
Glasvahl himself has some placeholder dialogue that is not used:
glaswahl: [i can't wait to be disintegrated!]
If you convinced them to return to
The White that Wends
,
Vatnir
in
Beast of Winter
will speak of this during
An Honored Guest
:
"A few years ago, a man named Glasvahl led his clan to the Frost-Hewn Breach in Eir Glanfath, seeking a path from this world to the realm of Rymrgand."
"They returned with a story of a portal to the White Void, and the cold that flowed from it. A tale that reminded many of the Vytmádh."
Otherwise if you let them through or left them in
Noonfrost
, he will follow up with:
"They never returned."
Journal
quests\12_twin_elms_oldsong\12_qst_the_streamstilled
ID
Objectives
0
Into the White Void
10000
Rymrgand showed me a vision of a gate - the Frost-Hewn Breach - and the pale elves trying to open it. In the vision, the elves crumbled into snow, and I sealed the gate with an ice crystal that I still had when the waking dream ended.
1
Find Noonfrost in Twin Elms.
10001

The locals must know something about this place.
20000
Noonfrost lies in Oldsong. Apparently, it's frozen the lake there.
2
Locate the Frost-Hewn Breach in Noonfrost.
10002
I've found Noonfrost, Rymrgand's temple in Oldsong. The Frost-Hewn Breach must be here. When I find it, I can use the shard from the vision to close it according to Rymrgand's wishes.
3
Seal the Frost-Hewn Breach.
10003
Finally, I've found the Frost-Hewn Breach. Placing the shard in the frozen doorway will close it.
4
Return to Teir Evron in Elms' Reach.
10004
I've completed the task Rymrgand gave me. Now, I can return to Teir Evron to ask for his blessing.
5
Return to Teir Evron in Elms' Reach.
10005
I gave Glasvahl the shard, which allowed him to pass into the White Void. With the shard gone and the Frost-Hewn Breach still open, there's nowhere to go but back to Rymrgand.
End states
30000
I closed the Frost-Hewn Breach in Noonfrost and prevented Glasvahl and his clan from passing through it. Rymrgand, Ondra, and Skaen were pleased and have promised to help me descend the pit to Sun in Shadow.
30001
I allowed Glasvahl and his clan to pass through the Frost-Hewn Breach, and I convinced Rymrgand that this also honors him. Rymrgand, Ondra, and Skaen vowed to help me descend the pit to Sun in Shadow.
Quest Failed
30003
I allowed Glasvahl and his clan to pass through the Frost-Hewn Breach. Rymrgand was displeased and has refused to aid me - I'll have to seek the help of another god.

Lair of the Eyeless
[WM2]
Details

Weight
XP
ST
1...6 (6)
1
178
0.3571429
End state
1
180
0.3571427
TOTAL
7
1248
2.5
(XP = Experience points, ST = Stronghold turns)
The experience listed is per party member, and does NOT include other
bonuses
, such as party size.
Outcomes and rewards
Complete the quest
Complete
The White March - Part II
Related quests
Previous:
The Rising Tide
Next:
-
Concurrent:
The Forgotten Army
Technical
Quest ID
px2_cp_ionni_brathr
Lair of the Eyeless
is a
main quest
in
The White March - Part II
.
The quest is automatically obtained after completing
The Rising Tide
.
Synopsis
After reforging the
recovered piece
of
Abydon
's hammer into a hammer of your own,
Ondra
has instructed you to go to
Cayron's Scar
. There you must enter a large fragment of the
Ionni Brathr
, the crashed moon, in order to stop the
Eyeless
in their quest to destroy the
Dyrwood
.
Walkthrough
Go to
The White Forge
and forge a
hammer
from the
Fragment of Abydon's Hammer
.
Talk to the people of
Stalwart
and its surrounding area.
Depending on your choices during the White March, you will have a number of allies in the coming battle:
Adaryc
, obtained during
The Iron Flail
, will give you the
Burning Pitch Trebuchet
talent
.
Matron Beregan
, obtained during
The Ogre Matron
, will give you the
Beregan's Battle Horn
figurine
.
Wengra
with
Durgan's Battery
's artillery, obtained during
Ready the Cannons
, will give you the
Heavy Cannon Blast
talent
.
The conversation decides which of them will be available to you, and you'll get abilities usable in Cayron's Scar to call for backup from them. Very handy if you're on higher difficulties.
Go to
Cayron's Scar
and make your way to the Large Fragment of Ionni Brathr. Make your way through the cavern. You'll be stopped for a conversation with
Ondra
not long after entering.
Continue forward.
There will be another pool, into which you can send someone to dive. Passing a
Constitution
17 check will allow you to take it without losing endurance. Unfortunately, this will attract a group of
lagufaeths
. If you have
Athletics
13, you can out swim them, but they will still follow you to the surface and will initiate a fight. If you have
Stealth
10, however, you can avoid the fight entirely. Failing both checks will result in both getting an injury and having to fight.
Interact with the cliff face, and go down into the small lake. You will not be able to go back up from this lake,
so make a save before entering
if you want to try the ending with different party compositions, as the possible endings will depend on which
party members
you have with you. Decisions made during their
companion quests
may contribute to the arguments you make.
Kill the
Kraken
.
Interact with the crystal, deciding who will be left to hit it. Whomever strikes the crystal will be ignored by the Eyeless. You have several choices for the volunteer and their escape:
Devil of Caroc
is a natural candidate, as she doesn't feel cold and doesn't need air, so she'll just casually walk out (though the murk does get in the way of a pleasant stroll).
Sacrifice an ogre in place of a companion with
Beregan's Battle Horn
if you sided with
Matron Beregan
and accepted her aid.
Use
Iverra's Diving Helmet
(the helmet must be in your inventory and not equipped). The Watcher will pass the helmet to the chosen companion if they don't volunteer directly.
Pass a 19 constitution check. Edér with the Girdle of Eoten Constitution may work.
If you released the Pargrunen souls at the White Forge, they will possess the lagufaeth in the depths and hurry the drowning volunteer to the surface.
The retreating party have to put up with the advancing Eyeless and pass several checks. Note: The retreat checks may not trigger if you defeat the Kraken and strike the crystal with a party of one.
The first check is to distract several Eyeless. Several abilities count towards remediating the danger;
Mirrored Image
,
Repulsing Seal
,
Ringleader
, or
Smoke Cloud
, otherwise they need to dodge past the Eyeless.
The second challenge comes in the form of a clogged passageway. You need
Twin Stones
,
Frenzy
,
Outlander's Frenzy
, or a
prybar
and
Mechanics
7 to clear the obstacle in time for the party to escape out to the ice outside.
Finally, to clear the lake, you need either good scouting abilities (Survival 12, or as a
Naasitaq
),
Kalakoth's Freezing Rake
,
Zealous Charge
,
Blessed Was Wengridh, Quickest of His Tribe
or to have the character
background
The White the Wends.
Ending
After the moon is gone, the Eyeless' souls will gather and decide to have a final talk with you, accusing you of consigning them to oblivion. You will be able to learn about their history with the
Engwithans
and use
Perception
14 to point out that the destruction of memory upsets them. The Eyeless believe that people cut off from their history will wither - and deserve no less. Ultimately, they will decide that they need to return to themselves and restore
Abydon
.
Your decisions so far in the game, and which directions you steered your companions will have an impact on the ending and your ability to persuade them. You will have two base choices (1, 2), and a third (3) if you can provide convincing evidence for your statements.
Ask them not to reforge
Abydon
.
Let them reforge Abydon with the memory of Ondra betraying him.
Convince them to reforge Abydon, but a tempered version, where his memories are restored but given context beyond what he remembered at his death. There are three convictions on which you need to provide at least two examples to convince them: the burden of memory, the burden of legacy, and conflict between the gods.
Tempering Abydon
To provide him with context, you need to convince the Eyeless on three counts:
That history doesn't always serve progress or provide a good example;
That memory can be a burden, and;
That some knowledge should be forgotten due to the inherent danger it poses.
You need to present six arguments (two per category). These depend on your choices throughout the storyline, so save before climbing down to face the Kraken.
The hazard of history
To convince them that history can be a hazard, rather than a benefit, you can bring up the Pargrunen, the strife between Dyrwood and Eir Glanfath, the Iron Flail, and the Devil of Caroc (if you resolved her
quest
). Each example needs to backed up with an explanation as to
how
history and obsession over legacy can go wrong.
For the
Pargrunen
, it's pointing out how their legacy and marvels they created eventually tore them apart.
For Readcerans and Dyrwood, it's the fact that experience of history is subjective and whatever common ties they have pale when you consider the fact that kith care about what is immediate and recent over what's in the past.
For the
Devil of Caroc
, it's how her memory of the past drove her to murder people.
If you have
Pallegina
in your company and know about the ducs' plans to undercut the Dyrwood, you can bring it up.
The burden of memory
Arguments about the burden of memory allow you to bring up Maneha, Zahua, and yourself.
For yourself, you can argue that moving on is in itself development and growth; that some questions cannot be resolved.
The Eyeless will also accept
Maneha
's argument if she chose to forget.
Zahua
's arguments will actually serve the Eyeless' stance if you decided to keep his Legacy low.
As of version 3.7.0, there is an exploit where the Eyeless will accept
Maneha
's argument even if you didn't let her forget the past. There's also a bug that always makes
Zahua
's arguments fail to convince the Eyeless, no matter how much Legacy and Persistence he has.
The danger of knowledge
Convincing them that some knowledge is dangerous involves the White Forge and animancy. It is quite tricky to achieve as it depends on many factors.
Argue that the
White Forge
is too dangerous for mortal hands. This will be dismissed by the Eyeless if:
You did not join the
Knights of the Crucible
, but helped create the
Forge Knights
by completing
Built to Last
.
You joined the
Knights of the Crucible
and did not discourage
Commander Clyver
from pursuing the
Forge Knights
in
Winds of Steel
.
You bound the Pargrunen souls to the White Forge in the conclusion of
The White Forge
.
Argue that
animancy
is dangerous because mastery over souls would bring about stagnation with no further challenges before kith.
This will fail if you convinced the
duc
to let animancy research continue unfettered in the
animancy hearings
.
If you learned that
Aloth
is a member of the
Leaden Key
, you can call upon him to explain why some secrets are best kept hidden away.
This will fail if, during your travels, you pushed Aloth to stand up for himself against authoritative figures such as his father and
Thaos
(meaning Aloth's Autonomy value is greater than his Authority value).
Exposition
Upon finishing the quest, the following text will be shown, along with a narration. This marks the end of
The White March - Part II
.
The Eyeless disappeared beneath the lake, their bond to Ondra finally severed. With this threat ended, hope returned to the White March.
Hunters returned to the wildernesses and travelers to the roads. Tales of the Watcher's bravery replaced stories of monsters in the woods. The villagers of Stalwart busied their hands with work and their tongues with song, eager to seize on the promise of peace.
https://pillarsofeternity.fandom.com/wiki/File:Px2_areanotifications_0008.ogg
Narration by Dave B. Mitchell
Notes
If you cannot convince the Eyeless, then you'll have to edit the source files or cheat.
This tutorial
provides a handy guide on how to ensure success if you can consistently win 5/6 arguments. See the
talk page
for more information.
Trivia
In the scripted event at the end of the quest,
Godlike
characters are suddenly able to wear the diving helmet as an option of escape, even though they aren't permitted to equip any helmets during the entire playthrough, save another scripted event with the Woedica Hood.

: Very Major Positive reputation

side quest

Related quests

-
Next:
The Champion of Berath
Technical
Quest ID
07_qst_lords_of_a_barren_land
Lord of a Barren Land
is a
side quest
in
Pillars of Eternity
.
Synopsis
Gilded Vale
is ruled by Lord
Raedric
, who resides in the keep beyond the Esternwood. With the
Dyrwood
in the grip of the curse known as
Waidwen's Legacy
, he has resorted to harsh measures in order to root out the cause. It has made him enemies, many of whom would see him removed from power. The Watcher is asked to help depose the tyrant by any means necessary.
Walkthrough
Meet
Kolsc
in
Esternwood
. He will only appear if you have a Good/Neutral
reputation
with
Gilded Vale
. You may agree to help him or choose to give time to think about it. Either way, travel to
Raedric's Hold
.
Raedric's Hold is a heavily guarded fortress, to enter you may choose to:
Fight your way through the front door (difficult, but lots of loot). No need for a walkthrough this way as long as you know your tactics. But is recommended to have at least 3 fighters/barbarians in your team. Assuming you take the shortest route you will fight at least 10 Raedric's Guards, 3 Spellwrights, 6 Champions of Berath, 6 Archers, 6 Sellswords, 2 Priests of Berath, 1 Archmage and Raedric VII.
Climb the west wall to reach the lower ramparts (requires either
Athletics
3 or a
Rope and Grappling Hook
unless you want to get Fatigued. All characters in your party that have Athletics 5 or less will receive a level of Fatigue). You will fight at least 3 Archers, 3 Guards and 1 Priest of Berath on the walls.
Sneak your way through the sewers at the east side of the hold (requires either
Might
18 or a
Prybar
unless you want to get Fatigued. All characters in your party that have Athletics 0 or less will receive a level of Fatigue)
Alternatively, if you're visiting
Raedric's Hold
later in the game, you might use
Iverra's Diving Helmet
to avoid the
athletics
skill
check.
Consider that you may change your approach at any given time until you meet Raedric.
There are 3 routes to complete this quest:
Kolsc route
Sneak through the Dungeons and ask for help from
Nedmar
(requires either
Resolve
18 or
Intellect
12 and
Perception
12)
Enter via the sewers.
Kill all of the undead guarding the cells and to the sewer secret entrant. You may optionally kill
Osrya
.
Free
Giacco
from his cell by unlocking it and talking to him.
If you kill Osrya
before
talking to Giacco and then tell him she is dead (avoid answer #1, choose answers "I take it you ended up here…" and then "Osrya's dead"), you will receive [Gilded Vale: Moderate Positive]
reputation
for that.
You will receive another [Gilded Vale: Moderate Positive]
reputation
when you tell Giacco he is free (answer #1). After that, Giacco will leave.
Note that if the path to the secret entrance is not safe, Giacco will be killed! If he escapes successfully he will be found standing in the house at
Magran's Fork
.
Make your way to the kitchen. Ignore this floor and continue up the stairwell.
Take the
Berathian Priest Robes
from the chest. Equip them to any character to blend in with the other acolytes. Keep in mind you will be stopped by some of the characters in this area, and you must pass a unique check for each to proceed unhindered (see the item page for more).
Make you way to the chapel towards the southeast. Sneak and press the hidden button at the second skull.
Talk to Nedmar and tell him Giacco is safe. He will give you a Master Key. (Note: Nedmar will allow you to rest in his room)
Go to the Library, open the locked door and descend to Raedric's Throne Room.
Osrya route
Sneak through the Dungeons and ask for help from Osrya (requires either
Intellect
12 or
Resolve
18).
Enter via the sewers. This requires
Might
18 to bend the bar of entrance by hand. Alternatively you may use a
Prybar
or swim through wearing
Iverra's Diving Helmet
.
Make your way to
Osrya
.
Talk to her and agree to kill Nedmar. Alternatively, kill her, and discover the switch to open the secret passage (
Mechanics
7) by going into stealth mode. (Note: You can agree to kill Nedmar to avoid immediate combat, ask her to let you rest then kill her, it's not necessary to kill Nedmar even if you made an agreement with her)
Take the stairs to the kitchen. Ignore this floor and continue up the stairwell.
Take the
Berathian Priest Robes
from the chest. Equip them to any character to blend in with the other acolytes. Keep in mind you will be stopped by some of the characters in this area, and you must pass a unique check for each to proceed unhindered (see the item page for more).
Make your way to the chapel towards the southeast. Sneak and press the hidden button at the second skull.
Talk to Nedmar and kill him.
Go back to Osrya. She will show you a hidden passage. Ascend to Raedric's Throne Room.
Scorched earth route
Do whatever you want.
Make your way combining sneaking and fighting. There is no alarm on the castle, so knocking off someone doesn't put the castle on alert.
Kill the weak guards in the courtyard, and enter through the wine cellar looking entrance on the left.
This entrance has a full staircase from top to bottom with no guards.  The top floor spits you out right next to the Robes of Berath.
Ally with Osrya or Nedmar for access to the throne room.
Dealing with Raedric
Once you've reached the throne room, talk to Raedric. You can either kill him or join him.
If you
kill
Raedric. Kolsc will give you full access to the hold and you may loot everything. (Note: you can tell him you're going to kill Kolsc to avoid immediate combat, make the necessary formation and attack, depending on the level of difficulty you will be mobbed to death otherwise)
If you
side with
Raedric you will be sent to
Magran's Fork
to hunt Kolsc.
Kill Kolsc [Gilded Vale: Major Negative] (Note: Before killing Kolsc, you can get
Giacco
to leave by talking to him, supposing you saved him, combat will start if you fail)
Return to Raedric to receive your reward. Optionally you can kill him for +1 Cruel disposition.
Notes
A few notes regarding the hold:
Mechanics
9 will open ANY door.
Stealth
4 on all the members of your party can prevent any fight.
Resolve
18 or
Intellect
12 will give you full access to the chapel.
Your actions while completing this quest will slightly affect the quest
The Champion of Berath
depending on your Journal Entry.
Having a bad reputation with the Gilded Vale will trigger instant aggro from Kolsc and Raedric leading to the 30002 entry in your Journal no matter what you do.
If you kill Giacco (before speaking to him, because he will disappear after the dialogue), he will drop
Skaen Cultist Robes
and around
50
cash.
In the room with Osrya, you can find a non-aggressive
Black Cat
, that you can speak to (discovering that it is a zombie cat), and when you pick it up, it is added to your inventory as pet.
Journal

20002

5

End states

Raedric Ends Quest

Force-Killed Both (Never spoke to turn in to either)
30002
All the living heirs to the Raedric holdings have been slain by my hand. Gilded Vale will have to carry on without its 'saviors'.

Drunkard's Regret

Side with the cultists

Cowl of the Piercing Gaze
(dropped by

)
