Technical
Quest ID
09_QST_Erol_is_Missing
Guid
22b8fc97-d68b-4fd6-b93e-65d7f0c9a621
Lost Dues in Good Faith
is a
side quest
in
Pillars of Eternity II: Deadfire
.
Synopsis
Kahn
asks you to help her find
Oswald
, an old elf who owes her 5,000 coppers. Oswald favored
Aenalys
, one of the courtesans on the second level of
The Wild Mare
.
Overview
Talk to
Kahn
in
The Wild Mare
.
Find
Oswald
's whereabouts by obtaining his journal, either by stealing it from
Aenalys
' armoire, or by convincing her to give it to you, returning to Kahn once you have it.
Travel to
Oathbinder's Sanctum
and either rescue Oswald from the cultists, or let him die.
Return to Kahn and tell her what happened, or lie and give her money
Walkthrough
This quest can be started by either coming across
Oathbinder's Sanctum
during your travels (see
below
), or by talking with Kahn in The Wild Mare.
Talk to Kahn to get the rundown of the quest. Oswald owes her
5,000
, and she's offering 20% if you can find him and return the money. You can push her a bit in order to bump it up to 40% (no check required), and with
Diplomacy
5 up to 60%, and then with the
Merchant
background up to 80%.
Kahn tells you that Oswald was especially fond of
Aenalys
, one of the courtesans upstairs. Head upstairs and talk to find out more. She will deny knowing him and try to kick him out. You can use
Diplomacy
4,
Streetwise
3, or
Insight
4 to make her spill the beans (or pay
250
). She will explain that Oswald was quite sentimental and visited her to listen to old epics in Eld Aedyran. However, he started behaving like a paranoiac in recent days and eventually gave her his
journal
for safekeeping, then disappeared. She will direct you to her armoire.
Otherwise, you need to break into her armoire and pilfer it for yourself, then return to Kahn to read through it. She will wonder where they've taken. You can prod her memory with
Survival
4 or a Deadfire origin. She will eventually mention a weird old temple in the
Karatapu Channel
to the southwest of Neketaka. Head to the
Oathbinder's Sanctum
by ship.
Finding Oswald
Make your way through the sanctum. On the fourth floor you will come across a group of Woedican cultists led by
Inquisitor Lödwyn
. They accuse Oswald of oath breaking, and sentence him to death. You can choose to fight and save Oswald, watch him die from the shadows, convince them to let him go, or side with them and allow him to die.
If you discovered the soul of a murdered innocent on the second floor, you can either side with the cultists, or talk them down by convincing them they have wronged many souls.
If you're honest with them and say that they killed only one innocent, you can then let them carry on with their judgement. They will reward you with
1,400
for your good faith. Additionally you'll gain positive relationship with
Aloth
, but incur a negative with
Serafen
. This completes the quest, and you will be escorted back to the entrance.
If you lie and say that they wrongly killed "A handful". Passing a hidden
Bluff
3 check will cause her to question you (looping back to the previous dialogue options), and failing will cause her to attack immediately.
You can talk them down by lying, saying they killed "Too many to count". This requires a hidden
Bluff
7 check to succeed, otherwise they will attack immediately. Accepting what you have said, Lödwyn agrees to let Oswald go, and the cultists escort you both back to the first floor of the dungeon.
If you step in and save Oswald (either by talking them down, or killing the cultists), and have 6
Diplomacy
or 5
Intimidate
, he will give you a unique ring,
Drunkard's Regret
that saves you from hangovers if you drink while wearing it. He will also offer to join your crew. Oswald will also come clean, and tells you that he's broke and can't afford to pay Kahn back.
If you let him die (either by hanging back and letting the judgement carry out, or killing the lot of them, NOT by siding with the cultists, as they will escort you out) you can loot the ring from his body, and discover that he doesn't actually have the money after all.
Note that if you started the quest via entering the sanctum (and not via Kahn), deciding Oswalds fate will complete the quest.
Either way, you can head back to the Wild Mare and talk to Kahn to wrap things up.
If you let him die, you can tell her that he was executed. She will shed a tear for Oswald, but will accept the fact  and leave.
If you let him live or die, Kahn will be nonplussed about the whole affair, all she cares about is the 5000 coppers. You can lie to her and give her the "cut" (between
1,000
and
4,000
, depending on your prior arrangement) and she'll leave happy. Otherwise you can be honest and tell her than Oswald was flat broke. She'll be annoyed, deeply, and storm off, leaving the Archipelago.
Alternative route
If you went to Oathbinder's Sanctum prior to talking with Kahn, the quest will complete once the fate of Oswald is decided. Upon returning to Kahn (assuming you do) the following interaction will occur:
You're able to tell her that you met him not long ago, in a humongous pyramid dedicated to Woedica, or lie to her and say that he's elsewhere
If you let Oswald die - You can tell her the truth, and say that he died, or lie to her and say that he's totally fine and went his own way.
If you let Oswald live, and either didn't add him to your crew (or he isn't on your current active crew) - You can tell her that you saved him, but left him to his own devices.
Either way, she doesn't care for the details, and asks if he had any coin on him.
You can tell the truth again and say that he was broke. She thanks you for the information, accepts the fact, and leaves.
Or you can lie to her, and say that Oswald was loaded, and that his riches belong to you now. She'll ask you to hand over the coin (see below).
If you let Oswald live, and he is currently part of your active crew, Kahn opens by asking if you're the captain with the "ancient Aedyran elf on your crew", claiming that he owes her money and that it's your responsibility as his captain to pay his debts.
The debt is
5,000
, though with
Diplomacy
6 you can negotiate it down to half the amount (
2,500
).
If you pay her, she takes the money, and tells you that she's sorry that Oswald dragged you into this, though she's glad that you've given him honest work.
Otherwise, you can tell her that Oswald is nothing but a broke old drunk. As above, she will storm off angrily.
With
Intimidate
8, you can express your badassery for just having waded through an ancient pyramid filled with monsters and cultists. This will shut her up, and she will quickly down her drink and leave.
Interestingly, you can re-trigger the start of the quest again by saying "Why do you care?", or lying about Oswalds whereabouts upon first talking to Kahn. While this doesn't un-complete the quest, it essentially makes the quest act as if you had already talked to Kahn before finding Oswald, thereby opening up the "returning" dialogue tree as per the Kahn quest route.
You gain no XP for any of this, as the quest will already be finished.
Bugs
Picking the response "
I come for justice, sister. Now see to it promptly. Slowly. I desire to watch.
" unique to
Steel Garrote
and
Priest of Woedica
incorrectly leads to the quest advancing similar to if you never interrupt the execution, but your being lead away as if you had agreed with the courts decision. The quest will advance to "Search Oswald's body", but if you return to the area Oswald's body will have become a loot pile. Interacting with the pile won't trigger the next stage of the quest.

Guid
548e0b25-4f52-4398-b1d2-ac3b2831c9ae
Mapping the Archipelago: Port Maje
is a
quest
in
Pillars of Eternity II: Deadfire
.
Synopsis
Sanza
, the excitable cartographer in
Queen's Berth
, wants to compile a complete guide to the islands of the Deadfire, but he can't do it on his own. He asks the Watcher to do it, beginning with the two uncharted islands to the north and south of Port Maje.
Walkthrough
To complete the quest, you need to find the islands, explore them, and then name them. The first island is in the southern part of
Karatapu Channel
and requires you to explore the
Cavern of Xaur Tuk-Tuk
. The other is south in
Kangati Islands
and will complete once you complete
Temple of Tangaloa ruins
.

Mapping the Archipelago: Razai Passage
is a
quest
in
Pillars of Eternity II: Deadfire
.
Synopsis

To complete the survey, you need to discover the
Fampyr's Crypt
(also called
Cave of Threshing
, located northeast of Crookspur),
Kohopa's Fang
, and
Outcast's Respite
.

subterranean temple

Cut content
This article or section refers to content that has been cut from the final release of the game.
It may include partially developed content, content existing within game files but not fully present or usable in game, and/or early and unreleased concepts.
Melody of the Anvil
Details
Type
Main quest

is a
quest
in
Avowed
.

Notes

Armor Fit for the Wilds
.

Technical
Quest ID
cp_qst_confront_lka
Memories of the Ancients
is a
main quest
in the fourth and final act of
Pillars of Eternity
.
It leads to the final confrontation with
Thaos
and the end of the game.
To be clear: after initiating this quest (specifically, entering the final area), you will not be able to exit, use the map, finish other quests, tie up any loose ends, return to your stronghold, etc. The game will end and if, after the closing credits, you select "Continue," it'll take you back to just before the final boss battle.
Synopsis
The Watcher has done as the delemgan advised - I have been carried safely into the depths of Breith Eaman with the blessing of the gods, who would have them stop Thaos before he sends the souls of Dyrwood's Hollowborn to be consumed by his goddess,
Woedica
.
Whether or not that happens, they have unfinished business, and the Watcher needs to find him as soon as they can. The whispers are louder now, and no amount of rest is any relief. By the time the Watcher reaches him, they may be as lost as
Maerwald
was.
Walkthrough
Find
Iovara
's soul prison in Breith Eaman, to the northeast, and speak to her.
Enter
Sun in Shadow
and make your way to the Court at its heart. Make your way to the machine at the center of Sun in Shadow down the walkways and confront Thaos. This completes the quest, and will start with the Watcher reliving the moment that wounded his soul and tied him to Thaos (at a sanctum similar, but not identical to the one at Sun in Shadow):
Click expand to view dialogue
"Did you lie to me when you said the gods could end suffering?"
Thaos says nothing.
"Are there no gods?"
Thaos' face remains a mask of impassivity. "What IS a god? Hm? A higher power? A rewarder of good deeds and punisher of the wicked? Something men can turn to in their darkest moments, when their days seem only like bridges from one tragedy to the next? Our gods are all these things."
With Thaos' every word you find yourself losing control of a boiling cauldron of rage and doubt and fear and hopelessness, until you can take it no more.
"Are there no gods?!"
With maddening calm, Thaos puts an index finger to his lips. "We are in a sacred place, within earshot of the gods themselves. This is not the time.
"You have been through much these past few months."
"You will return home, and you will rest. When you feel you have recovered, you may rejoin us at the trials. The Inquisition is far from over, and I will have need of you."
"There are many who continue to spread the lies of the Apostate. The Inquisition will not end until we have pronounced judgment on ALL of them."
His eyes linger on you, and there is no mistaking the threat behind it, and your rage shrinks at the image of an iron wheel in your mind, monolithic and caked with the dried blood of thousands. You do not ask your question again.
Thaos will confront you afterwards and annoy your followers, trying to throw them off-balance. Bringing
Hiravias
to the party is worth it just to see his reply. Either way, you will wind up fighting him and the two massive statues,
Woedica's Judge
and
Woedica's Headsman
respectively. They are a challenge, especially as Thaos will go into hibernation when wounded and transfer his essence to one of the statues.
After his defeat, you will witness the birth of the gods.
Click expand to view dialogue
You come out in the room you are standing in now, but it is new and pristine and filled with people - thousands of them, all turned towards the great adra pillar and the machine that encases it.
Thaos stands at the machine, and you are one with him now. You look out at the crowd, at faces of shriveled old men and cherub-cheeked little girls, at mothers bouncing infants to quiet them and fathers clasping their children's restless hands and watching you with somber acceptance. A woman with tears in her eyes gives you a small nod.
You turn back toward the machine, your breaths constricted beneath the weight of unwanted knowledge, preparing yourself to set out alone on a journey without end. You close your eyes and open them again to find the machine still in front of you, beckoning.
You take your place in front of it and place your hands upon a large mechanical disc at the base of the great crystal column and speak a single word. Giant rings creak to life, building speed, setting arrays of carved draconic mouths aglow and sending tremors through the platform beneath you.
The entire room shakes now with the force of the accelerating machine, all sound drowned out by its deep, deafening thrum. Brilliant tendrils of light arc outward from the pillar in all directions, and you look over your shoulder to see them engulfing the crowd, burning them brightly like hot iron.
One by one, the tendrils disappear, leaving ashen effigies where people once stood, many of them disintegrating into gray heaps under the stress of the tremors.
You look above to the adra pillar, and a glowing spherical mass has begun to coalesce atop the column where the arcs converge. It grows and pulses, translucent and bulbous like some immense chrysalis, suspended in slow rotation as though it were being spun from the arcs of light.
When the last arc disappears, the spectral mass hangs a moment, no longer rotating, so bright you must shield your eyes with an outstretched hand, and it seems to you as though it is looking at you.
You bow your head in acknowledgment, and look back up to see it melt into the pillar like warm candle wax. The pillar flares with a flash of light bright as the sun itself, then fades to darkness.
The machine slows down to an abrupt halt, and when the last echo of its grinding cogs has passed, the chamber is still, and you are alone.
After disposing of Thaos, you will receive five options:
[Return it to the cycle.] "You will remember this, Thaos. Remember that no man can hide the truth forever, and all you've done was done in futility. Your memory shall be your penance."
→  Return his soul unaltered to the cycle.
[Destroy all memories contained within.] "Your memory makes you a slave to your own past. Perhaps it can't be undone, but it can be forgotten. It shall trouble neither of us any longer."
→ Return his soul to the cycle, but with all memories of his past lives erased.
[Tear his soul apart.] "You caused me great suffering, Thaos. You do not deserve to exist."
→ Do so savagely
[Tear his soul apart.] "I do not envy you, Thaos. I of all people realize the sway memories can hold. It is time you were granted peace."
→ Do so compassionately
[Banish his soul to Breith Eaman.] "Thaos ix Arkannon, there can be no forgiveness for your deeds, only justice. You shall escape judgment no longer."
→ He does
not
like this.
Afterwards, it's time to decide. The decision affects which
endings
take place:
[Send the souls back into the reincarnation cycle.]
(for
Berath
)
[Return the souls to the bodies they were intended for.]
(for
Hylea
)
[Entropically disintegrate the souls, ending their existence.]
(for
Rymrgand
)
[Distribute the essence to existing souls in Dyrwood to strengthen them.]
(for
Galawain
)
Alternatively, if the associated god proposed an alternative (see
Sun in Shadow
for more information about this), you may:
[Allow the souls to pass through the vein to Woedica.]
(for
Woedica
/
Skaen
)
[Disperse the souls to an unknown location.]
(for
Wael
)
Congratulations, you have ended the
Hollowing of the Dyrwood
- and ushered in a new era.
In Pillars of Eternity II: Deadfire
See:
Histories#The fate of the souls
Your decision made in regards to what you do with the souls will influence the
endings
slides, but otherwise has no real repercussions in
Pillars of Eternity
.
That said, should you choose to import your save into
Pillars of Eternity II: Deadfire
, you'll have to face the consequences (or reap the rewards) of your actions. If you choose an option that goes against your promise made in
Teir Evron
during
Council of Stars
, you will have to endure a punishment. Likewise if you keep your promise, you'll gain a boon or reward later in
Pillars of Eternity II: Deadfire
.
Note that these are only applicable if you actually made a promise to the associated god. Effects that occur regardless of if you made a promise are listed in the "Other effects" column below.

Coastal Farms
and give the meteorite to the Giftbearer.

Message from Afar
is a
main story quest
in
Avowed
.
Synopsis
You sailed to the Living Lands in order to deliver an important missive from the emperor of Aedyr. First, make your way into the port city of Paradis, a rugged coastal settlement and home to the Aedyran embassy. Then find the ambassador and complete your task.
Walkthrough
Travel north from
Claviger's Landing
to reach
The Strangleroot
. You will need to burn brambles along the way to pass.
Kill the afflicted bear upon reaching
Ambassador Falscen Hylgard
, then talk to him.
Interact with the nearby
adra
pillar:
Accepting The Voice's gift grants the
Dream Touch
ability, and the grove will be healed.
Declining The Voice's gift grants the
Godlike's Will
ability, but the grove will not be healed.
Lead the ambassador out of The Strangleroot.
Access to
Paradis
is now unlocked. Enter the city to complete the quest.

Technical
Quest ID
28_QST_Drums_in_the_Deep
Guid
e2de3f1d-7545-4180-864d-fbbacba91767
Nemnok the Devourer
is a
side quest
in
Pillars of Eternity II: Deadfire
.
Synopsis
The Junvik people speak of Nemnok, a mysterious god whose lair is in the depths of the Drowned Barrows. Nemnok takes offerings from the tribe, most recently a boy named Kaali.
The prospect of a new god is troubling at best. Unless "Nemnok" is merely some new aspect of Skaen or Wael playing tricks on the world, the matter bears closer scrutiny.
Walkthrough
The goal of the quest is to reach the Sanctum in the
Drowned Barrows
. Approach
Kaali
, who will then summon
Nemnok the Devourer
into the world. Nemnok will then introduce himself and demand your supplication after Kaali introduces you as the newest sacrifice. You can tell him off with Intimidate 18, or question him on him being an imp. You can then use Diplomacy 8 to receive the quest to deliver Nemnok grimoires. In doing so, you also secure Kaali's release - though he will be nonplussed.
If you do not have Diplomacy 8, you will be forced to fight Nemnok and Kaali. After the fight, you can convince Nemnok to come along as a pet (which provides +1 to empower and +1 casts of level 1 druid, priest, and wizard spells). During the fight, its possible to leave Kaali and many of the worshipers alive even if they attack you; just down Nemnok and combat will end.
Note that it is also possible to agree to help Nemnok, which frees Kaali; then, you can attempt to take the treasures he has as reward for
The Lost Grimoires
- the chests are not even locked, but will aggro everyone in the chamber.  This way, you can get both the treasures and Nemnok as a pet (while saving Kaali to boot). This will actually cause you to fail
The Lost Grimoires
, though.
Return to
Tatok
.
Companion reaction
After Kaali introduces you as the newest sacrifice, if
Xoti
is in the party and the Watcher romanced her, she will comment:
Companion
Dialogue
Xoti
"Watcher, you know I only play pretend under the sheets."
She crosses her arms pointedly.
Behind the scenes
The quest was designed by
Matthew Pérez
and
John Cotto
.

Through Death's Gate
Undying Heritage
Technical
Quest ID
cp_qst_ff_ruins
Never Far from the Queen
is a
main quest
in
Pillars of Eternity
.
Synopsis
Maerwald
has told the Watcher that he might find the
Leaden Key
in the "city of defiance," "never far from the queen."  The
steward
in the Great Hall of
Caed Nua
appears to have been the only other sentient being in the fortress...
Walkthrough
You will automatically get this quest after completing
The Old Watcher
. The easiest path to
Defiance Bay
leads by talking to the
Steward
and rebuilding the
Eastern Barbican
to open up a path.
Then you need to head to the Bay. The intended path is to visit the ruined temple of Woedica in
First Fires
and speak to the ghost there (Lord
Adwellen Rügfald III
), who will direct you to the catacomb entrance in
Copperlane
. It's worth talking to him just to see your companions' reactions.
Otherwise, enter the
Copperlane Catacombs
in southwest Copperlane. Proceed to the southwest corner of the map, to find the entrance of the
Temple of Woedica
. If
Aloth
accompanies you, he will make a comment that these people are not trustworthy, accompanied by another companion telling him to calm down.
After descending the stairs leading to the Temple of Woedica, you can head north east and enter stealth to discover a hidden switch, opening a room hidden by a fake wall containing two traps and minor loot.
Now you have two options:
Option 1:
Infiltrate the
Leaden Key
base non-violently
Optionally pick up the
Woedica Hood
from the corpse in the catacombs, in a cave-like area infested with
sporelings
and
trolls
. Note that since
Godlike
are unable to wear headgear, you will have to choose other options when talking to Leaden Key members in their base; for the meeting with
the Acolyte
there is a special option (see below).
Get the
guards
to let you in by either bribing them with
350
, passing a check of at least 13
Intellect
or
Resolve
, or – as long as you're not Godlike – equipping the Woedica Hood before talking to them.
Talk to the
initiate
in the northernmost room of the base. Listen to his rehearsal. If you didn't pick up the Woedica Hood earlier, you can demand one from him by passing a check of at least 14
Might
or 14
Resolve
.
Put on the Hood and enter the initiation chamber alone. Godlikes will get a special dialog option at the door to put the hood on their heads.
Respond to
the Acolyte
according to the lines you've rehearsed with the initiate:
State your name and purpose. → My name belongs to the gods and my hand to their service.
What company do you seek? → I seek the company of shadows, that our labors may remain secret.
Tell me of your labors. → To see that the craft of kith and wilder does not disturb what bones the gods have buried.
How do we know your purpose? → You shall know it by the confession of my tongue, the deeds of my hand, and the oath on my soul.
And how is your oath guarded? → It is sealed by the Leaden Key.
Watch the visions she shares with you.
Option 2:
Fight through the Leaden Key base
Kill the guards and enter the base. Fight your way to the initiation chamber through the passage to the west and kill
the Acolyte
and her accomplices.
Read the memories of the Acolyte's spirit.
Either way...
The result is the same: You will learn the information which automatically starts the three follow-up quests:
The Man Who Waits
Through Death's Gate
Undying Heritage
Role-playing tips
Once you have completed one or more of the received quests, you might be tempted to report back to
the Acolyte
– however, there is no new conversation option that lets you do so.
After having concluded the conversation with the Acolyte, you can bring your companions into the chamber without repercussions. This makes leaving the temple much quicker, since you can simply use the stairs in the back.

de1c9fd3-e72a-476b-b913-f61d1c45c519
Of Like Minds
is a
faction quest
in
Pillars of Eternity II: Deadfire
.
Synopsis
Director Castol has a contact among the Príncipi, Captain Furrante, who is apparently in a position to aid certain Vailian trade prospects on the island of Crookspur.
Walkthrough
Upon finishing
Dirty Laundry
, Director Castol has yet another matter he needs your assistance with, concerning one
Captain Furrante
of the
Príncipi sen Patrena
. Castol knows that it's ironic that he should now be the one associating with the Príncipi, but assures you that it's in the interests of the Company. He tells you to meet up with Furrante to assist him with problems they're having with a hostile tribe (referring to the Wahaki tribe on
Ori o Koīki
).
This quest requires completing
A Shrewd Proposition
, and by extension
Tip of the Spear
, by siding with Captain Furrante and assisting
Master Kua
and the slavers. Visit Furrante in the
Balefire Beacon
to start, and return to him when it is complete, then finally return to Castol. You will gain Moderate Positive reputation with the VTC. There is also a potential monetary reward, however due to a missing script on some responses this only occurs on responses after saying "You know, I could do a lot of damage with those slavers on my side."
Otherwise, if you freed the slaves and/or wiped out Crookspur, Castol will be flustered, but will understand that you did as you saw fit, even though it might not have been for the betterment of the company. You will gain Minor Positive reputation with the VTC.

On Strange Shores
Details
Type
Main quest
Difficulty
1
Quest region
Fort Northreach
NPCs involved
Garryck
Experience gained
XP type
Normal
XP level
1
XP weight
28
Related quests
Previous:
-
Next:
Message from Afar
Technical
Quest ID
0701_CQ_Prologue
Guid
2DE1AA9F-4B7F4CD1-95722443-461B8DEA
On Strange Shores
is a
quest
in
Avowed
.
Synopsis
You find yourself shipwrecked on an island off the coast of the Living Lands. Investigate your surroundings and find a way to reach the port of Dawnshore.
Walkthrough
After a brief cutscene of your character coming to, talk to
Garryck
, who suggests that you enter the fort to seek the help of the soldiers there. He will direct you to pick up a nearby weapon and a
health potion
. Equip the weapon, and continue up the beach to a breakable wooden wall. Attack the wall and then walk through.
Climb up the wooden ledges, then crouch to go below the broken pillar. Continue to follow the quest marker around the cliff, then climb up the stone ledges to enter a tunnel. Jump down and fight the two
xaurips
in the clearing. Continue on and climb a ledge to exit the clearing. On the ledge, Garryck will point out a pair of tripwires, which you can choose to disarm.
At the end of the path, cross the stream and climb the ladder to the left. Climb another ledge and enter Fort Northreach.
Jump down into the fort. On a table to the left are the
Chirurgeon's Notes
. Interact with it to learn of the strange malady that seems to have infected the fort's inhabitants. Past the table is a locked door that can be unlocked with the
Fort Northreach Storage Key
.
Continue into the next room and take a left to encounter a pillar of
adra
. To the left of the adra is another room with a breakable wooden wall containing
Steadman's Log, First Entry
and
second entry
. To the right of the adra is a dead body, from which the Fort Northreach Storage Key and a
Common Round Shield
can be looted. Take the key and unlock the door from the first room. In the room are some copper skeyts, a health potion, and a chest containing a
Common Breastplate
.
There is a secret room to the east corner of the room with the adra. Interact with the switch and fight the Dreamthrall swarmer behind the wall. In the room is a lockbox containing vambraces.
Return to the adra pillar.
The Voice
will speak to you.
Continue through the fort and down from the ledge. Two xaurip guards and three xaurip scouts will attack. Use power attack to break the shields. Fight them off, then continue to the large doors.
Open the door just before the burnable brambles and speak with
Ilora
in the jail, whom you can offer to free or not. Opposite the cell to the right of the banner, climb up the crates, then jump across to the other side of the room, then climb up to the highest level. Loot and equip the bow, then use it to shoot the
Magran's Fury
over the brambles to burn them. Before jumping down, smash the breakable floor by the wall grate, then jump down into the room to loot the key. Optional: Free Ilora with the key.
Kill the xaurip in the next cavern, then jump along the cliffs to continue to the next area. Use the tall grass to your advantage by crouching/hiding in it for sneak attacks.
Continue to the next set of brambles. Loot and equip the grimiore from the dead mage, then use
Fan of Flames
to burn them away. Jump down into the next cavern and kill the xaurip there, then continue through the cave and jump down into the water.
Speak with
Steadman Ralke
, who has been affected by the
Dreamscourge
. Ultimately, he will attack, and you will need to defeat him.
Continue on to another adra pillar, where The Voice will again briefly speak to you, then to the breach where you can take the boat to
Paradis
.
Journal

Our Dreams Divide Us Still
Details
Type
Main quest
Difficulty
18
Quest region
Galawain's Tusks
NPCs involved
Marius
Councilor Kostya
Komendant Mihala
Inquisitor Lödwyn
Experience gained
XP type
Normal
XP level
14
XP weight
15
Related quests
Previous:
Shadows of the Past
Next:
The Heart of the Living Lands
Technical
Quest ID
0202_CQ_MagranBreath
Guid
7DE9C823-713245D9-8FF0FD3C-4FD4EEB7
Our Dreams Divide Us Still
is a
quest
in
Avowed
.
Synopsis
After using the Ekidan device you found in Naku Kubel to decipher the location of the Garden, the time has come to enter Galawain's Tusks and find the path to Sapadal's prison.
Walkthrough
Go to
Galawain's Tusks
. You will be forced to camp and confer with your companions first, but upon entering the area, head toward the place marked by the Ekidan device. After a brief encounter with
The Steel Garrote
, you'll find the bridge to
The Garden
is impassible.
Hoping for an alternative route, head to
Solace
with
Marius
and the guard will reluctantly let you pass. Speak to
Councilor Kostya
and
Komendant Mihala
. Mihala will prohibitively deny helping you find The Garden, but after the meeting, Kostya will take you aside and inform you that The Steel Garrote is at
Mt. Forja
.
At the mines, fight your way past The Steel Garrote members afflicted by dreamscourge to find
Inquisitor Lödwyn
, who will victoriously proclaim:
Today, the Living Lands unites under one flag! One people! One law! One true god!"
Sapadal
, fearing
Woedica's
impending occupation of the Living Lands, will send out a shockwave, causing the volcano to erupt and turning more kith into dreamthralls. This confirms that the source of the dreamscourge is the imprisoned god themself.
Speak with Lödwyn. She will give you a choice: Promise to kill Sapadal or become at odds with the inquisitor and face her in battle.
After deciding (and fighting Lödwyn or not), speak with Sapadal via the nearby adra pillar. Confront them about being the source of the dreamscourge, and they will be remorseful, claiming ignorance, and then recite how the gods (spear-headed by Woedica) attacked without provocation. Sapadal will request that you release them, which will end the dreamscrouge, then grants you another power.
Head to Solace (fight or run through the dreamthralls) to find it in chaos. The lava flows below the keep are rising, threatening to destroy it. Speak with Komendant Mihala. By now its obvious that Councilor Kostya has been working with Lödwyn. Mihala will ask you to follow Kostya, who has provided the location of The Garden's entrance to The Steel Garrote.
Follow the path down under the keep, fighting skeletons along the way. Eventually, there will be an adra of pillar beckoning you, which turns out to be the soul of Solace's founder,
Nandru
. Nandru will explain that they built the keep atop of
Ekida
ruins as a reminder of Sapadal's fury, and that they should never be freed. He will ask that you destroy the imprisoned god.
Continue down to the sluice gates where Kostya is. He will explain that he wants the keep to be destroyed so that the
Pargrunen
will return to their nomadic roots. Allow him to destroy the keep or fight him. Optional: Open the sluice.
Exiting the area will return you to Galawain's Tusks. From there, follow the quest arrows down the lava tube to enter The Garden. Note that this is a point of no return.
Journal

Out of Eternal Horizons
[FS]
Details

Complete the quest
Celestial Grimoire
Celestial Agitator
Related quests
Previous:
A Rising Star

Technical
Quest ID
lax03_tsk_04_celestial_magic_03
Guid
c374099f-a04a-4ff8-babc-462519c5acc5
Out of Eternal Horizons
is a
task
in
The Forgotten Sanctum
.
Synopsis
Now that
Bekarna
has her grimoire back, she remembers why she came to the Black Isles in the first place - to recover a fallen star. Locating a sample of starmetal will enable her to scribe a unique spell, and she promises to share the results of her research with the watcher. She is certain that a celestial catalyst - a piece of charged starmetal - can be found somewhere in the Halls Obscured. A falling star struck this location recently, so the Watcher may have to look deeper.
Walkthrough
Deeper is an understatement. The quest will not be possible to complete until the player reaches
The Upper Bowels
. The starmetal can be obtained at the Celestial Remnant near the massive piece of Wael.
To avoid a fight, the souls in the starmetal must first be calmed. There are three ways of doing this:
Sacrificing the pet
Giftwrapper
.
Reading the book
On Celestial Spectres: Visitors from Farther than Beyond
and letting the spirits feed on a portion of your soul. This results in an injury.
Or by having read the book
On Celestial Spectres: Visitors from Farther than Beyond
and passing two Metaphysics checks:
Metaphysics 15
:
If you allow the spirits to commune with your soul, ig might prove a suitable distraction to claim a piece of the metal for yourself.
Metaphysics:
Try and commune with the spirits.
Metaphysics 20
:
You open a metaphysical bridge between your soul and the entities inhabiting the mineral.
The spirits will understand what your intention is, and aren't really bothered when they see that you're not hostile to them. They will let you snap of a piece of metal without incident.
Collecting the
Fragment of Starmetal
without doing either will cause Celestial Spectres to attack.
Once they are gone, it's a simple matter of returning to Bekarna to receive her grimoire and a unique tool, the
Celestial Agitator
, that allows for spawning star spirits.

FCT_QST_RDC03_Overgrown_Coral
Guid
11dd2414-37c0-4ef2-82ac-ed48b550f3ef
Overgrowth
is a
faction quest
in
Pillars of Eternity II: Deadfire
.
Synopsis
Fleet Master Okaya has asked the Watcher to discover the source of a magical disturbance causing the coral in
Sayuka
's port to grow out of control.
Walkthrough
This quest will begin on completion of
A Matter of Import
, after talking to
Fleet Master Okaya
. She will ask you to pay a visit to the recently-arrived
Iverra
at the
workshop
on
Sayuka
to find out what they've learned.
Upon entering the workshop, Iverra is in conversation with one of the
Coralmason
. They've determined that the wild coral growths are being caused by a source of magical disturbance that lies somewhere in the deep sea to the southeast of the island, the location of
Cignath Mór
, an old drowned
Engwithan
ruin. Iverra hands you her masterpiece, a set of
Diving Equipment
.
Leave the workshop, and you'll immediately be thrown into a yet another problem
Sayuka
is facing: Bog Druids. This starts
Hunting Season
, though it is unrelated to this quest.
Leave Sayuka and travel just southeast of the island to a new icon on the world map. Interact with it to dive down to the
Engwithan
ruin.
Cignath Mór
As you swim down to approach the ruins, you come to a magical barrier, which is holding back the water from consuming the area entirely. and creating a large pocket of breathable air surrounding the ruins.
See the main article,
Cignath Mór
, for a walkthrough on this area. Once you get to the lower level (and after slaughtering a bunch of seafolk) you will find the adra machine, the cause of the coral growths. It is under the protection of
Galawain
, who explains that it is feeding the creatures in the area, strengthening them so that they become "..strong and ruthless - and in so doing, worthy of [his] regard." You can do one of two things:
Disable the
Engwithan
machine that makes the corals grow. This will anger
Galawain
, who confronts you with a
Kraken
to fight on your way out of the structure. You can loot the unique helm
Mask of the Grotto Deep
and a
Kraken's Eye
off him. This result is preferred by the Fleet Master.
Leave the machine operating and disable three pylons to flood the ruins. If you attempt to disable the machine first,
Galawain
promises you a boon for disabling the pylons instead. Once the ruins are flooded, you dive up to your ship; the watcher must pass a
Mechanics
9 or
Athletics
10 check to avoid injury. If
Galawain
promised a boon, the watcher gains
Savage Cunning
(+1
Perception
, +2
Survival
). upon returning to the surface. The Fleet Master is unhappy with this result, which leads to a moderate reputation loss with the
Royal Deadfire Company
. The reputation loss can be recouped by passing a diplomacy check.
Notes
Disabling the
Engwithan
machine will gain approval from
Pallegina
and disapproval from
Xoti
.
Gallery
Diving down to Cignath Mór

Potions of Plenty
[SSS]
Details

Side quest

Complete the quest

Technical
Quest ID
LAX01_00_qst_potions_of_plenty
Guid
93b57a0d-981e-4ace-afdb-dd576934201d
Potions of Plenty
is a
side quest
in
Seeker, Slayer, Survivor
.
Synopsis
Neale
, the potion seller in the arena, is working on a project concerning
Kazuwari
's abundant soul essence. He enlisted the Watcher's aid as a research assistant and gatherer of samples.
Walkthrough
The quest can be completed while fighting through the Crucible's challenges and requires you to wear Neale's
belt
during the fights. The device will automatically gather samples of the ambient soul essence and will need to be returned after being charged. This produces an upgraded belt, which then needs to be worn to face the tougher challenges, and so on, and so forth. Naturally, selling or dropping the belt fails the quest.
If you've completed all of the trials but still need more belt energy, you can repeat previous trials.
Belt stages and rewards
You're first rewarded with the
Skull of the Fallen
, a reusable potion that grants +30 health, once per rest. Neale uses the collected essence to upgrade the belt to
Less Unstable Coil
.
Next Neale will reward you with the
Bubbling Chalice
, a reusable potion that grants +10 Freeze Armor Rating and will cause melee attackers to suffer 8-12 burn damage. It lasts for 45 seconds, and is usable once per rest. He then upgrades the belt to
Least Unstable Coil
(its final form), however it still needs to be fully charged.
Finally, Neale will reward you with
Liquid Adra
, a reusable potion that grants you the affects of a random potion or unguent, usable once per rest.

Into the White Void
Technical
Quest ID
12_qst_the_cold_of_noonfrost
Prison of Ice
is a
side quest
in
Pillars of Eternity
.
Synopsis
Alwah, a scout of the Fangs, went missing in Noonfrost, the temple on the lake. Larha had sent Alwah to investigate the cold spreading from the temple.
Larha believes the pale elves who recently came to the temple may be the cause of this. Regardless, the Watcher may be able to find Alwah - or evidence of her disappearance - in Noonfrost.
Walkthrough
Speak to
Larha
in
Galawain's Maw
, she will ask you to find
Alwah
.
Find and free Alwah inside
Noonfrost
where she is being held captive.
You can stealth your way in the back areas. Find Alwah in the right hand side of the temple and tell her to stay put.
Then you can explore the left hand side and find the leader of the elves, Glasvahl. Speak with him and pass a
Rational
2 check to have him free Alwah and let you go. Alternatively, pass a check as a
Shieldbearer of St. Elcga
,
Kind Wayfarer
, or
pale elf
.  You will need to be able to pick level 6 locks to do this.
It is easier to free her after you have completed the quest
Into the White Void
as all NPCs leave the area.
Note:
It is possible for Alwah to die while making her escape if you haven't cleared the path to the front door of Noonfrost.
Return to
Larha
and inform her of Alwah's fate.
If
Alwah
survived, Larha will reward you with the unique medium armor
Scales of the Raven
, extraordinary reputation increase with
The Fangs
and minor reputation increase with
Twin Elms
. While talking to Larha, Alwah will enter. Speak with her to learn the
Blooded Hunter
talent.
If
Alwah
did not survive (including if you killed her), informing Larha will result in a major reputation loss with
The Fangs
and a minor reputation loss with
Twin Elms
.
If you finished the quest via stealth, you receive a major reputation gain with The Fangs and a moderate gain with Twin Elms.

Prologue

as a crew member

Killer's Gloves

Related quests
Previous:
-
Next:
Stranded
To Hunt a God
Helping Hands
Prologue
is a
quest
in
Pillars of Eternity II: Deadfire
.
Synopsis
Eothas
has awoken beneath
Caed Nua
and destroyed the castle five years after the defeat of
Thaos
. Among the devastation, the
Watcher
remains on the brink of death as his soul is pulled by
Berath
into a special audience - the gods need the Watcher's help.
Walkthrough
This is the prologue and you cannot fail it (it is not technically a quest). Follow the luminous path to the end, until you come across the pillar of
adra
. Beyond you will meet the Usher, who will
usher
you into a chamber where the Pallid Knight sits. She will explain your situation, that Eothas awoke, snatched a part of your soul, and that you're needed by the gods to tie off the bloody stump that is the Dead God. She will then offer you the ability to confirm your past.
Apart from six pre-made legacies, you can import your endgame save at this point. To do so, it must be located in
Pillars
save game directory (this is automatic if you have Steam and
Pillars of Eternity
installed). The game will helpfully mark the choices you make.
You may choose to  defy Berath and say that you won't work with the gods under any circumstances, in which case you will be returned to the in-between and you will progress to an ending.
If you agree with Berath you wiill progress to character creation, where you can remake your character from scratch (the game doesn't import your character automatically). After you (re)create your character, follow the path to the captain's quarters on
The Defiant
- and
Edér
.
Of course, your ship comes immediately under attack by pirates as soon as you wake up.
Eld Engrim
runs in, reeking of alcohol to inform you of that sad fact of life in the Deadfire. The man in question is
Benweth
, a jumped-up sailor with delusions of grandeur. No matter what you say, you need to fight the enemy boarders. After either they're dead or you're knocked out, the scripted interaction starts. You first need to decide whether to save
Chitupec
or a crate of salvage from
Caed Nua
(the crate contains
Killer's Gloves
,
Oil of Allure
, and a
Thief's Putty
).
You get a glance of
Eothas
walking in the distance as the Defiant beaches itself on an island.
Gallery
Gallery
Scripted interaction
Unused graphics
The crate
Scripted interaction graphics:
Opening
Crate slide
Saving Chitupec
Saving crate
Eothas
Going overboard

Technical
Quest ID
05_qst_rise_and_fall
Rise and Fall
is a cut
side quest
in
Pillars of Eternity
.
Synopsis
An ancient Engwithan tower in
Heritage Hill
has wreaked havoc on the district, transforming its residents into bloodthirsty monsters. I may be able to restore the district to normal, but I'll have to get help from someone who's familiar with the tower and the ancient processes that govern it.
Notes
This quest was likely a precursor to the quest
Undying Heritage
, involving
Teir Nowneth
and its spell over the district. The journal entries share many similarities with the quest, and are in some cases copied verbatim.
One major difference is the mention of the
Godhammer Bomb
fragment, which is used to destroy the machines instead of simply overloading them as in Undying Heritage.
Another requires the Watcher to escape from an explosion if the machines are destroyed.
Journal
quests\05_defiance_bay_heritage_hill\05_qst_rise_and_fall

An ancient Engwithan tower in Heritage Hill has wreaked havoc on the district, transforming its residents into bloodthirsty monsters. I may be able to restore the district to normal, but I'll have to get help from someone who's familiar with the tower and the ancient processes that govern it.

The Bronze Beneath the Lake
Technical
Quest ID
01_tsk_rogue_knight
Rogue Knight
is a faction
side quest
from
the Dozens
in
Pillars of Eternity
.
Synopsis
Osric
, a freshly-made trainer of the
Dozens
and former member of the
Crucible Knights
, asks the Watcher to recover his family's heirloom, a breastplate that has been in it since the
War of Defiance
. The breastplate is held by novitiate
Penhelm
, a snitch who got Osric expelled from the Knights.
Walkthrough
Talk to
Osric
in the
Admeth's Den Expedition Hall
in
Copperlane
to receive the quest.
(Optional) Talk to
Penhelm
in the
Crucible Keep
.
Take
Penhelm's Affidavit
from the Crucible Keep's archives, which is right past the main hall.
Bring the affidavit to
Kurren
in
Hadret House
in
Brackenbury
.
Once you leave, Penhelm with some
Knights of the Crucible's Novitiates
will confront you and demand the affidavit back.
Choosing to give back the affidavit right away, or be
honest
and then give back the affidavit, will give you Moderate Positive
reputation
with the Knights of the Crucible. Being
clever
or
cruel
however will lead to a fight. (You may ask him for the importance of this before making your choice.)
(Optional) Ask Penhelm to return the favor and give back
Osric's Family Breastplate
. He asks why he should. Talking about the armor's importance to Osric will give you nothing, while your
diplomatic
or
passionate
response will at least earn you 200
. Only by asking him if he's that stuck in the past will he hand the armor to you – which is the only way to make both sides involved in this quest happy.
If you provoked him or refused to give him the affidavit, Penhelm and his friends will attack you. You can then loot the armor from his corpse.
Return to Osric to complete the quest.
Notes
You have to return the armor if you want to get the followup
Dozen
's quest
The Bronze Beneath the Lake
.
You can also kill
Penhelm
inside the
Crucible Keep
, though this will cause every single
Knight of the Crucible
to turn hostile – which will block access to some other quests and options.
If you are on overly good terms with the crucible knights Wenan will refuse to give you any quests.
Killing Wenan early in the game (specifically your first encounter) will automatically fail
The Bronze Beneath the Lake
quest. This will trigger a bug that makes the other factions think that you are an ally of the Dozens, hence you will have no choice but to rely on Lady Webb to get an invitation in meeting with the Duc.
Journal
quests\01_defiance_bay_coppleane\01_tsk_rogue_knight
ID
Objectives
0
Rogue Knight

Osric was kicked out of the Crucible Knights by a fellow Knight named Penhelm. Worse, his superiors confiscated a breastplate that's been in his family for generations.
Osric has asked me to retrieve a breastplate from Penhelm. He believes his rival might have forged a document from Dunryd Row verifying the purity of his soul. If I can prove Penhelm's forgery, I may have some leverage to get the breastplate back.
1
Find the heirloom breastplate.

15
Tell Osric what happened.

I allowed Penhelm to leave with his reputation... as well as Osric's breastplate. Osric will want to know the outcome.
16
Retrieve the breastplate from Penhelm's corpse.
10016
I need to retrieve Osric's breastplate from Penhelm's corpse.
17
Take the breastplate from Penhelm by force.
10017
With Kurren dead, I have no leverage over Penhelm. The only way to acquire Osric's breastplate is to kill Penhelm at Crucible Keep in First Fires and take it from his corpse.

Reasoned with Penhelm

After I returned the affidavit to Penhelm, I convinced him to return Osric's breastplate to me. Osric couldn't quite believe the turn of events, but he was happy nonetheless.
Stole the Heirloom

I managed to steal the breastplate for Osric. Not that he's going to tell the Crucible Knights about it; he was happy to have it back, and I was happy to get paid.
Killed Penhelm
30002
I got Osric's breastplate, but not without a few casualties. When I told Osric, he was furious. He told me to keep his heirloom. Despite his bitterness, it seems his oath to the order still mattered to him. He certainly didn't hate Penhelm as much as it first seemed.
Lied to Osric

I got Osric's breastplate, but not without a few casualties. I had to kill Penhelm, but I didn't mention this to Osric. Fortunately, he didn't ask too many questions.

Punishing Penhelm for the makeup of his soul didn't seem right, so I gave the affidavit back to him. I didn't get the breastplate from him, so I won't be returning it to Osric.

Llengrath's Tome of Preservation

The Collected Annals of Llengrath
, the sellable variant worth
20,000
Technical
Quest ID
lax03_qst_03_llengrath_01
Guid
7fbcd9fb-0842-4dc9-90f2-6f1cf9512936
Root and Branch
is a
quest
in
The Forgotten Sanctum
.
Synopsis
Scattered across Wael's temple are copies of the Annals of
Llengrath
, penned by the various incarnations of the archmage. To learn the complete story of the mage and his philosophy, the Watcher has to collect all five.
Walkthrough
This is a collection quest tied to the great archmage.
The Annals of Llengrath, Volume I of V
is collected from the first felled infested librarian and each book collected will allow you to commune with the spirit of that respective Llengrath. The other copies are located:
I
-
The Central Stacks
: Looted from the corpse of a librarian in the path leading to the
Quarantined Section
.
II
-
Quarantined Section
: Northeastern chamber with the infested librarians and dank spores.
III
- Quarantined Section: Southwestern chamber.
IV
- Quarantined Section: Northwestern chamber, opposite the one with the giant luminescent spore.
V
- Quarantined Section: Behind the giant spore that loves you a little too much.
Finding all volumes automatically gives you
The Collected Annals of Llengrath
. Deliver the whole package over to
Llengrath
back in the
Temple of Revelation
. You can give them to
Tayn
, who will turn the unmodified memories of the original Llengrath into
something
he considers a bit more dramatic and interesting, to give Llen some food for thought. Even if you give them to him, you can demand the unmodified version back. Alternatively you can choose to keep the collection for yourself, which Llengrath isn't too happy about.
Note: The dialog to hand the tomes to Tayn may become unavailable if done after completing
The Inner Mysteries
. To hand him over the books, first show the books to Llengrath, then use the option "Tayn would love to see these books". The option to give Tayn the books should become available in his dialog.
Completing the quest will increment favor with one of the mages, depending on completion. This counts towards
The Seeds of Deception
quest.
Journal

Sabotage at the Brass Citadel
Details
Type
Faction quest
Level
14
Quest region
Neketaka - The Brass Citadel
Quest giver
Director Castol
Lueva Alvari
Faction
Vailian Trading Company
Locations
The Brass Citadel
Powderhouse
Experience gained
Total XP
4,050
XP type
Normal
XP level
14
Outcomes and rewards
Complete the quest
Related quests
Previous:
A Vote of No Confidence
Next:
-
Concurrent:
The Coming Storm
Taking Out the Traders
Technical
Quest ID
FCT_QST_VTC07_Sabotage
Guid
b26cb21d-4567-4610-a02b-7005d9b5c18b
Sabotage at the Brass Citadel
is a
faction quest
in
Pillars of Eternity II: Deadfire
.
Synopsis
Now that their position within the Vailian Trading Company is secure, the Director has outlined a plan to cripple the
Royal Deadfire Company
by destroying its powderhouse.
To keep anyone from suspecting the Company's involvement, they have suggested working with animancers to find a more clandestine means of executing the plan.
Walkthrough
Speak to
Flaune Elette
at the
Spire of the Soul-Seers
. She will open a portal to the
powderhouse
.
Once inside, sneak or fight your way to the vat in the north western alcove of the room.
If using stealth, items such as
Thief's Putty
can be useful. Note that only one character needs to sneak to the vat, the rest can stay by the entrance.
Plant the explosive and leave the powderhouse, but be quick about it, since the powder house will go up.
Return to the
Vailian Trading Company Headquarters
. A messenger will tell you you're needed at
Kahanga Palace
.
Attend the meeting at the palace, where the crown demands answers. If you maintain your innocence and deflect suspicion from the VTC, the Company will be in the clear while having crippled the RDC irreversibly.
In order to deflect suspicion, all you need do is introduce yourself, and then say nothing over and over.
Consequences
Starting and/or completing this quest will cause all active RDC quests to fail if they haven't already.
The Brass Citadel
will be under lockdown for 3 in-game days, and is inaccessible during this time.
Completing this quest will make you lose
Maia Rua
as a companion. See
Taking Out the Traders
for more information.
Tekēhu
will have some dialogue after completing this quest, but can easily be swayed into staying in your party.
Switching sides
Instead of continuing with the quest, you may instead take the opportunity to turn against the Huana and/or VTC. To do so, simply hand the
Timed Explosive
(s) to
Hazanui Karū
in
The Brass Citadel
(triggers minor
Pro-Rauatai reactivity). Doing so has the following consequences:
If you tell her that the
Vailian Trading Company
planned to destroy the powderhouse, this fails
Sabotage at the Brass Citadel
, fails all active VTC quests, and locks you out of all VTC questlines.
If you tell her that the
Huana
planned to destroy the powderhouse (triggers minor
Anti-Huana reactivity), this fails
Taking Out the Traders
, fails all active Huana quests, and locks you out of all Huana questlines.
If you tell her that both the
Vailian Trading Company
and the
Huana
planned to destroy the powderhouse, you get both of the above consequences, but also reactions from
Tekēhu
and
Pallegina
if they're in the party. Pallegina also gets major negative relationship.
If you're already allies with the faction you outed, you'll get no help from any faction during
The City Lost to Time
.
If you're not already allies with the faction you outed, you will no longer be able to ally with them during
The Coming Storm
.
Journal

End states

Someone vital to the completion of this quest has been killed.
30001
The powderhouse is a smoking ruin, and I maintained the governor's innocence when facing accusations from the queen at the palace. The Vailian Trading Company has successfully crippled their chief rival for Ukaizo.
30002
The powderhouse is a smoking ruin. Thanks to my testimony at the palace, the director has taken the fall for the destruction, leaving me in the clear and the Vailian Trading Company without a leader.

Technical
Quest ID
13_qst_sacrificial_bloodlines
Sacrificial Bloodlines
is a
side quest
in
Pillars of Eternity
.
Synopsis
Simoc, anamfath of the Three-Tusk Stelgaer, has led his tribe for nearly two hundred years, but he has failed to produce offspring with souls fit to succeed him. Simoc confided his fears that his tribe, which defends the Glanfathan border, will grow weak without aggressive leadership.
Walkthrough
If you talk to
Simoc
in the
Hall of Warriors
in the
Elms' Reach
district of
Twin Elms
, he'll task you with kidnapping a newborn child of his clan and sacrificing it in
Blood Sands
in order to distill a spirit potion for him to make him stronger.
When you approach the exit of the hall after the conversation, Simoc's son
Lliras
initiates conversation with you and asks you to poison his father instead in order to save the baby's life.
You can now choose between Five possible paths to resolve the quest:
Attack Simoc
You can tell Simoc that he deserves to die right in your first conversation with him, or just attack him at your discretion at any later time. That will result in an intense fight against him and everyone in the Hall of Warriors except for
Lliras
. You receive:
reputation:
Twin Elms
:
Moderate Positive
Ovates of the Golden Grove
:
Moderate Positive
Ethik Nôl
:
Extraordinary Negative
items:
Thy Clef
, plus
9,300
+ worth of loot from the Hall of Warriors
Kidnap & sacrifice the baby, and give the essence potion to Simoc
Go to
Hearthsong
district and use the
rusty key
you got from
Simoc
to enter the previously inaccessible
Glanfathan Home
in the south part of the map (just north of the
Celestial Sapling
inn). Take the
baby
from the cradle. When you leave the house, you will be intercepted by
Lliras
and attacked, no matter what you say.
Now return to the
Elms' Reach
district, enter
Blood Sands
, and talk to
Keeper Wrdha
. Give her the
baby
, and she'll sacrifice it and hand you the
Distilled Essence Potion
.
Note:
Doing this will cause
Grieving Mother
to leave the party permanently, and her companion quest
Dream and Memory
to fail.
Go back to Simoc and give him the potion. You receive:
reputation:
Twin Elms
:
Moderate Positive
Ovates of the Golden Grove
:
Major Negative
Ethik Nôl
:
Extraordinary Positive
items:
Thy Clef
, plus
3,200
+ worth of loot from fighting Lliras
Poison Simoc
To do so, go to the
Golden Grove
just east of the Hall of Warriors, and ask
Bledha
to give you a flask of poison. It is irrelevant what kind of poison you choose.
Go back to Simoc and give him the potion, but he will hesitate to drink it. If you choose an answer without attribute check, he will attack you, outcome as above (1). With
Resolve
19 or
Intellect
19, you can persuade him to drink it, but after that, you need another
Resolve
20 or
Perception
19 to avoid him attacking you. You receive:
reputation:
Twin Elms

but it won't be usable. And quest notes say: "

".

Distilled Essence Potion
to Simoc instead. Refer to rewards for helping Simoc when doing this.
In all cases you then complete the quest.
Notes
You can snatch the baby and kill Lliras, but then take the "poison Simoc" or "attack Simoc" route (e.g. to maximize loot or kills), this will not change the results. You can also get the poison first, poison Simoc, and then still steal the baby and kill Lliras.
If you kill Simoc after the quest has ended in a way that he gave you
Thy Clef
, you can loot another
Thy Clef
from him (bug?). This attack will not yield any reputation changes.
Verify
- fixed in patch 3.8.0.87535
If you kill everyone of the Three-Tusk Stelgaer clan in the hall, leave it and enter again or load a save from there, it will be repopulated with default Glanfathans (warriors, children).
Taking the baby without sacrificing it, i.e. keeping it for yourself, will be reflected in the game
ending
.
If you took the child for yourself, she'll
appear
in
Pillars of Eternity II: Deadfire
.

Simoc, anamfath of the Three-Tusk Stelgaer, has led his tribe for nearly two hundred years, but he has failed to produce offspring with souls fit to succeed him. Simoc confided his fears that his tribe, which defends the Glanfathan border, will grow weak without aggressive leadership.
However, he told me he's found a way to endow his next child with a stronger soul.

Shadows of the Past
Details
Type
Main quest
Difficulty
15
NPCs involved
Yatzli
Numena
Tanusas
Kutenrus
Ryngrim
Inquisitor Lödwyn
Experience gained
XP type
Normal
XP level
12
XP weight
10
Related quests
Previous:
Face Your Fears
Next:
Our Dreams Divide Us Still
Technical
Quest ID
0403_CQ_CritPath_3
Guid
9EB2F930-9A684138-99CC9D1C-479B2ABD
Shadows of the Past
is a
quest
in
Avowed
.
Synopsis
Ryngrim gave Yatzli a grimoire that will dispell an arcane barrier the archmage conjured - one that blocks entry into Naku Kubel, an ancient Godless site in Shatterscarp. The path is now clear for you to enter the ruins, where Yatzli believes you will find clues to the location of the Garden - and perhaps the origins of the Dreamscourge.
Walkthrough
Journey with
Yatzli
to
Naku Kubel
and make your way through the area to Ryngrim's Barrier (quest arrows will guide you). When ready, interact with the barrier to have Yatzli dispel it, then enter the ruins.
Upon entering the central chamber with the pillar of adra, an ancient Ekidan ghost will appear and explain that you will need to pass a series of trials before moving forward. First is the Trial of Strength, a straightforward series of combat. Second is the Trial of Wisdom, a pathway full of traps. Several sections of traps may be avoided by taking tunnels in the walls. The last trial is without detail, and after taking the leap of faith through the fog, you will be confronted by the ghosts. By this point, the reliefs make it evident that
Sapadal
is prone to outbursts with terrible consequences, and the ghosts will not allow another disaster. They will attack.
After defeating the ghosts, examine the
Ekidan Device
to find the way to The Garden, then interact with the adra pillar. Sapadal will have some denial about the role they played when the other gods (spearheaded by
Woedica
) attacked the
Ekida
, in which they did as much harm as the invading
maegfolc
. Sensing a follower of Woedica in
Inquisitor Lödwyn
, Sapadal will warn you of her arrival.
Sapadal's tantrum will attract a horde of dreamthalls to the ruins, which you must run through upon waking. After escaping the ruins, you will find Lödwyn in an intense debate with
Ryngrim
. After a discussion (in which Lodwyn says Woedica spoke to her directly about Sapadal), you will be forced to make the following decision.
Allow Ryngrim to sever the adra in the ruins, stopping the dreamscourge in Shatterscarp. However, doing so requires a significant amount of essence, killing dozens of people.
Giata and Yatzli will approve of this decision.
Allow Lödwyn to destroy the ruins, stopping the dreamthrall in the region from attacking
Thirdborn
. However, the dreamscourge will continue.
Kai will approve of this decision.
Following your decision, speak with
Temerti
in Thirdborn to gain permission to pass into
Galawain's Tusks
.

End states
30000
In order to permanently halt the creation of new Dreamthralls in Shatterscarp, Ryngrim cast a spell that severed the adra in the region. The spell required a tremendous amount of essence, and the cost killed many inhabitants of the area at random.
30001
Inquisitor Lödwyn destroyed Naku Kubel with cannon fire, exterminating all of the Dreamthralls inside the Godless ruins. While this act will temporarily reduce the number of Dreamthralls in Shatterscarp, it will do nothing to prevent the spread of the Dreamscourge and the appearance of new Dreamthralls in the future.

07_QST_Skipping_Ahead

d242649f-0b1d-4e43-ba91-9381975ac1f6
Skipping Ahead
is a
faction quest
in
Pillars of Eternity II: Deadfire
.
Synopsis
The animancers have gathered their resources and enthusiasm, and are intent upon a second attempt to cross the In-Between.
Walkthrough
This quest becomes available after the main story quest
He Waits in Fire
.
After talking to
Flaune Elette
atop the
Spire of the Soul-Seers
in
Neketaka
, you are teleported to the lower level of
Pāhowane
full of
xaurips
on the same island, and must make your way back to Neketaka on foot.
Examine the wall near to where you enter to reveal a place for a lever. Find the lever in a stash to the east and use it on the lever socket to open a door to stairs leading up to the ground floor. From there, the exit to the outside is to the west.
Back in Neketaka, return to Elette to discover that her tower is being attacked by
Royal Deadfire Company
mercenaries. Clean them out,be sure to grab
Animancer's Energy Blade
, then return to
Director Castol
to end the quest.

before proceeding further with the experiments. Apparently the Company likes to keep track of who is involved with their animancy projects.

Return to the
Vailian Trading Company Headquarters
.
10009
The animancers are safe, for the time being. Flaune suggested I go to the
Vailian Trading Company Headquarters
in Queen's Berth and inform
Director Alvari
of the events at the Spire.
10
Speak with
Flaune Elette
atop the
Spire of the Soul-Seers
.
10010
Flaune
has sent me a missive, informing me that the animancers have made the necessary adjustments and calculations to make a successful leap between two adra pillars.
Maybe I should brace for the worst.
End states
30000
Someone critical to the completion of this quest has been killed.
30001
Well, the experiment did transport me - clear across the island. I dispatched the mercenaries that took advantage of my absence to attack the Spire, and Flaune was grateful for the assistance.
30002
Well, the experiment did transport me - clear across the island. I dispatched the mercenaries that took advantage of my absence to attack the Spire, but several lives were lost. Castol was grateful for my intervention, though it seems he'll be facing some consequences for this incident.
-
30004
Well, the experiment did transport me - clear across the island. Unfortunately, Flaune and the other animancers haven't survived the mercenary attack that followed. That puts an end to their foolishness.

Stranded
Details
Type
Main quest
Level
1
Quest region
Port Maje
NPCs involved
Governor Clario
Benessa
Eothas
Locations
Vilario's Rest
Port Maje
Port Maje Harbor
Engwithan Digsite
Arena Sub-level
Experience gained
Total XP
1,147
XP type
Major
XP level
1
Outcomes and rewards
Complete the quest
Unlocks world map travel and the Defiant
Upgrades
Phantoms of the Lost
(to either
Sympathy for the Lost
,
No Pity for the Lost
, or
Chorus of the Lost
)
Related quests
Previous:
Prologue
Next:
The Veins of Eora
Basic Provisions
Skeleton Crew
Concurrent:
Helping Hands
To Hunt a God
Technical
Quest ID
CP_QST_09_Port_Maje
Guid
b34631e8-90f0-49f7-8733-0dc4728894b3
Stranded
is a
quest
in
Pillars of Eternity II: Deadfire
.
Synopsis
The Defiant has run aground after suffering an attack by pirates, bringing a temporary halt to the hunt for Eothas. The Defiant's suffered heavy damage. There's not much sailing to be done with the hull so battered.  Most of the islands in this region are inhabited. With a little luck, you'll find some assistance nearby.
Walkthrough
This is the first of the storyline quests and culminates in getting off the island with
Port Maje
and off to hunt
Eothas
.
Find some help
Your first priority should be completing
Helping Hands
(to get some crew for a start) and then leave
Vilario's Rest
.
Then head to
Port Maje
up north.
Enter the city and approach the platform in the center of the square. After a brief exchange of words between the governor and the Huana representative, all the eyes will turn to you - and the commoners will take you for a pirate. After resolving the situation, the governor will invite you to his mansion.
There, he will reveal The
Vailian Trading Company
's interests in this island center around the 'luminous adra' to be found at an
Engwithan Digsite
not far from
Port Maje
. The town's animancers have not returned, and the governor would rather keep his guards close by in case the pirates stage a tactical assault. He will ask the Watcher to investigate on his behalf, in exchange for which he'll see that
the Defiant
is repaired and set out to sea.
Investigate the
Engwithan Digsite
Head to the
Engwithan Digsite
. Eothas has definitely set foot in this place, literally.
Clear out the beasts in the center, then ask
Benessa
to open
the cage
. There you find the survivors of the animancer team, including
Engferth
. Note that you can ignore them and choose to find Oderisi first. In which case,
Engferth
will be waiting for you back at
Port Maje
.
To find Oderisi's corpse, descend to the
arena sub-level
. He is found at the pillar in the northern section. Retrieve
Oderisi's Notes
to satisfy the governor. The shortest route is to turn right at every crossroad.
A scripted interaction will follow as you realize that the adra pillar seems dead,
disconnected
from Berath's wheel. You touch the pillar and before you realize it, you are pulled into the
In-Between
, slowly making sense of it, then rapidly following a golden connection that terminates right in the back of your mark:
Eothas
. Your choice here determines which Watcher ability you get upon leaving the sub-level, you can:
Interact with the souls, reaching out to them → Grants
Sympathy for the Lost
Try to push past them to find your own soul → Grants
No Pity for the Lost
Shout at Eothas to stop → Grants
Chorus of the Lost
No matter what you choose, Eothas will stop and take notice - and admire your ability to navigate the In-Between. He will then promise that something beautiful is coming, that the sun will yet rise - and terminate the connection, restoring the pillar of luminous adra.
Once you exit the sub-level, you will meet hundreds of souls who refuse to go to the adra pillar for fear of never reaching the Beyond and they will ask to follow you. You can accept them. If
Xoti
is present, you can permit her to harvest the souls, upon which some of the souls will head to her.
Return to Clario
Return to the Governor. You can lie that you have not found any of
Oderisi's research
, retaining them for yourself and later selling them to
Flaune Elette
for a tidy profit of
1,000
. Otherwise, Clario will be giddy and relieved that
Port Maje
will continue to receive VTC support and funds. He will also attempt to dissuade you from your attempt to follow Eothas. As a
Priest
/
Mystic
,
Paladin
, or
Explorer
, you can explain your desire to pursue. Then repeat your request for aid. With
Intellect
12, you can surmise that Eothas is using adra pillars for an energy source, allowing you to find him.
Governor Clario will arrange for a team of workers to haul my ship back out to sea. Once you are ready to leave the island, meet his people out at the beach by
the Defiant
:
You'll be met by a mix of Huana and Vailian workers led by
Storm Speaker Ikawha
, who will keep the storm at bay just enough for you to set sail.
Otherwise if you killed
Storm Speaker Ikawha
, you'll be met by a group of Vailian workers led by
The Reckless
.
Killing Clario (alternate)
If you decide to kill Clario, you won't be able to raise the Defiant using
Port Maje
. Once you complete
Helping Hands
(where you recover your crew), and
To Hunt a God
(where you find out where Eothas is headed), return to the Defiant and talk to the Steward. She has good news, and says that the high tide in a week should submerge much of
Vilario's Rest
, allowing
The Defiant
to sail once again.
This starts a cutscene, after which the ship sets sail, placing you on the
world map
. Despite the Steward saying that it takes a week for the tide to come in, one week of in-game time does not pass.
One major downside of killing Clario is that your crew saved in
Helping Hands
(
Beodul
,
Eld Engrim
,
Irrena
,
Chitupec
, and
Vela
) will not join you, and will remain at
Vilario's Rest
forever (this is possibly a bug).
Setting sail
Leaving the island completes the quest, and starts
Basic Provisions
,
Skeleton Crew
, and
An Honored Guest
(the
Beast of Winter
introductory quest).
God conversation
Shortly after completion of
Stranded
on
Port Maje Island
, the Watcher will commune with
Berath
and the other gods regarding
Eothas
' intentions. This interaction occurs as a random event while sailing, sometimes immediately after leaving the port.
Your choices of dialogue during this conversation will determine which upgrade to
Visage of Death's Herald
the Watcher receives. Also see
A Distant Light
and
He Waits in Fire
for later god conversations and strategies around upgrading this Watcher-specific ability.
A score is tallied representing the amount of times the Watcher made a response of a particular Disposition. Each disposition is associated with an upgrade to
Visage of Death's Herald
. You must choose at least 3 of the dialogue options for the associated ability, and your final "score" must be greater than that of the other two scores, with exceptions - see below.
Upgrades
In order of precedence. All of the following abilities are upgraded from
Visage of Death's Herald
.
If you have 3 or more Aggressive responses, and have more Aggressive responses than Diplomatic and Sassy responses
Grants
Ire of Death's Herald
If you have 3 or more Sassy responses, and have more Sassy responses than Diplomatic responses
Grants
Wit of Death's Herald
If you have 3 or more Diplomatic responses:
Grants
Mien of Death's Herald
If you have less than 3 of Diplomatic, Aggressive, or Sassy responses
No changes (keep
Visage of Death's Herald
).

600
Aefre
moves into
The Salty Mast
Technical
Quest ID
03_qst_the_taint
Supply and Demand
is a
side quest
in
Pillars of Eternity
.
Synopsis
Street thugs are harassing prostitutes and clients at the
Salty Mast
.
Maea
, the proprietress, would like to put an end to it, but the agitators may have their own grievances.
Overview
Talk to
Maea
in the
Salty Mast
in
Ondra's Gift
.
Talk to
Aefre
in the
Ramshackle House
in Ondra's Gift.
Return to Maea and complete the quest or agree to talk with
Bricanta Doemenel
.
Talk to Bricanta Doemenel on the second floor of
Doemenel Manor
in
Brackenbury
.
Complete
Hard Feelings
.
Return to Maea and complete the quest.
Walkthrough
Witness the assault outside the
Salty Mast
and enter the establishment.
Speak to
Maea
and accept her request to investigate the thugs harassing her clients.
Leave the Salty Mast and be confronted by a gang of thugs. Defeat them and retrieve a
key
from their corpses.
Use the key to open the door to the
Ramshackle House
near the
Lighthouse
in
Ondra's Gift
(or pick the lock with
Mechanics
4 and above).
Speak with
Aefre
, a lieutenant with the
Dozens
. Depending on your
disposition
(
Rational
2 /
Benevolent
2 /
Honest
2) and Attribute scores (
Intellect
15 /
Resolve
16), you can:
Agree with her and offer to speak to Maea about lowering prices.
Persuade or threaten Aefre into stopping her harassment of the Salty Mast.
You may of course also directly attack Aefre and her thugs. (After the fight, you may take Aefre prisoner if you have a prison built in
Caed Nua
.)
Return to the Salty Mast and talk to Maea. Depending on how the conversation with Aefre went, you have the following options:
Tell her that you have taken care of the problem (in some way or another) and get your reward. This completes the quest.
Convince (
Intellect
17 or 18) or intimidate (
Resolve
16) her into lowering her prices. This also completes the quest.
Talk to her about her prices and offer to talk to
Bricanta Doemenel
about lowering the prices of bitter squash. This is a roundabout way to solve this and gain major positive reputation.
If you have agreed to speak with Bricanta Doemenel, there is still work to be done:
Travel to
Doemenel Manor
in
Brackenbury
. If you do not already have access to the second floor, talk to the
Doemenel Doorman
and tell him that you have business with Bricanta Doemenel to do.
Talk to her and agree to do her a favor first (if you haven't already).
Complete Bricanta's
quest
, then get her to lower the bitter squash prices.
Return to Maea for your reward and complete this quest.
Optional:
If you complete the quest peacefully by arranging a lower price, either by convincing Maea to do it herself or by making an agreement with Bricanta Doemenel, Aefre can later be found on the second floor of The Salty Mast, where speaking to her will gain you a Minor Positive reputation bonus with
The Dozens
.
Companion reactions
Some companions react hilariously when they hear that Maea will arrange a nice reward for you if you manage to solve her problems:
Aloth
:
Aloth wipes his hands on his robes, looking around. "I would... request that thanks in copper if I were you."
Durance
:
There is a look in Durance's eyes like a lion that has stumbled upon a herd of deer. His knuckles are white as he clutches his staff with both hands as he surveys the room. He laughs to himself, low and rumbling.
Edér
:
"If she means to bake us something, my vote's for cookies."
Hiravias
:
"Well if you aren't interested in the reward, I'll solve her problem myself!"
Kana
:
"I'd rather just go chat up that guard with the wide hips, personally. But we ought to stop her people getting thrashed, at least."
Pallegina
:
Pallegina's golden eyes roll skyward.
Sagani
:
Sagani glances over at Kana. "Cover your ears, lad."
If you solved the task in such a way that Maea offers you a discount, some companions will react very funny to the outcome:
Aloth
:
"Um, you go ahead. Really. I'll just wait by the bar. With a glass of... something that kills disease."
Edér
:
"So... she's not baking?"
Hiravias
:
"A discount! Well, in that case, I can't afford NOT to sample the house specials!" Hiravias' hands dart about his tunic for loose copper. "You're all welcome to watch. Anyone? Anyone?" He shrugs and continues to search his clothes for coins. "Your loss."
Kana
:
"Might as well have a drink or two. This seems like as good a place as any to pick up a few sordid secrets. And, er, sundry diseases, at that..."
Pallegina
:
"Patronizing brothels isn't how I prefer to pass time, but don't let that stop you." Her eyebrows furrow in annoyance. "It certainly never stopped any of my freros."
Sagani
:
"Sagani raises one eyebrow. "Someone's given you 'the talk,' right? Because Kallu's the one who handles it with our children. But if no one's ever explained it to you..."

(n_Maea_Discount_Given = 1).

prostitutes
at

, either a deep discount (n_Maea_Discount_Given = 1), or a slight discount (n_Maea_Lowered_Prices = 1).

's "The Common Room" and "Shiny Pearl" (b_Maea_Free_Room = 1).

Bugs

). A workaround – other than simply attacking her – can be found

.
