Quest ID
FCT_QST_Principi_Undead
Guid
25f42850-b2f2-49fc-8d8b-2ea35b4bfe7c
Symbols of Death
is a
faction quest
in
Pillars of Eternity II: Deadfire
.
Synopsis
Captain Furrante has sent a messenger to summon the Watcher to Dunnage. The Príncipi, head of the Consuaglo mes Casitàs, wishes to discuss a potential business opportunity.
Walkthrough
Head to
Dunnage
and the
Balefire Beacon
.
Captain Furrante
will cover matters with
Benweth
first, then proceed to the reason he asked you to come:
The Floating Hangman
, a ghost ship haunting the Deadfire. You can react with
Bluff
8 to boast of your encounters with it,
Arcana
8 to ask to document it, and
History
6 regarding the accounts of a ship flying the Darcozzi flag. The options give a Minor Positive reptuation with the Príncipi. Furrante will then summon
Selinia
, a survivor of the latest attack by the Hangman on their fleet. You can question her on the attack, then Furrante will dismiss her.
He will ask you to keep the affair a secret from Aeldys. You can use Perception 17 to figure out that he and Aeldys are at odds regarding the values of the Príncipi, use Good 1 reputation with the faction to reply in kind, or wait for him to speak. He will then direct you to
the Sacred Stair
in
Neketaka
, to find
Yseyr the Berathian
. If you completed
Eulogy for the Dead
, you already have all the knowledge you need to complete the quest right here and now. Otherwise, you need to complete
The Hanging Sepulchers
and find all the eulogy tablets.
Next, he will ask you for assistance in a delicate matter...
Notes
During or after the completion of this quest, you'll have an encounter with The Floating Hangman first hand - though they spare you:
Out of nowhere, a heavy fog rolls over the ocean, so dense that you cannot see more than a dozen feet out from your ship. With the fog's arrival, the winds die. The waves calm. And The Defiant is left aimlessly drifting, the only sounds that of the hull groaning as if in dread of what's to come.
Eerie green lights pierce through the fog as the silhouette of a massive galleon approaches. Black sails, although tattered and rotting, catch in an otherworldly wind, slicing the ship forward at a rapid pace.
The captain - a skeleton outfitted in tarnished armor, with glowing eyes, surveys you from the quarter deck of the decaying galleon. She raps two fleshless fingers against her chest before pointing right at you.
"What the goddess of Death has marked, I will leave untouched." She nods solemnly, once, then with a tilt of her skull, steers the ship away. As the Floating Hangman turns, several undead sailors shamble across the deck, hungry for a look at your crew. They scratch at the chipped and rotted railing, heads rolling on their exposed spines.
The ship disappears back into the fog. A few minutes pass before the fog fully dissipates and the winds pick back up, rustling your sails. The sea is clear, empty. The other ship is nowhere to be found.

Taking Out the Traders
Details

Total XP
8,100
XP type
Normal
XP level
14
Outcomes and rewards
Destroy the powderhouse
Related quests
Previous:
Fruitful Alliance
Next:
-
Concurrent:
The Coming Storm
Sabotage at the Brass Citadel
Technical
Quest ID
FCT_QST_05_Destroying_Rivals
Guid
189a5426-1670-42ca-aff2-dd7a0d795f1e
Taking Out the Traders
is a
faction quest
in
Pillars of Eternity II: Deadfire
.
Synopsis
Queen Onekaza has asked that the Watcher aid her in a plot to destroy Rauatai's powderhouse and implicate the
Vailian Trading Company
. This would debilitate Rauatai's armaments and disgrace the Vailian's colonial leadership in one fell swoop.
Walkthrough
Note:
Accepting this quest will cause
Maia
and
Pallegina
to leave the party immediately. They may be convinced to stay temporarily, but completing the quest by blowing up the powderhouse will always result in them leaving (see
consequences
below)
Infiltrating the powderhouse
The Queen leaves the sabotage to your discretion, though you can't leave witnesses (not a big deal, given that anyone detecting you will attack). Infiltrating the powderhouse can be done either way. See
Powderhouse
for more info.
Getting in
The sneaky way involves entering the powder house by picking the doors leading inside. The upper door can be picked with
Mechanics 15
or by using the appropriate
key
, and you can sneak in undetected. Alternatively you can go in the front door with
Bluff
12, to convince the guard at the door that the
Hazanui
gave you permission to enter. You'll need to make sure to be in stealth mode before entering, otherwise you're likely to be immediately detected.
The not-so-sneaky way involves going killing the guards and either obtaining the key for the upper door, or walking through the now-unguarded lower door.
Alternatively, you can make use of the VTC questline (
Sabotage at the Brass Citadel
) to be teleported inside.
Deal with the guards
The barrels dotted around make it extremely easy to pick off the guards, even from stealth as they can be attacked without drawing attention to yourself. If you want to do so from stealth, make sure to use a long range ability/weapon (preferably with low noise) to stay out of earshot, and make sure to retreat immediately after attacking. Best position is from the staircase or from the far southeastern corner. It's a good idea to use a low damage attack too, that way the barrel will be caught alight rather than explode immediately, which draws nearby guards towards it to investigate.
Plant the bomb
Make your way to the lower floor of the powder storage, and plant the explosive in the vat, located in the western part of the room.
It's entirely possible to plant the explosives without detection, with a high enough
Stealth
skill. Consider removing your companions from the party (double back to your ship after lockpicking the door - it will stay unlocked). Five people are a lot more visible than one.
Sneak out the way you came in, but be quick about it, since the powderhouse will go up around your ears if you tarry.
Post-powderhouse
After the deed is done, make your way back to the palace.
During the confrontation at the palace that follows, you can lean a number of ways:
Either follow through with with Onekaza's plan, which results in the VTC Director being banished to the Gullet and both of the trading companies getting significant setbacks. The RDC will be given the Director's holdings as recompense.
Reveal yourself as the culprit, which will result in a battle where you will have to kill the Prince and the Hazanui.
Use the opportunity to switch over to follow the
VTC questline
, by claiming the innocence of the VTC director.
Alternative
If you wish to save the VTC director and keep Maia, you can skip this quest and still side with the Huana by doing the following.
Before talking to the queen or the VTC director, first talk to
Hazanui Karū
. Tell her you will commit to the
Royal Deadfire Company
, but then tell her you can't go through with her plans to assassinate the queen.
Everyone will turn hostile and you'll have to fight your way out.
If you have Maia in your party she will NOT turn hostile. She will continue to stay in your party, even if you do side with the Huana afterwards. However, she will refuse to speak with you anymore.
Then speak with the queen. She will tell you she had plans for the trading company, but because you took action first, she will offer you aid right away after committing to the Huana.
Consequences
Completing this quest will make you lose both
Maia
and
Pallegina
as a companion, and force you to side with
Queen Onekaza II
for the final attack on
Eothas
.
Even if you switch Maia and Pallegina out before accepting the quest from the queen or don't bring them along, you won't be able to keep either in the party. You will later receive a letter from each (while sailing), explaining that as a result of your actions they cannot follow you any longer.
Pallegina's Farewell
.
Maia's Farewell
, if you don't include her in the party when accepting the quest, and leave "on good terms" with her.
The
Royal Deadfire Company
's opinion of you changes depending on whether you chose the stealth or violent route while sabotaging the powderhouse:
Stealth:
If you planted the explosives without being seen by anyone, you are still friendly with the people in the Brass Citadel and can go back there, though they will ask you to leave since someone sabotaged their powderhouse, and they're busy cleaning it up. If you try going past them , they'll attack.
Violence:
If you killed anyone during your infiltration, the rauatai in the Brass Citadel will attack you on sight if you show your face there.
Switching sides
Instead of continuing with the quest, you may instead take the opportunity to turn against the Huana and/or VTC. To do so, simply hand the
Timed Explosive
(s) to
Hazanui Karū
in
The Brass Citadel
(triggers minor
Pro-Rauatai reactivity). Doing so has the following consequences:
If you tell her that the
Vailian Trading Company
planned to destroy the powderhouse, this fails
Sabotage at the Brass Citadel
, fails all active VTC quests, and locks you out of all VTC questlines.
If you tell her that the
Huana
planned to destroy the powderhouse (triggers minor
Anti-Huana reactivity), this fails
Taking Out the Traders
, fails all active Huana quests, and locks you out of all Huana questlines.
If you tell her that both the
Vailian Trading Company
and the
Huana
planned to destroy the powderhouse, you get both of the above consequences, but also reactions from
Tekēhu
and
Pallegina
if they're in the party. Pallegina also gets major negative relationship.
If you're already allies with the faction you outed, you'll get no help from any faction during
The City Lost to Time
.
If you're not already allies with the faction you outed, you will no longer be able to ally with them during
The Coming Storm
.
Convincing Maia to stay temporarily
When accepting this quest with Maia as a companion in your party, the Queen will be hesitant to give you the details of the mission. Maia will leave the party immediately after accepting the quest, as the Queen's plan conflicts with Maia's duty.
However, when accepting this quest with Maia as a companion in your party, she will have additional dialogue before leaving, giving you the chance to convince her otherwise. This will allow you to
temporarily
keep Maia on board, instead of having her leave immediately, which lets you continue adventuring before proceeding with the quest. Keep in mind that after destroying the powderhouse, Maia will leave regardless of the checks passed here. There is no way to avoid this without using console commands, unless you resort to other means of completing the quest (see the
alternative
route above).
The simplest solution is by simply not having Maia in the party when accepting the quest. However this isn't always foreseeable, so the following assumes that Maia is kept in the party on accepting the quest, or on approach to the powderhouse.
In dialogue with
Queen Onekaza II
with Maia in the party upon starting the quest
All of the following checks are required to keep Maia on your team for the duration of the quest.
Pass one of the following checks in conversation, when Maia asks
"How do we get past this, captain?"
:
Diplomacy
5 or
Insight
4:
"You want to stay. You'll hate yourself if you don't."
Bluff
6:
"Whatever we do, you only have to worry about it if we get caught."
Be in any level of relationship with Maia:
"I need you to stay. You're a constant in my life, and I'm afraid of losing you."
When Maia says
"But Rauatai needs all hands on deck, and I won't just turn about on my career"
, respond with:
"You're an assassin. Let the company hire another"
(only if you learned Maia is an assassin during
The Courier's Calling
(
n_Maia_Quest_State ≥ 5
))
OR
"They have enough rifles and cannons to punch a hole in the Deadfire without you".
Complete
The Courier's Calling
with the best possible outcome, where Maia's friends
Tuaha
and
Harama
are still alive (
n_Quest_Harama_State = 2
,
n_Quest_Tuaha_State = 3
).
PASS:
"You were more than complicit in my... activities, captain. I seem to recall you handled yourself well enough." Maia nods slowly.
FAIL (either Tuaha or Harama died):
"One of my friends died. You think what I'm doing is easy, or that any of us aren't ready to make the same sacrifice?" She gives you a look of disgust.
FAIL (both Tuaha and Harama died):
"Both of my friends DIED, captain. I knew them a lot better than I know you, and even then I kept my vows to the navy."
Be currently at full romance with Maia (
n_Maia_Romance_State = 4
), and don't have a negative relationship with Maia.
PASS:
"And I want you to get out of this in one piece, captain. Eora is better when you're around, but..."
FAIL:
She regards you with fresh clarity, and by her bearing it's clear that she's made a decision she doesn't like.
Pass a silent
disposition
check where
Diplomatic
and
Shady
is NOT rank 2 or above.
PASS:
"Damn it. But nothing." Maia wipes her face with the back of her hand.
FAIL (Shady):
"I know I can't trust you."
FAIL (Diplomatic):
"You don't always know how to get a job done. It can be... more straightforward than you allow."
If you're successful, Maia will say
"Whatever you're planning, just... try and leave me out of it? You're holding the rifle. Don't make me pull the trigger."
(sets
b_Maia_Quit
to 1)
If you're unsuccessful, Maia will say
"You don't know what you're talking about." Maia squeezes her eyes shut and sighs.
and
"Not exactly how I wanted to end this friendship, but here we are." She shakes her head slowly.
On approach to the Powderhouse with Maia in the party
Another opportunity to convince Maia arises upon approaching the entrance to the Powderhouse in
The Brass Citadel
, via either the ramparts or the main gate. This only occurs if you did not speak to the Queen with Maia in the party in the conversation above (and therefore didn't get the chance to convince her):
She will say:
"I've gone this far with you, but I'm thinking this is where we part ways."
After which you can pass a subset of the above checks to achieve the same outcome as if you had succeeded earlier:
Say
"Stay with me. [Player Ship] needs you and I do as well."
Be currently at full romance with Maia (
n_Maia_Romance_State = 4
)
PASS:
"I... I know. I need you as well."
FAIL:
"I've given this a lot of thought already. We're no good for each other while we're working at odds. This hurts, but it's better."
Tuaha
and
Harama
still alive as a result of the quest
The Courier's Calling
(
n_Quest_Harama_State = 2
,
n_Quest_Tuaha_State = 3
).
PASS:
"You've helped me out of a tight spot before. Ishi and I don't forget that easily."
FAIL:
"I've given this a lot of thought already. We're no good for each other while we're working at odds. This hurts, but it's better."
Have a "very positive"
companion relationship
with Maia (shown as "2" in the Reputations tab of the character sheet).
Interestingly, a bit of dialogue exists for if you already convinced Maia, though since the conversation is only triggered if you didn't speak to or convince her, it will never be shown. In the case it happens to trigger, all dialogue options lead to:
"I suppose I'm with you for the long haul. Not that it gives me any peace of mind."
If you're successful, Maia will say:
"I... damn it. If I'm being honest, the idea of sailing without you is downright unappealing. "Guess I'll stay."
(sets
b_Maia_Quit
to 1)
Later you will need to ensure that Maia is NOT in the party upon entering the
Powderhouse
, as she will snitch on you if you are spotted (but notably not if you attack first), and will turn on you as soon as you enter the
Powderhouse Storage
, causing her to become hostile.
Note that the condition for Maia to leave the party (via missive and not immediately) will be set as soon as you kill the guards on the lower floor (with Maia in the party - you'll know because she says
"Captain... oh, no."
in bark dialogue), or when you plant the explosive. This sets
b_Maia_Exit
to 2, which is part of the
Maia's Farewell (post-Powderhouse)
missive condition.
If you are successful with either of the above solutions and convince her to stay, she will turn a blind eye for now and will later leave on good terms once you destroy the powderhouse (you receive
Maia's Farewell (post-Powderhouse)
instead of no letter at all). This influences Maia's
endings
, causing her to be dishonorably discharged and leave the RDC in her end-game slides.
Forcing Maia to stay via console commands
By using the in-game
console
, Maia can be forced to stay permanently even after blowing up the powderhouse. Naturally, this counts as cheating and will invalidate achievements.
If Maia has not yet left the party, and you haven't had a chance to convince her otherwise, you can force all the "convincing" conditions to be met prior to the conversation (assuming you don't already meet these conditions):
If you have yet to speak with Maia at the start of the quest during your conversation with the Queen (or
Director Castol
/
Lueva Alvari
if doing the VTC equivalent quest), use:
SetGlobalValue n_Maia_Romance_State 4 & SetGlobalValue n_Quest_Harama_State 2 & SetGlobalValue n_Quest_Tuaha_State 3 & SetGlobalValue n_Maia_Quest_State 5
before starting the conversation. After this, proceed to "THEN" below.
If you have spoken with the Queen without Maia in the party, but haven't yet blown up the powderhouse, you still have the pre-powderhouse chat to convince her. You'll have to use a slightly different set of commands before approaching the entrance to the Powderhouse with Maia in tow:
SetGlobalValue n_Maia_Romance_State 4 & SetGlobalValue n_Quest_Harama_State 2 & SetGlobalValue n_Quest_Tuaha_State 3
CompanionAddRelationship Companion_Maia(Clone) oei_player Positive Major false & CompanionAddRelationship Companion_Maia(Clone) oei_player Positive Major false & CompanionAddRelationship Companion_Maia(Clone) oei_player Positive Major false & CompanionAddRelationship Companion_Maia(Clone) oei_player Positive Major false
RepeatCommands 1 & RepeatCommands 1 & RepeatCommands 1 & RepeatCommands 1 & RepeatCommands 1
Proceed below.
THEN
If you just (or have already) convinced Maia to stay in the party and she has not already left, continue as normal with the quest and sabotage the powderhouse (boot her from the party first of course). Any time after placing the explosive, but prior to travelling on the world map (with 1 hour of leeway after leaving port), run
SetGlobalValue
b_Maia_Exit 0
, then optionally
SetGlobalValue
n_Maia_Romance_State 4
to maintain the romance state. This will cause the missive condition to fail, and will prevent Maia from leaving.
If Maia has already left the party (either via missive or in person) she will have to be re-added to the party. Do so after blowing up the powderhouse, otherwise she will leave again:
If she left the party on the world map (via missive), if you have left a zone (a collection of areas), or if you have left the immediate map and reloaded (any of which would clear the character from the cache), a new instance will need to be added. Only run this if
PrintInstance
b1a7e802-0000-0000-0000-000000000000
returns null:
SpawnPrefabAtMouse
Companion_Maia
, then continue below.
If an instance of Maia still exists, use
AddToParty
Companion_Maia(Clone)
, then run
AISetScriptedUseObject b1a7e802-0000-0000-0000-000000000000 00000000-0000-0000-0000-000000000000 & AISetScriptedBehavior b1a7e802-0000-0000-0000-000000000000 None & ActivateObject b1a7e802-0000-0000-0000-000000000000 true & SetGlobalValue b_Maia_Left 0 & SetGlobalValue b_Maia_Exit 0 & SetGlobalValue n_Maia_Romance_State 4
Trivia
The powderhouse explosion sequence occurs in real time. Standing around or engaging in combat for too long will run down the clock, bringing the building down around your head.
If you speak to Maia after blowing up the powderhouse but before leaving port, she refuses to speak to you:
Maia simply glares at you.
Journal

Quest ID
FCT_QST_VTC01_Poko_Kohara
Guid
c0f05e98-ced9-4245-ad8f-ba4552eb81bc
Terms of Trade
is a
faction quest
in
Pillars of Eternity II: Deadfire
.
Synopsis
The Vailian Trading Company sent an expeditionary force to find the island of Poko Kohara. They never returned. The Watcher is  asked by the Vailian Trading Company to track down the original expedition to Poko Kohara and, if necessary, complete their assignment. The first step is sailing to Tikawara.
Walkthrough
This is a faction quest tied to
The Storms of Poko Kohara
. The VTC wants the pillar restored, while the RDC wants it destroyed permanently.
Return to the governor once the quest is complete. She will escort you to
Director Castol
who will debrief you on the quest, thanking you for providing information as to the outcome of the expedition.
If you restored the pillar, you will gain Major Positive reputation with the VTC, and
1,700
.
If you destroyed the pillar, you will gain Minor Positive reputation with the VTC, and
1,700
.
Bugs
Upon first speaking with
Lueva Alvari
, leaving the dialogue before asking "What kind of work?" will cause the quest to be unobtainable (until much later), since the dialogue used to initiate the quest will not appear the next time you speak with her.
Journal

The Animancy Method

Quest ID
0101_cq_fiormesiverno
Guid
7E5D1BD2-D33245CD-85E66E57-113C3303
The Animancy Method
is a
quest
in
Avowed
.
Synopsis
Investigating the Dreamscourge has pointed you toward Fior mes Ivèrno, an intellectual haven of animancers. Ambassador Hylgard told you the governor would be a point of contact.
Walkthrough
Travel to Fior mes Ivèrno
Go east from
Paradis
through the
Southern Embrace
. Guard
Edda
will allow you to pass, though hints you should complete
Dawntreader
if not already having done so. Once in the
Emerald Stair
, you will be forced to camp. Then, you will encounter
Yatzli
who will start the quest
Scofflaw's Vase
.
Follow the quest marker to enter
Fior mes Ivèrno
then speak with
Captain Aelfyr
. Continue into the city until locating
Governor Ignasi Vidarro
, who is in the middle of an argument with
Giatta
. The conversation will end with Giatta being banished from Fior.
Speak With Giatta
Go south west of the Vidarro Family Mansion to Giatta's House. She will send you to find three animancers:
Consult with
Monto Pastor
Monto will request an Adragan heart, which can be found in
The Delemgan Glade
. Successfully sneaking through the glade will result in the
Delemgan Queen
giving it freely without a fight.
Consult with
Elia Rosell
Retrieve the
Blighted Crop
from the nearby revenant
Ask Elia about the dreamscourge, who will in turn ask that you kill
Sílvo
, a rogue revenant
Before the barn, to the right among the rocks, climb through a small tunnel to access the barn
Constul with
Ivette Riggo
and
Remi Soler
Answer their questions about being a Godlike
Retrieve Giatta's Phantasmal Focus
Return to Giatta after helping all the animancers. She will then request that you go to her basement and retrieve one of her family's inventions, the
Phantasmal Focus
. In the basement you will discover that her parents have turned into trees and are presumed dead.

Normal

2,500
Moderate Positive Reputation with Huana
Return the tablet to Tumara
2,500
Moderate Positive Reputation with RDC
Related quests
Previous:
-
Next:
A Cordial Invitation
Technical
Quest ID
05_QST_Arkemyr_Vault
Guid
553369bf-7bb6-4702-ad78-3c7ac8423772
The Archmage's Vault
is a
side quest
in
Pillars of Eternity II: Deadfire
.
Synopsis
You must find a way inside Archmage Arkemyr's Manor, in
Periki's Overlook
, to retrieve the Harapo Epic - an ancient tablet said to contain clues of the lost island of
Ukaizo
and a priceless historical artifact for the whole of
Huana
. Scholars want it particularly badly, to analyze the language structure, narrative voice, and use of metaphor to determine when and how it was made. For the Rauataians, it has historical purposes, and is only useful as a clue for where Ukaizo may be.
Walkthrough
Find a way inside Arkemyr's Manor
The front door is locked. The key can be attained by pickpocketing it from
Fassina
in the Dark Cupboard in Periki's Overlook or completing her quest
Velvet Glove, Brass Fist
and saving Bertenno from RDC slavery.
From the top of the Dark Cupboard's window, the player can use a grappling hook with Athletics 3 to sneak to the roof of Arkemyr's Manor.
Talk to
Ifren
in The Dark Cupboard to discover that he and an orlan partner previously tried to break into the manor via the natural rock passages underneath the
Luminous Bathhouse
. At the back of the boiler room of the bathhouse there is a crack in the wall. This will take you into Arkemyr's basement. In order to gain entry you must break down a recently built wall using 16 Might or by using a
Hammer and Chisel
.
The manor is full of patrolling
Ironclad constructs
and
Imps
, as well as a number of other enemies downstairs in the laboratory. While the imps are fairly weak, the automatons certainly make up for that, and can be difficult to take down depending on your level.
To make things easier, head up to the bedroom (accessed from the manor's rooftop) and grab
Arkemyr's Old Robe
from the wardrobe beside his bed, and
Arkemyr's Notes
from the desk drawer. Wearing the robe will allow you to explore the manor freely. Posing as Arkemyr requires the Watcher to lie to the imps (
Shady
).
Locate Arkemyr's Vault
The vault is located in the basement of the Manor, in the Laboratory.
The laboratory contains a few more enemies than upstairs, more
Imps
,
Ironclad constructs
, as well as additional
Storm blights
and a
Steelclad Prototype
.
You can interact with a soul in the room to the north, an orlan thief (an associate of
Ifren
) who was killed by the constructs while trying to break into the vault - and now destined to be transferred into a construct. If you have
Xoti
in your party, she will harvest the soul.
Unlock Arkemyr's Vault
The vault is protected by some kind of combination lock involving rare gems and a combination.
The
combination
can be found in a bookcase in Arkemyr's Study on the ground floor. Alternatively, you can get the combination by reading the soul of the orlan thief.
Yellow-Eye Three
, an imp in the second floor lounge has the gemstone - a
Translucent Crystal
. You can either pickpocket it from the imp, loot it from his dead body, or convince him to give it to you. Combined with 5
Mechanics
(or a 9
Mechanics
on a companion), however, you can workaround the missing gem and enter the combination without it.
To assure the imp that you're no intruder, you'll require either 8
Bluff
or 9
Diplomacy
. Alternatively, if you have
Arkemyr's Old Robe
equipped, you can also disguise yourself as Arkemyr, and lie to the imp - saying that you're "..testing out a new illusion". He'll ask for a passphrase, to which you can respond with 8
Bluff
to convince him that you've stopped using passphrases, or by providing the true phrase "Cider and cheese", available only if you have read
Arkemyr's Notes
.
Asking about the crystal will allow you to persuade Yellow Eye to hand it over. You can do this by threatening him with 4 Intimidate, tricking him into thinking that the crystal is cursed with 2
Bluff
, or by pretending that you're an imp too with 3
Streetwise
or 3
Arcana
. Most of these dialogue options can be followed up with additional optional checks. He'll also be willing to give the crystal to you in exchange for
500
. If you have convinced Yellow-Eye Three that you are Arkemyr, his master, he'll hurriedly give you the crystal if you ask about it.
Once you have everything, head down to the vault. Opening it is straightforward, place the crystal in the recess, and enter the combination. After opening the vault, all the creatures in the area will become hostile, so make sure to do this after exploring the rest of the lab, unless you posed as Arkemyr, in which case they will remain passive
Retrieve the tablet from the Vault
.
The vault itself contains many treasures and artifacts, mostly in locked chests of varying difficulty, including
Ninagauth's Teachings
,
Cloak of Greater Protection
,
Brass Teo
(x123),
Stone of Power
,
Ivory Wurm Figurine
,
Cabalist's Gambeson
and a number of other items. Careful though, as there are a number of traps on the floor here.
The
Harapo Epic
is contained in the southern-most chest.
As the manor will be on alert, you can safely exit via the passage behind the wall, just down the hall from the vault. It leads back to the
Luminous Bathhouse
boiler room.
Return the artifact to Tumara or Netehe
:
Tumara awaits outside of
Imperial Command
at
The Brass Citadel
, and
Netehe
in front of the
Kahanga Palace
in
Serpent's Crown
.
Both will reward you with
2,500
and a moderate reputation increase with their respective factions. You will also receive and invitation to Arkemyr's mansion from the Archmage himself. Ooops.
Upon completion of the quest,
Fassina
, the shop keeper at The Dark Cupboard can be recruited as a sidekick.
Gallery
Interaction 1
Interaction 2
Interaction 3
From
The Dark Cupboard
's window:
Tunnel from
Luminous Bathhouse
boiler room to the manor:
Interation with the vault's locking mechanism:

Technical
Quest ID
cp_qst_hunt_down_lka
The Assassin at Large
is a
main quest
in
Pillars of Eternity
. It starts automatically upon completing the second act.
Synopsis
Thaos has killed the duc and Lady Webb, and sent Defiance Bay into chaos. But Lady Webb saw where he intended to go next - to the city of Twin Elms. It is there the Watcher must go if there is any chance of catching up to him.
Walkthrough
Upon completion of the previous main storyline quest (
The Hermit of Hadret House
), head to
Lady Webb
and interact with her soul to learn that Thaos finally caught up with and killed her. You will automatically leave the burning
Defiance Bay
and land at the
Aedelwan Bridge
. Make your way eastwards through
Stormwall Gorge
, where the flood has now conveniently receded, allowing you to travel onward to
Elmshore
and then
Hearthsong
.
Deal with the
Leaden Key
assassins on the way. If you choose answer
Aggressive
2, you can avoid a fight and take
Naelde
prisoner (assuming you have you have built the
Dungeons
at
Caed Nua
).
In
Hearthsong
, talk to
Anamenfath Bethwl
at the
The Passage of the Six
to gain access to
Oldsong
and
Elms' Reach
(you receive Twin Elms: Minor Positive).
Travel east to Elms' Reach, and talk to
Sîdha and Rîhenwn
who are standing in front of the entrance to
Teir Evron
on the east of the map. The quest will complete after the conversation.
Gallery
Journal

End states

Outcomes and rewards
Defeat Lord Gathbin
Gathbin Family Signet
Gauntlets of Ogre Might

Related quests
Previous:
A Return to Court
Next:
-
Technical
Quest ID
15_qst_yenwood_3
The Battle of Yenwood Field
is a
side quest
in
Pillars of Eternity
, added as of patch 3.0.
Synopsis
Lord Gathbin
is assembling an army of mercenaries in an attempt to wrest control of
Caed Nua
from the Watcher by force. The Watcher will need to raise their own army to stop him. To fight Lord Gathbin's forces,
Marshal Forwyn
will assist in the logistics of it all, coordinating things at the Great Hall in Caed Nua.
Walkthrough
Preparations
Speak to
Marshal Forwyn
. To maximize your chance of success, you must gain as much support as you can:
All
hirelings
employed at the start of the battle will fight alongside you.
Korgrak
may be helpful here.
Depending on your side choice, a contingent of the
Crucible Knights
,
the Dozens
or the
Doemenels
will assist you.
Ogres
from
Matron Beregan
(requires peaceful solution of
the Ogre Matron
).
Readceran
Pathfinders from
Adaryc
.
Pay
10,000
for an extra group of mercenaries.
When you feel ready, start the battle. You can choose to come along or travel for yourself to
Yenwood Field
(which will appear on the
world map
to the east of
Caed Nua
).
Scripted event
The first part of the battle is a scripted event.
The more groups of supporting troops you can acquire, the more of
Lord Gathbin
's forces are defeated during the dialogue, and the less you have to fight yourself afterwards.  To manage your assault correctly
Use Archers against the
Bleak Walkers
,
Ogres against the Battlemages,
Crucible Knights / Dozens / Doemenels against Berserkers.
This will cause his regulars to flee from battle.
Emery
, if she's still alive, you managed to impress her during
a Return to Court
, and you're winning the battle, will join you by betraying Lord Gathbin.
Combat sequence
Your party, your hirelings, and many of your soldiers will then face off against Gathbin and his remaining forces in combat.
After the fight, you will be asked to forward some of your tax income to Erl
Bademar
. If you do so, your tax harvest will be diminished (17.5% of your keep profit every turn will from now on be given to the Earl), but if you object, you will receive
Defiance Bay
: Extraordinary Negative.
Behind the scenes
The audio in
Yenwood Field
was recorded at Obsidian. The audio designers got a number of Obsidian employees together in the audio booth to scream and shout as if they were in a battle, which supposedly resulted in a noise complaint from the companies on the floor above and below their offices.

The Brass Empire
is a
faction quest
in
Pillars of Eternity II: Deadfire
.
Synopsis
Hazanui Karū leads the Rauataian forces in the region. Through the Royal Deadfire Company, Rauatai has territories around the archipelago. That makes the hazanui - and her people - a power of note.
Walkthrough
This quest can be started either upon witnessing the meeting between faction delegates in
Kahanga Palace
(where you are first introduced to
Maia Rua
), or during general dialogue with
Queen Onekaza II
.
To complete the quest, simply head to the
Imperial Command
and talk to the
Hazanui
.
Speak to
Atsura
downstairs to start the next RDC quest
Dim Prospects
, and the overarching quest
The Storms of Poko Kohara
.

The Bridge Ablaze
[BW]
Details
Type
Main quest
Level
15
Quest region
The Dead Floe
Companion
Edér
Xoti
Locations
The Bridge Ablaze
Experience gained
Total XP
1,552
XP type
Normal
XP level
15
Related quests
Concurrent:
Death's Depthless Dominion
Technical
Quest ID
LAX02_qst_critical_path_quest_03_godhmr_plane
Guid
f70feb43-e522-47f8-b10f-a457c8308c11
This article is about the quest in
Beast of Winter
. For the location, see
The Bridge Ablaze
.
The Bridge Ablaze
is a
quest
in
Beast of Winter
.
Synopsis
Far younger than the other two echo realms, this is the
Evon Dewr Bridge
at the Citadel, frozen in the moment just miliseconds before the Godhammer was detonated. The battle is frozen in time - and the Watcher needs to figure out why.
Walkthrough
One of the more interesting places in Rymrgand's realm. After taking in the splendor of the area, interact with
St. Waidwen
, then reach out to his soul. The following exchange, captured in the moment before the detonation. As you talk to Waidwen, it becomes plain that he is actually trapped in an endless cycle where the last moment of the
Saint's War
repeats endlessly. Regardless of what you do, the events will culminate in an explosion again. This will transition into the Moment of destruction, as Eothas was shattered by the Godhammer and just Waidwen was left behind. Collect
Waidwen's Sundial
from around his neck, then fight off the spirits and talk to
Eothas
.
Your next task is to collect the fragments of Waidwen buoying this realm and let him become one together. You will need to pass through each moment of detonation to collect 4 pieces of him. The first is located atop the tower to the west, accessed from the entrance in front of the soldiers dying to sunbeams. The next needs to be collected by traveling to the different moments. There are three, each accessed with the Sundial:
Dawn: The Moment Before (when you enter)
Noon: The Moment Of
Dusk: The Moment After (where you landed)
Using the ray of light before Waidwen and travel to the Moment After (Dusk).  Collect the
Evon Dewr tower key
from Waidwen’s remains.  Use the portal to transport you to under the bridge. Use the ray of light there to travel to the Moment Before (Dawn) and talk to the priest (it is very difficult to see her) to discover that the detonation mechanism for the bomb, hidden by a powerful illusion spell, was a shard of adra containing a fragment of Waidwen's soul suspended between spiked copper plates. This magical fuze was activated by Waidwen's proximity - a bomb for Waidwen, made
from
Waidwen. Exit the door on the left and go to the Tower, using the tower key to unlock the dock. Retrieve the
Memory of Magranite Incantations
from a barrel on top and return to the priest. You can extract a fragment of Waidwen's soul - either by detonating the bomb (thus retaining the sequence of events), or using a Magranite Priest/Fire Godlike background or an Explosives 17/Sleight of Hand 15 check to carefully remove it.
If you spoke to the chained spirit in the Queries, you can use
Betrayal
to assume
Durance
's form and force the priest to reveal the construction of the bomb. Then you can imitate Durance (or refuse to do so out of disgust at Durance) and gain the schematics. Disarming the bomb grants you the
Detonator Shard
, a little piece of a time paradox.
That same ray of light reached through the portal can transport you to the Moment Of (Noon), where another Wandering Soul awaits.
Then you can use the next ray to travel to the Moment Before (Dawn), and head to the Sunlight patch by the area entrance. Access the Moment Of (Noon) to collect some minor loot or to the Moment After (Dusk) to grab the final part of his soul, located under the bridge.
The fourth part reveals that in the end, Waidwen was a firm believer - and when faced with the inevitable end of the march, he did not flinch. He finally understood that the kith were supposed to stand against the dawn together, not rely on it (and that Eothas knew about the bomb all along).
Using the Sunlight patch next to the initial entrance, travel to the Moment Before (Dawn) to return to Waidwen. You can ask him to aid against either
Neriscyrlas
, Eothas or let him just descend into the void and his soul will dissolve. To fight the the dragon you will have to argue with him. Once persuaded he will exclaim "lead the way" and exit through a portal. If you convince him to stand with you against Eothas he will accompany the (invisible) trail of souls behind you.
Journal

Technical
Quest ID
01_qst_mercenaries_for_hire
The Bronze Beneath the Lake
is a
side quest
in
Pillars of Eternity
.
Warning:
Accepting this quest means choosing The Dozens as your faction in
Defiance Bay
, meaning you can no longer ally with nor perform quests for
House Doemenel
or the
Knights of the Crucible
.
Synopsis
Wenan
fears a new scheme by the
Crucible Knights
and is looking for powerful weapons to hold them back. He asks the Watcher to find mighty
Engwithan
weaponry from the ruins of
Lle a Rhemen
. A rival adventuring group,
Byne
's Giantslayers, was sent but has been unsuccessful.
Walkthrough
You will receive the quest from
Wenan
in
Admeth's Den
after completing
Rogue Knight
.
Travel to the
Woodend Plains
to meet with the Giantslayers. Their encampment is located on the south end of the map.
Either persuade or kill the Giantslayers to get the
Engraved Adra Disc
. You have several options to persuade:
Might
17 intimidates them.
Dexterity
17 allows you to pick
Byne
's pocket without causing a fuss.
Resolve
or
Intellect
16 allow you to convince him to back off.
Travel to
Stormwall Gorge
and approach the stone circle. Defeat Commander
Cafel
and the rest of the Knights.
Wait until night and insert the
Engraved Adra Disc
into the slot for the water to recede.
Enter
Lle a Rhemen
. Make your way past the cobweb area.
Locate and speak with
Nridek
. Either choose to let it go or kill it.
Note that killing Nridek is necessary to enchant the unique spear
Cladhalíath
.
Approach the sarcophagus behind Nridek and collect the ancient Engwithan weapon.
Return to Wenan in Admeth's Den to receive your reward.
Notes
Accepting this quest bars you from taking
The Changing of the Guard
(for
House Doemenel
) or
Winds of Steel
(for the
Knights of the Crucible
).
Completing this quest allows you to get an invitation to the
Animancy hearings
from
The Dozens
.
The dialog choice with
Wenan
about the
Crucible Knights
affects the
end-game slides
for Defiance Bay.

(XP = Experience points, ST = Stronghold turns)

Outcomes and rewards
Kill Lord Raedric
He Carries Many Scars
Gilded Vale
: Extraordinary Positive reputation
Related quests
Previous:
Lord of a Barren Land
Next:
-
Technical
Quest ID
07_qst_deathguard
The Champion of Berath
is a
side quest
in
Pillars of Eternity
.
It is available only if the player chose to kill Raedric in
Lord of a Barren Land
, and is received from a
Frightened Villager
at the
Celestial Sapling
in
Hearthsong
.  The quest can also be started by speaking to the statue in Caed Nua, who will give you a letter from Raedric himself.
Synopsis
“
You have done me grievous injury, and I will answer in kind. Know this: Your efforts in Gilded Vale have brought only ruin upon you, for I will not suffer you to walk Eora in peace while I draw breath. My subjects will pull your keep down to its foundations, brick by brick, until you return to face your final judgment in honorable combat...
”
Called upon to slay Lord
Raedric VII
in
Lord of a Barren Land
, the Watcher accepted and fulfilled the grim task of tyrannicide, assuming that with this act of violence
Gilded Vale
would be purged of his influence for good. Raedric, however, had other plans, and an encounter at the
Celestial Sapling
in
Twin Elms
with a guard from Gilded Vale reveals that he has returned from death to his keep to seek vengeance for his murder.
Kolsc
— if the Watcher installed him on the throne — is now dead at his hand. What is more, Raedric has issued an ultimatum: either the Watcher will return to face him again in person, or his army of the undead will raze
Caed Nua
to the ground.
Walkthrough
When you enter the area, you are met by a Fampyr. You can kill her, but if you ask her instead to take you to Raedric, it saves having to search for him. After you killed Raedric, you can recruit her for your
Stronghold
(-3 prestige, +5 security,
20
per day). Hiring is only possible if the quest
A Servant of Death
has been completed.
Raedric can be found on siege platform (top level of the ramparts, north-east), accessible from the middle of the sanctuary. When the fight starts, his first action is a wide-area fire attack that does a lot of damage.
Killing Raedric completes the quest. You gain an Extraordinary amount of Reputation with Gilded Vale, and a large amount of experience.
Afterward, the hold is abandoned, so if you didn't clean it out before, you can do so now with impunity.
Notes
If the player kills Raedric in Lord of a Barren Land but does not complete The Champion of Berath, the
ending
reveals that Raedric has returned and slaughtered the inhabitants of Gilded Vale.
The game files indicate that this quest may originally have been named
Deathguard
.

======================================================================
PAGE: The Champion of the Hunt
CATEGORIES: Pillars of Eternity II: Deadfire expansion quests, Seeker, Slayer, Survivor main quests

The Champion of the Hunt
[SSS]
Details
Type
Main quest
Level
17
Companion
Konstanten
Experience gained
Total XP
2,025
XP type
Major
XP level
16
Related quests
Previous:
The Crucible
Next:
The Hunter Hunted
Technical
Quest ID
LAX01_00_qst_main_quest_04
Guid
6cfff3ca-01e2-4b07-8069-98321de16103
The Champion of the Hunt
is a
quest
in
Seeker, Slayer, Survivor
.
Synopsis
By completing the challenges presented by one of the Faces, the Watcher has earned the right to represent one of the Faces of the Hunt in the Crucible's bloodiest battle. If they prove victorious, they will be named Champion of the Hunt - and become able to commune with the Faces of the Hunt themselves
Walkthrough
This is the penultimate challenge in the DLC, where you face off with two other packs in a free for all battle. Each of the packs represents a Face. After defeating them all, you will be permitted to commune with the Faces through the Pool of Memories.
Tips for the battle go here!
Once you commune with the Faces, you are drawn into
the Beyond
. In there, you will find the answers to the crisis plaguing Kazuwari, once again encountering all three souls first met during
the Crucible
:
Erūke
the kith soul behind the Surviving Face, will reveal that
Whehami
, the Slaying Face, severed the island's connection to the Beyond. Erūke wants this bond restored.
Aexica
, the kith soul behind the Seeking Face, wants you to defeat Whehami and restore the island's connection to the Beyond as well, but for reasons of her own.
Whehami
, on the other hand is holding Galawain's realm hostage, severed from the rest of the Beyond. He wants another soul - yours - to take the place of the Faces of the Hunt so that he may walk again in the flesh.
Though centuries separated their lives from one another, Aexica, Erūke, and Whehami became the Faces of the Hunt at the same time. By interrogating the souls, you can learn that the three actually share a single soul. Some clever deduction on the Watcher's part will also reveal that Aexica provided Whehami with the knowledge he needed to sever Kazuwari's ties to the Beyond and that she manipulated Whehami into clogging the flow of souls between Kazuwari and the Beyond for her own ends.
The decision in this quest affects the ending for Kazuwari:
You can defeat Whehami in combat. Aexica will then attack you, and you will have to defeat her as well.
With Intimidate 22 and high Aggressive (unconfirmed) or Stoic reputation, you can convince Whehami to continue serving the Crucible as the Slaying Face and he will allow you to restore the flow of souls and leave.
If you chose to become the Slayer's champion during
The Crucible
by completing
The Slaying Face (quest)
, you have an intimidate 16 option, as well.
You can convince Whehami to switch his soul with someone else's. After speaking to Whehami about his plan, return to Aexica and ask her if it will work. This will unlock the option to propose a swap to Whehami. There are three possible trades:
You can sacrifice
Konstanten
's soul, to his and the rest of the party's dismay.
If you funneled soul energy into Muatu during your fights in the Crucible, you can offer him as a replacement. The rest of the party disapproves of this, as well..
If you chose to become the Slayer's champion during
The Crucible
by completing
The Slaying Face (quest)
(and received
Slayer's Claw
as your reward), you can convince Whehami that you will help him replace his soul with that of Galawain's beast in
The Hunter Hunted
.
If you chose to become the Seeker's champion during
The Crucible
by completing
The Seeking Face (quest)
and have some very high skills, you can expose Aexica's manipulation of Whehami and either kill her or convince them to stand down. There are three parts to this:
Discover that Aexica, Whehami, and Eruke are all the same soul by uncovering three pieces of evidence:
The first time you speak with Aexica, you
MUST
select the dialogue option asking if Whehami is the first to attempt to escape Toamowhai's realm ("Have others left their posting here, or is Whehami's attempt the first?") to make Aexica reveal that the three Faces have always been together. The option does not reappear in later conversations, and there is no way to get this information from her otherwise.
Speak with Whehami and ask him about the memory you saw when approaching him to make him reveal that he was awakened to the past lives of his soul as fighters in the Crucible.
Speak with Eruke about how many spirits are present in Toamowhai's realm, and whether there were other Faces before the current three.
After gathering all three pieces, speak to Eruke again with Metaphysics 12. Then, return to Aexica and inform her that the three are a single soul warring against itself and that they should stop fighting. Failing to complete this stage will prevent a completely peaceful solution.
Discover Aexica's desire to escape and her manipulation of Whehami to that end:
You can uncover Aexica's unrest with Insight 17 during your first conversation with her by asking if Whehami is the first to attempt to escape Toamowhai's realm, or with Insight 18 after speaking with Whehami by telling her Whehami's plan and asking if it will work.
Then, speak with Whehami and ask him directly about Aexica's wish to leave. He will not believe you, but will tell you about the
Tooth of Toamowhai
.
Return to Aexica and confront her about manipulating Whehami. She will deny it, but as her champion, you can request the truth. This is the only way to make her confess her plan.
Convince Aexica to abandon her scheme, or reveal her manipulations to Whehami:
After informing Aexica that you are aware she shares a soul with the other Faces and getting her to confess her manipulations, ask her to explain exactly how she was planning to escape. After her explanation (and some party banter), you will have a Diplomacy 20 option to convince her to abandon her scheme and set things right. Then return to Whehami and tell him that Aexica has admitted to manipulating him and wants to set things right. He will allow you to repair the flow of souls and leave.
Alternately, if you can't muster 20(!) Diplomacy or can't complete the discovery of the Faces' shared soul, you can go to Whehami after extracting Aexica's confession and tell him that she has admitted to manipulating him. He be angry and demand you kill Aexica, after which he will allow you to repair the flow of souls and leave.
If you chose to become the Survivor's champion during
The Crucible
by completing
The Surviving Face (quest)
, you can convince Whehami to stand down with Eruke's aid.
Discover that Aexica, Whehami, and Eruke are all the same soul via the same steps as above. You do not need to pass either Insight check with Aexica, but you do need to ask her if Whehami's escape attempt is the first of its kind during your initial conversation with her, and pass the Metaphysics 12 check with Eruke. Eruke will tell you to speak with Whehami.
Speak with Whehami about his shared soul with the other Faces. There appears to be some bugged dialogue here--you have to exhaust all three options to get through to him, but his reaction or any intended intervention by Eruke is missing. However, he will concede and let you repair the flow of souls and leave peacefully.
You can agree to Whehami's plan and replace him as the Slaying Face. In that case, after your companions make sure that you understand the consequences, your game ends there and you will see the ending slides (see below).
All steps finally require you to restore the flow of souls using the
Tooth of Toamowhai
.
Gallery
Ending slides if you allow Whehami to take your body, replacing him as the Slaying Face

Technical
Quest ID
04_qst_change_guard
The Changing of the Guard
is a
side quest
in
Pillars of Eternity
.
Warning:
Accepting this quest means choosing House Doemenel as your faction in
Defiance Bay
, meaning you can no longer ally with nor perform quests for
The Dozens
or the
Knights of the Crucible
.
Synopsis
Speak with
Gedmar Doemenel
after completing
A Two Story Job
, and he'll request that you murder Marshal
Wenfeld
of the
Knights of the Crucible
, and place a token of
The Dozens
(which he puts in your inventory) on a nearby altar to frame them for the murder. Before you leave the manor,
Abrecan Doemenel
tells you not to place the token on the altar so that everyone knows who is responsible.
Walkthrough
Go to the
Crucible Keep
in
First Fires
and wait in the altar (east side of the building).
Marshal
Wenfeld
will eventually stop by to pray, where you can then attack him. He casts priest spells and wears heavy armor, but should be no issue for a full party.
After you've killed Wenfeld, either place the Dozens token on the altar to frame The Dozens, or return to the manor without placing it.
Completing this quest gives
The Watcher
a bonus talent;
The Merciless Hand
, and the ability to hire a Doemenel Thief as a hireling at the stronghold for
20
per day (grants +2
and +4
). In addition you can choose a favor from
Gedmar Doemenel
, either money (
10,000
) or
Misery's End
.
Notes
Accepting this quest bars you from taking
The Bronze Beneath the Lake
(for
The Dozens
) or
Winds of Steel
(for the
Knights of the Crucible
).
Completing this quest allows you to get an invitation to the
Animancy hearings
from
House Doemenel
.
Your choice to pin the murder on the Dozens or not affects the
end-game slides
for Defiance Bay.
Journal

Outcomes and rewards
Finish the quest
Your choices influence
Pallegina
's
end-game slides
Technical
Quest ID
companion_qst_pallegina
The Child of Five Suns
is a
side quest
and
companion quest
for
Pallegina
in
Pillars of Eternity
.
Synopsis
Pallegina feels she has been wasting her time on trivial tasks in Defiance Bay. She believes there are more serious threats to the Vailian Republics and wants the Watcher's help in pursuing them.
Despite her best efforts, Ambassador Agosti has insisted that Pallegina carry out her orders to broker a trade agreement between the Vailian Republics and the Glanfathans with the anamenfath in Twin Elms. Such an agreement would steal trade away from the Dyrwood, an act that would increase tensions between the Dyrwood and the Republics.
Pallegina is caught between honoring the wishes of the ducs bels and pursuing the leads she believes will be more beneﬁcial to her country in the long run, which involve learning about animancy.
Walkthrough
Pallegina's personal quest is received after she joins your party at the end of
At All Costs
, by talking to
Vicent Agosti
in the
Vailian Embassy
in
First Fires
. The quest cannot be resolved until Act 3, when
Twin Elms
becomes available. Upon approaching the anamenfath at the
Passage of the Six
, Pallegina will confide in you that she has reservations about going through with the deal.
When you ask her what options she has, she responds:
"I could put the idea in the anamenfath's head that the Republics are not interested in exclusive trade, but limited trade of goods that they do not already trade with the Dyrwoodans."
To which you can reply with a number of options. Don't worry about your choice here, since the decision is entirely determined by your response in the upcoming dialogue with
Bethwl
(with one exception - see below)
"It's the right thing to do. The Dyrwoodans are at the mercy of the Republics and they don't even know it. Your actions today can prevent further suffering."
(
Benevolent
)
'"Follow your heart, Pallegina. You alone know what must be done."
(
Passionate
)
"If you do that, I doubt the ducs will be forgiving. Consider your options carefully, Pallegina. The ramifications for yourself and these nations could be great."
(
Diplomatic
)
"That's a terrible idea and the ducs will surely eject you from the brotherhood if you go through with it. Stop and think for a minute."
(
Rational
)
Otherwise you can choose to ignore her entirely when she brings it up
"You've had enough time to wrestle with your inner turmoil. I need to talk to the anamenfath and I'm not waiting on your conscience. Understand?"
(
Cruel
)
During her dialogue with
Bethwl
, you can either confirm what you said or deny it by the motion of your head. If you choose to nod or shake your head, she will always follow these choices regardless of your previous thoughts or dialogue.
[Nod head, smiling warmly.]
→
She will defy the ducs and preserve the current trade agreement between the Glanfathans and the Dyrwood.
[Shake head, eyes wide.]
→
She will follow the ducs orders, robbing the Dyrwood of their trades with the Glanfathans
[Shrug.]
→
She makes her own decision (see below).
Your decision here affects what proposal she will make, to two ultimate outcomes:
Follow her orders and establish an exclusive trade deal with the Glanfathans, stealing their trade away from the Dyrwood to benefit the economy of the Republics, though leading to increased tensions with the Dyrwood.
Disregard her orders and modify the trade deal with the Glanfathans, limiting it to goods that they do not already trade with the Dywoodans, preventing the Republics from stealing the Glanfathan trade away from the Dyrwood and preserving the relations between the two nations, though at the expense of her loyalty to the Republics and the chance of a more profitable outcome for the Republics.
Her choice, along with your own decisions at the end of the main quest
Memories of the Ancients
(primarily whether you distribute the souls to the
Dyrwood
or not) will be reflected in the
end-game slides
,
and will also influence Pallegina's initial location, introductory dialogue, back story, and what breastplate she wears in
Pillars of Eternity II: Deadfire
.
Pallegina's own decision (alternate)
However if you choose to shrug at Pallegina, she will be forced to make up her own mind, and her decision is entirely dependent on your dialogue with her throughout the quest, as well as in your previous conversation with her regarding the matter. If you have been "pro" Pallegina, and have supported her 3 or more times during the course of the quest, she will defy the ducs (equivalent to nodding above), otherwise she will follow her orders (equivalent to shaking head above).
Supporting Pallegina is done through 4 main dialogue chains - a minimum of 3 of these are required
(1st point) Firstly, during your conversation with
Vicent Agosti
after Pallegina joins the party, if you pick the following dialogue options:
"I understand, but we're still going to investigate it, responsibilities or not."
OR
[Lie] [Wink obnoxiously at Pallegina.] "I understand completely and will make sure she completes this important mission for the ducs."
(2nd point) Next, only if you you made
Stoic
dialogue choices, or if you took Pallegina's side in the conversation with
Vicent Agosti
:
Stoic against Vicent (requires all of the following):
[Remain silent.]
[Say nothing.]
[Say nothing.]
[Nod noncommittally.]
Side with Pallegina (requires only 3 of the following):
"Ambassador, please hear her out."
"Quit stonewalling and pay attention for once!"
"If it makes any difference, Ambassador, I'm already looking into the matters Pallegina has spoken of."
"What kind of spineless do-nothing are you? If you and the ducs aren't going to take her seriously, shove off!"
"Ambassador, that's some cold compassion considering what these people have been through."
"I understand, but we're still going to investigate it, responsibilities or not."
During your dialogue with Pallegina immediately following, if you fulfilled either of the above requirements, you must say the following:
If picked Stoic dialogue options:
Pallegina:
"And you couldn't say anything, really? Not a word the whole time."
"I'm an outsider. The discussion was between you and the ambassador."
"I think you're in the right, but I didn't want to make the ambassador any angrier."
If took Pallegina's side in the conversation
Pallegina:
"I appreciate that you stood up for me, but it's hard to persuade the ambassador to go against the ducs' orders."
"I know you're right, Pallegina. We may find a way for you to fulfill your obligations while looking into what's really going on around here. Don't worry."
You'll know if you're successful if she says:
"Enough wisdom for both of us. Now I just need to find out how to get to Twin Elms before the ambassador loses his patience."
(3rd point) Immediately after the above portion of conversation, regardless of if you are successful:
Pallegina:
She pauses in thought. "Still, I fear that making this deal with the Glanfathans will further weaken the Dyrwood and make trouble for the Republics in the years to come."
"Follow your heart. Do what you believe will be best for the Republics."
Pallegina:
"I wish I knew."
(4th point) Upon approaching the anamenfath
The Passage of the Six
, Pallegina has mixed feelings about the deal:
Pallegina:
"I could put the idea in the anamenfath's head that the Republics are not interested in exclusive trade, but limited trade of goods that they do not already trade with the Dyrwoodans."
"It's the right thing to do. The Dyrwoodans are at the mercy of the Republics and they don't even know it. Your actions today can prevent further suffering."
(
Benevolent
)
OR
'"Follow your heart, Pallegina. You alone know what must be done."
(
Passionate
)
Although you don't have to follow these steps (you can instead simply nod or shake your head, with no prior checks), it's an interesting alternate route nonetheless.
Gallery
Child of the Five Suns

End states
Pallegina followed orders.
30000
Pallegina followed her orders from the ducs in the Vailian Republics and brokered the trade agreement they insisted upon. However, stealing the Glanfathan trade away from the Dyrwood will lead to greater tensions between the Dyrwood and the Republics - possibly even war.
Pallegina altered the deal.
30001
Pallegina changed the terms of the trade agreement the ducs in the Vailian Republics had given her, preventing the Republics from stealing the Glanfathan trade. In doing so, she preserved relations between the Dyrwood and the Republics, but if the Republics ultimately gain nothing from this deal, Pallegina will lose her position in her order.
Fail State
30002
Pallegina died.

The City Lost to Time

Technical
Quest ID
CP_QST_12_Ukaizo
Guid
72910fdc-e97b-4cc2-b76c-ea3311d68d4f
The City Lost to Time
is a
quest
in
Pillars of Eternity II: Deadfire
.
Synopsis
The game's final story quest and endgame. The relatively short quest sees the Watcher travel to
Ukaizo
, fight its mechanical guardian, and confront Eothas as he prepares to finally break
the Wheel
.
Walkthrough
Sailing to Ukaizo
Use your best ship, or the one you received in your faction alliance quest, to finally sail to
Ukaizo
. Fire giant longships will attempt to intercept you. When at you arrive at the opening in
Magran's Teeth
in the northeast of the map, the game will warn you that you are arriving at the point of no return: you cannot go back to exploring the archipelago after this event.
In the following scripted event, you can either join the battle between your allies and their rival faction, or venture into the storm.
If you liberated
Scyorielaphas
, he'll help you out here by fighting off a guardian monster.
If you used the Rauataian submarine, you'll approach Ukaizo without further problems.
Ukaizo Harbor
As soon as you venture into
Ukaizo Harbor
, a dragonlike construct, the
Guardian of Ukaizo
, intercepts you. It's surprisingly talkative, but you'll have to fight it (unless Scyorielaphas has already destroyed it).
The Guardian initially summons several
Adra Animats
, then two
Engwithan Sentinels
, and finally two
Engwithan Hierarchs
who can Dominate your team (preventable by eating a
Captain's Banquet
beforehand). As the fight progresses, the Guardian will gain two additional heads and regenerate.
As always in boss fights, it is advisable to use your tank (a character built for defense) to occupy the Guardian, and the others to kill his henchmen and then damage the Guardian. This is the game's final boss fight, so don't hold back on your consumables (but rest up afterwards if you're wounded, as there is one last, lesser fight coming up).
Grand Promenade and Ondra's Spire
The next area accessible to you is the
Grand Promenade
. There's nothing of importance here except a spot in which to talk to your companions one last time. Go on to
Ondra's Spire
.
Atop the spire, shut down the machine to trigger another conversation with the gods.
Return to the Grand Promenade to see that one of the factions has braved the storms and caught up with you and won't listen to reason. The faction that comes after you will be your
strongest rival
, no matter who ends up at the island after you, there will be no negotiating. Put them down. Compared to the Guardian, they shouldn't pose any challenge at all. Proceed to
Mói Gweath
.
Mói Gweath
As you face
Eothas
one last time in a conversation, the quest concludes, together with the game. You bear witness to Eothas's shattering of
the Wheel
, and to its consequences.
Each of your companions will have their own questions for Eothas. If you've completed the
Beast of Winter
(DLC) and chose to have Waidwen follow in your trail of souls, he too will appear and have a short conversation with Eothas.
Journal

The Coming Storm
Details
Type
Main quest
Level
15
Quest region
Neketaka - Serpent's Crown
Locations
Neketaka
Dunnage
Experience gained
Total XP
10,800
XP type
Normal
XP level
15
Related quests
Previous:
He Waits in Fire
Next:
The City Lost to Time
Concurrent:
Taking Out the Traders
The Final Maneuver
A Vote of No Confidence
Sabotage at the Brass Citadel
Honor Among Thieves
A Dance with Death
Technical
Quest ID
CP_QST_Ukaizo_Preparations
Guid
980a5414-ce0e-43c9-a5e7-b2f209f035a3
The Coming Storm
is a
quest
in
Pillars of Eternity II: Deadfire
.
Synopsis
In this phase of the main quest, you need to ally yourself with one of the four
factions
in order to be able to reach
Eothas
at
Ukaizo
. The quest concludes after you finish the final faction quest of your chosen ally.
If you don't wish to ally with a faction (or manage to antagonize all four factions), you can try to reach Ukaizo on your own.
Walkthrough
Completing this quest requires you to follow one of the options below, in which you will decide who gets
Ukaizo
. The quests are mutually exclusive and when you talk to the faction leaders you will be asked to commit to their quest. In certain cases you can back away if you have good reputation with them, otherwise they may turn hostile. If you choose one of the "go it alone" options, no faction will control Ukaizo.
Options
Taking Out the Traders
(
Huana
): Return Ukaizo to the Huana and start a war between the trading companies. To achieve this, the Queen wants you to blow up the powder store at the
Brass Citadel
and pin the blame on the Vailian Trading Company. Onekaza pledges her support, and her
watershapers
will combine their powers to clear a path through the Mortar.
The Final Maneuver
(
Royal Deadfire Company
): Hazanui Karū is ready to seize control of the archipelago by assassinating the Queen. Doing so gives you use of a submarine
Blade of Takōwa
, to slip completely under the storm.
A Vote of No Confidence
and
Sabotage at the Brass Citadel
(
Vailian Trading Company
): The VTC plans to eliminate Rauatai from the archipelago... right after the matter of directorship is decided. The Vailian animancers will make use of their recent advances in teleportation technology, using the adra pillars to teleport your ship through the ethereal plane and to the other side of the storm.
Honor Among Thieves
and
A Dance with Death
(
Príncipi sen Patrena
): First, the matters of leadership need to be attended to, then you can reach Ukaizo with the ghost ship,
Floating Hangman
.
Go it alone (prepare your ship): If you choose to go at it alone, you will need the
Dragonwing Sails
and the
Blackwood Hull
. Otherwise, the storm of Ondra's Mortar will sink your ship.
Go it alone (take the Floating Hangman): Yet another option is to complete
A Dance with Death
up to the point of obtaining the undead galleon, and then proceed to Ukaizo without reporting back to the Príncipi leader at
Dunnage
. Since you do not ally with the Príncipi unless you report back to Dunnage with the undead galleon, this method will allow you to travel to Ukaizo without allying with any of the factions.
Gods meeting
The gods will elect to meet you when you set sail again. They will be having an argument, particularly between
Woedica
and
Magran
. The argument covers the steps the gods should take against Eothas, including the option of possessing their godlike children or killing them to empower themselves. Then they will task the Watcher with entering Ukaizo - and warn against the
Guardian of Ukaizo
left behind by
Engwith
, their progenitors. Then you're dismissed after they ask for your opinion.
Strongest rival
The faction you help the most and don't align with is identified as your
strongest rival
(denoted with the global variable
n_Strongest_Rival_Faction
). This faction will confront you at Ukaizo after completing
The Coming Storm
and will be factored into the end-game slides. If you sail to Ukaizo alone, the strongest faction will confront you, instead.
If you've allied with the RDC, your strongest rival can only be the VTC or the Príncipi (Huana is excluded).
If multiple factions share the highest strength, if you've allied with the Huana, VTC, or RDC, the strongest rival will default to the Príncipi.
If multiple factions share the highest strength and you're allied with the Príncipi, the strongest rival will default to the VTC.
The strongest rival is determined by completing these quests or taking these actions:

The Cornett's Call
Details
Type
Side quest

8

Neketaka - The Gullet

Dereo the Lean
Cloaked Messenger

Cloaked Messenger
Dereo the Lean
Takano
Ateira
Gwenfin
Locations
Dereo's Lair
Takano's Estate
Undercroft
Old City
Experience gained
Total XP
810
XP type
Normal
XP level
8
Outcomes and rewards
Find the mural
Kitchen Stove
Príncipi Colors
Triumph of Dereo the Lean
10% discount at
Ernezzo's Supplies
Technical
Quest ID
06_QST_Treasure_Hunt
Guid
5827ef09-f91e-432d-a8c0-f55e3c981417
The Cornett's Call
is a
side quest
in
Pillars of Eternity II: Deadfire
.
Synopsis
Dereo the Lean, a crime lord in the Gullet, seeks a valuable artifact known as the Cornett of Waves. However, it's only a part of a bigger effort to find one of Neketaka's long-lost treasures.
Overview
Talk to
Dereo the Lean
in
the Narrows
, or be visited by the
Cloaked Messenger
(after completing
Harsh Medicine
) to begin the quest.
Acquire the
Cornett of Waves
from
Takano's estate
in
Serpent's Crown
, either by stealing it, paying for it, or with a series of other checks.
Return to Dereo, and deal with
Ateira
and her gang on the way over.
Head to the
Old City
, either:
Via the
Undercroft
- speaking to
Gwenfin
to open the door leading to the
Old City Ruins
using the shell.
Via the lift in
the Gullet
, leading to the
Old City Overlook
itself.
Optionally:
Dive down into the pool in the center of the Old City Overlook (area with the lift) to retrieve the
Cornett of Depths
.
Open the Temple of Ondra (located in the northwestern section of the Old City proper) using the shell, kill or avoid the
giant cave grub
inside. If you have both shells, you can access the hidden room.
Inspect the mosaic on the rear wall of the temple, then return to Dereo to complete the quest.
Walkthrough
Cornett of Waves
Upon meeting Dereo, you will be asked for a favor: retrieve the
Cornett of Waves
from
Takano
at his estate. While talking to him, you can notice him wanting the artifact badly with
Perception
16 and can pursue the matter with
Insight
6 - though he will not reveal any more.
If you have already obtained it, you can also show him the
Cornett of Depths
to trigger the next phase of the quest. Otherwise, acquiring the Cornett of Waves is simple enough.
If you already have the
Cornett of Depths
and
you inspected the mosaic before talking to Dereo, you can immediately complete the quest without getting the other shell, but you will miss out on some loot and experience. Dereo will compliment the Watcher on their wondrous abilities.
Head to
Takano's estate
in
Serpent's Crown
.
Moon godlike
- Simply ask Takano for it and he will give it to you with no fuss.
Steal the cornett yourself
- It is found in a locked chest (difficulty 5) at the end of
Takano's estate
, next to the staircase. The
key
can be found in the wardrobe upstairs. Time the theft with the movement of the guard in this area.
Play on Takano's materialism
- Takano will decline an offer to pay him directly for the shell, saying that he already has everything that he could want. Reading his soul reveals otherwise, and allows you to suggest that he could use the money to spruce the manor up, perhaps add a new window. This will set you back
2,500
, or
2,150
with
Bluff
3, suggesting that Takano's neighbors boast of bigger houses, and that he should out-do them.
Convince Takano that the cornett deserves a better keeper
Ask
"Why is the Cornett of Waves so special?"
to discover a bit of the cornett's history, imparting a bit of your own knowledge upon Takano (with an optional,
History
1 +
Island aumaua
+
Deadfire Archipelago
,
Arcana
5 or
Religion
5 check).
Then tell him
"The Cornett of Waves is no mere trophy. It deserves a keeper who knows its history."
.
Finally, warn him that
"Ondra punishes those who refuse to let go"
with
Religion
3, otherwise Takano will be reluctant to part with it and you'll have to pay
750
.
Bluff
5 -
Lie to Takano
"I shouldn't say this, but I'm on a mission from the palace. A symbol as important as the cornett would help."
Then pay
500
, or pass either a
History
7 or
Diplomacy
4 check.
Threaten Takano's life
- Requires at least
Intimidate
6 to avoid combat, resulting in Moderate Negative reputation with the Huana. Otherwise the other options will start combat with everyone in the estate.
Hire an assassin
-
Rust
, an assassin in
Delver's Row
can fetch the artifact for you. He'll ask for
800
, or
1,000
if you want to keep Takano alive and a day for him to do the deed. You can use the wait function to skip the time. Curiously, even if you only pay
800
for his services, Takano will still be alive and well at his estate after you fetch the cornett from Rust, and a second Cornett of Waves will still exist in the Takano estate chest. This is probably a bug.
Cornett of Depths
-
Show Takano that you have a special shell too
- This won't get you anywhere,
Aloth
and
Tekēhu
will react to this:
Pride
.
Return to Dereo.
If you have the
Cornett of Waves
in your stash while travelling to or from
Serpent's Crown
, there's a chance you'll encounter
Ateira
, and her group of thugs. Ateira is the one Dereo originally hired to retrieve the cornett. She'll want the
Cornett of Waves
, and will be adamant to fight for it. There's no option to give her the shell without a fight, but you can convince her otherwise with one of the following:
Diplomacy
4,
Diplomacy
6, then
Bluff
3 or reputation
Rational
2 - The cornett is just a worthless shell, and Dereo is just using it to frame someone. Picking any of the other checks will cause her to attack.
Intimidate
4, then
Intimidate
6 - The thugs will make easy pickings. Note that this fails if you have
Benevolent
4 or a paladin of
The Shieldbearers of St. Elcga
, with one the thugs calling you "the softest sap in the Deadfire."
Bluff
4, then
Bluff
6 - Dereo sent you to tie up some loose ends.
With Positive reputation (2) with the
Príncipi sen Patrena
, the thugs recognize that you're in with the Príncipi, and that it's likely that Dereo would send you to clean up.
Having reputation
Honest
2 will likewise result in them believing you.
Or you could just attack her, and get Minor Positive Reputation with Delver's Row Criminals.
Returning to Dereo with the
Cornett of Waves
will gain you Minor Positive reputation with Delver's Row Criminals, and
300
. If you encountered Ateira, you can tell him about it to gain further Minor reputation. At this point you can also push him further about his true motives regarding the cornett with
Intellect
12, but to no avail. You're then instructed to take it to the
Undercroft
below
Delver's Row
.
Cornett of Depths (optional)
If you wish to access the hidden room in Ondra temple, make sure to detour by the
Old City Overlook
and grab the
Cornett of Depths
from the sunken statue near the center of the area.
With
Perception
13, you can hear the calls of the cornett from the pool.
With
Survival
6, you'll notice the suspicious bones clustered around the water's edge.
This scripted interaction requires a character with at least
Constitution
16 to retrieve the conch unharmed - otherwise the character will become
Fatigued
.
After obtaining the conch, you'll be attacked by a bunch of skeletons.
Old City Ruins
Find
Gwenfin
in the
Undercroft
near the entrance to the ancient Huana temple in the northern part, overlooking the bay. With
Athletics
4 you can safely take the skiff shortcut on the main dock. Enter the chamber and inspect the statue. Place the cornett in the statue's hand to open the door. The Temple of Ondra isn't where it should be, so guess who's going to look for it inside? Head deeper into the
Old City
, north through the ruins. The temple you want is in the northwestern corner of the
Old City
proper. Note that while Gwenfin says she is a bruiser, her health is low and she will not fight back if enemies are lured to her so low level parties cannot count on using her for aid. She will die quite rapidly but it does not result in quest failure.
Either the
Cornett of Waves
or the
Cornett of Depths
can open the way to the temple, which is guarded by a
giant cave grub
. Killing it is a major challenge, but you can always sneak by and accomplish your objective.
The giant grub is tough, with lots of health, and has strong but very slow attacks. It also summons other grubs to assist it. The trick to killing it is having a
cipher
cast
Whisper of Treason
on it. The giant grub will be charmed, and start killing the other grubs it summoned, reducing your burden. Similarly, the cipher spell
Pain Block
can help healing your frontline during the long fight.
If you take out the Grub Burrow in the Northernmost corner either before or shortly after the fight begins less grubs will spawn and you only have to deal with the giant grub and its summons.
If you obtained both conchs, place them inside the mural to open the secret wall. This leads to a hidden room containing the unique large shield
Cadhu Scalth
in the locked and trapped sarcophagus (difficulty 7) on the left, and the unique pollaxe
Wahai Pōraga
in the sarcophagus on the right.
To complete the quest, inspect the
Ukaizo
mosaic at the back of the temple. Sneak around the giant cave grub if you didn't kill it.
Return to Gwenfin and then to Dereo to collect your rewards.
Collect your rewards
While talking to Dereo, you can use
Bluff
6 or
History
7 to find out that Ukaizo is sought by the Consuaglo mes Casitas of the Príncipi as a way to provide for themselves, as one of the richest and most fertile lands in the Deadfire. Dereo will dodge the question if you ask with
Insight
4. Uncovering this information allows you to ask for
500
hush money.
Dereo will then reward you with the unique blunderbuss
Kitchen Stove
. You can push him further and ask for a bonus:
Streetwise
4 or
Diplomacy
4 - An additional
200
and Minor Positive reputation with Delver's Row Criminals.
Uncovering Dereo's interest in Ukaizo - An additional
500
and Minor Positive reputation with Delver's Row Criminals.
Before leaving, he'll give you
Príncipi Colors
- presumably handmade by Dereo himself over the course of the quest, as well as the
Triumph of Dereo the Lean
, the only triumph not given to you for defeating an enemy ship.
Gallery

Quest ID
