Hiravias Spiritshifted
Journal
quests\13_twin_elms_elms_reach\13_qst_true_to_form
ID
Objectives
0
True to Form
10000
My traveling companion Hiravias has indicated a desire to speak with the druids that live in and around Twin Elms.
Hiravias is no longer welcome in his own tribe due to their fear of his spiritform - the Autumn Stelgaer. He would welcome the insight of druids from other traditions - preferably traditions that do not have such a hostile reaction to the creature.
1
Investigate the stone tablets within Blood Sands.
10001
We spoke with Naca, mystic of the Blood Sands. Though she was not entirely lucid, she seemed preternaturally aware of Hiravias' spiritform.
Though she knew only a little of Hiravias' condition, she insisted the stone tablets carved in the halls of Blood Sands contain information that Hiravias will find illuminating.
20004
Hiravias read from a stone tablet that spoke of human sacrifice - likening the process to feeding plants to animals and animals to people. The inscription unnerved Hiravias.
20005
We found a stone tablet that spoke of spiritual leaders of the Glanfathans being judged by the Autumn Stelgaer. The writing suggested that the Stelgaer kills unfit anamfatha - destroying their souls in the process.
20006
A stone tablet in the Blood Sands spoke cryptically of souls splitting over the ages - one soul slowly becoming split into many. The tablet also hinted that great power comes from merging souls that were once split from the same origin.
20007
We discovered a stone tablet that told of two men meeting at Burial Isle and being reunited... by way of being eaten by an Autumn Stelgaer.
2
Investigate the stone tablets within Blood Sands.
10002
We spoke with Tamrwn, an Ovate of the Golden in Twin Elms. He was able to sense Hiravias' connection to the Autumn Stelgaer.
Tamrwn reluctantly suggested that we travel to the Blood Sands and read the stone tablets carved into the cavernous expanse. He hinted that the tablets contained information of great use to Hiravias.
20008
Hiravias read from a stone tablet that spoke of human sacrifice - likening the process to feeding plants to animals and animals to people. The inscription unnerved Hiravias.
20009
We found a stone tablet that spoke of spiritual leaders of the Glanfathans being judged by the Autumn Stelgaer. The writing suggested that the Stelgaer kills unfit anamfatha - destroying their souls in the process.
20010
A stone tablet in the Blood Sands spoke cryptically of souls splitting over the ages - one soul slowly becoming split into many. The tablet also hinted that great power comes from merging souls that were once split from the same origin.
20011
We discovered a stone tablet that told of two men meeting at Burial Isle and being reunited... by way of being eaten by an Autumn Stelgaer.
3
Explore the Burial Isle.
10003
Hiravias read the parable written on the stones of the Blood Sands. The story spoke of the Autumn Stelgaer being sent to test anamfatha of their worth - those that fail are consumed by the Stelgaer so that their weakness does not live on and multiply.
The tablets also mentioned that many anamfatha would travel to the Burial Isle to meditate and be tested by these Autumn Stelgaers. Hiravias now wishes to explore the Burial Isle for one of these pilgrimage sites.
4
Investigate the stone tablets within Blood Sands.
10004
We have spoken with both Naca of the Ethik Nôl of Blood Sands, and with Tamrwn of the Ovates of the Golden Grove. Both of these druids were passingly familiar with Hiravias' spiritform, and both recommended reading the tablets in the Blood Sands - insisting the tablets contained wisdom Hiravias needed to read for himself.
20000
Hiravias read from a stone tablet that spoke of human sacrifice - likening the process to feeding plants to animals and animals to people. The inscription unnerved Hiravias.
20001
We found a stone tablet that spoke of spiritual leaders of the Glanfathans being judged by the Autumn Stelgaer. The writing suggested that the Stelgaer kills unfit anamfatha - destroying their souls in the process.
20002
A stone tablet in the Blood Sands spoke cryptically of souls splitting over the ages - one soul slowly becoming split into many. The tablet also hinted that great power comes from merging souls that were once split from the same origin.
20003
We discovered a stone tablet that told of two men meeting at Burial Isle and being reunited... by way of being eaten by an Autumn Stelgaer.
5
10005
6
Find a druid who knows more about the Autumn Stelgaer.
10006
Hiravias revealed that he was cast out of his circle because they considered his spiritshift form, the Autumn Stelgaer, an abomination. He wants to learn more about it, and feels that there may be a druid in Twin Elms who would know more.
End states
Wael's Peace
30000
On Burial Isle, we encountered Scathden, another druid capable of assuming the Autumn Stelgaer form. Though a fight seemed inevitable, we managed to walk away from the meeting without a fight. Hiravias can't change the fact that his tribe resents his spiritform, but he seems happier knowing why it all happened - because Galawain 'gifted' him with a little too much hunter potential...
Wael's Champion
30001
On Burial Isle, we encountered Scathden, another druid capable of assuming the Autumn Stelgaer form. Scathden and Hiravias have souls that split from the same ancestor, and Scathden was insistent on seeing who was the stronger half - Hiravias obliged, now Scathden is no more. Hiravias praised Wael for guiding him to some answers - he seems much more at ease now that he knows his mauling was a 'gift' - albeit a difficult one to bear.
Galawain's Champion
30002
On Burial Isle, we encountered Scathden, another druid capable of assuming the Autumn Stelgaer form. Scathden and Hiravias have souls that split from the same ancestor, and Scathden was insistent on seeing who was the stronger half - Hiravias obliged, now Scathden is no more. Now that he understands that his mauling was a gift (albeit a painful one), Hiravias seems to have reconciled his faith in Galawain.
QUEST FAILED
30003
We were unable to fully uncover the story behind Hiravias' near-death experience. Perhaps he will come to these answers in his own time.
QUEST FAILED
30004
With Hiravias dead, there seems little need to further investigate his personal history.

Quest ID
companion_qst_aloth_and_iselmyr
“
I've tracked Iselmyr's essence throughout the exchange. She had a particularly high density index during the most heated portions of their argument, and her essence seemed to localize most clearly in the lower portion of the subject's left ribcage. That's right around the spleen, which, of course, means that she is triggered by black bile. No doubt the subject's characteristic melancholy is to blame.
”
“
That's utter horseshit.
”
~
Bellasege
and
Aloth
Two-Sided
is a
side quest
, and
companion quest
for
Aloth
in
Pillars of Eternity
. It is obtained after completing
Never Far from the Queen
by speaking with Aloth.
Synopsis
Aloth revealed that an Awakened soul, Iselmyr, dwells within him. He's asked for help in finding an expert who understands his predicament.
Walkthrough
After
Never Far from the Queen
, Aloth will come clean, explaining that his soul is also awakened. His awakening however, is a presence named Iselmyr that shares his body and manifests from time to time to voice her opinions. Aloth says that he has heard there is an expert on awakenings in
Defiance Bay
, and that maybe they could help him, starting the quest.
Travel to the
Brackenbury
district, enter the
Sanitarium
, and head downstairs.
Talk to
Bellasege
and volunteer Aloth as a test subject, despite his hesitancy.
Bellasege states that in order to draw out the 'interloper,' Aloth must answer personal questions. How you guide Aloth though this dialog, and
several others like it
, can affect
his ending
.
Dialogue
Aloth Trait
Iselmyr Favor
"You can't let her make these decisions for you."
+1 Authority
-1 Favor
"I think she's trying to help you stand up for yourself."
+1 Autonomy
+1 Favor
"It's not clear yet whether this is a help or hindrance."
No change
No change
The player can either allow Aloth to steal Bellasege's notes, stay neutral, or tell him not to, concluding the quest.
Notes
This quest fails after the meeting with the duc during the main quest
The Hermit of Hadret House
, if you haven't completed the first objective.
Aloth mentions that he has none of the memories of Iselmyr, and that judging by her accent and dialect, she is from much further in the past.
Aloth mentions that when you first saw him having an argument with the villagers of
The Gilded Vale
, it was because of Iselmyr.
Gallery
You Aedyrans are so uptight

is a

in

violently

Walkthrough
This quest is started by entering the Collections wing and will require the player to reach

, accessible through the

. Killing it will complete the quest and grant access to the aperture required to complete

.

Outcomes and rewards
Turn off the machine
Defiance Bay
: Major Positive reputation
St. Garam's Spark
Destroy the machine
Defiance Bay
: Extraordinary Positive reputation

Defiance Bay
: Moderate Positive reputation

Related quests
Previous:
Never Far from the Queen
Next:
The Hermit of Hadret House
Concurrent:
The Man Who Waits
Through Death's Gate
Technical
Quest ID
cp_qst_heritage_hill
Undying Heritage
is a
main quest
in
Pillars of Eternity
.
Synopsis
The Acolyte
showed the Watcher a vision of an ancient tower in a derelict district of
Defiance Bay
. There was a strange man in the tower working on some kind of machine. There was also a woman with a scar over her heart. She may hold the key to whatever happened there. The Leaden Key was interested in this tower for a reason. The Watcher needs to understand what it does if they are to figure out their plans.
Walkthrough
You automatically get this quest after you complete
Never Far from the Queen
.
Head to
Heritage Hill
, which is accessible from the
First Fires
district. The gate will be locked and the guards will tell you to speak to
Wyla
at the
Crucible Keep
back in
First Fires
.
There are several dialogue options that enable you to bypass this, such as bribing the guards.
Alternatively, you can speak to Wyla before going to Heritage Hill to avoid back-tracking.
Once in, make your way to the
tower
, located in the southwestern corner of the map.
Talk with
Aldhelm
at the top level, who will tell you to speak with
Icantha
. He will also ask you to send him something tasty, and promises to tell you a secret about the machine if you do.
Exit the tower and proceed to
Icantha's House
, which is directly north of the tower.
Speak with Icantha and convince her to share her knowledge of how the tower works. Most dialogue will lead to you convincing her to give you the control words.
Optional: Send Aldhelm a victim for him to feed upon.
If you have cleared the undead from Heritage Hill (though this isn't a strict requirement), you can free the prisoners in the back room of
Icantha's House
for a Defiance Bay: Minor Positive reputation bump. Or, if you learned of his hunger, you can trick them into going to the top of the tower where Aldhelm will feed on them. This action results in a lower cruel reputation increase than sending
Saeda
.
Alternatively, you can trick
Saeda
to the same end, if you haven't completed
Safe Haven
.
If you sent Aldhelm something to eat, he teaches you how to absorb all the souls from the machine. Doing so gives your main character the talent
Gift from the Machine
(+1
Might
, +5% maximum
endurance
), increases your Cruel reputation, and gives you Defiance Bay: Moderate Positive.
This talent carries over to
Pillars of Eternity II: Deadfire
, and will be granted by completing the main quest
A Distant Light
on
Hasongo
. The effects are the same.
Return to the tower and choose a resolution.
Turn off the machine.
Gives Defiance Bay: Major Positive
Overload the machine.
Gives Defiance Bay: Extraordinary Positive.
Absorb the souls.
Gives Defiance Bay: Moderate Positive, major
Cruel
disposition, and
Gift from the Machine
. This also turns off the machine.
Note: How you choose to deal with the machine affects the
end-game slides
for Defiance Bay.
Reward
When nearing
Hadret House
in
Brackenbury
, you will be approached by the courier of a lady – who wishes to remain anonymous – and rewarded with
St. Garam's Spark
, a unique
pistol
.
Journal

Upon New Shores
is a
quest
in

Synopsis
I found
Masog
's spirit along a walkway within the
Serpent's Crown
. He had been pushed from the walkway, and was understandably distraught about the experience. I told him I would try to track down who had done this to him.
Behind the scenes
This quest is incomplete, and while journal entries for it exist, it seems that there is no way to start it.
The global variable
n_Upon_New_Shores_Main_State
is used for this quest, but doesn't seem to be set or queried anywhere.

is a
main quest
in
Pillars of Eternity
.
Synopsis
After surviving an explosion outside of the ruins of Cilant Lîs, the Watcher experienced a vision... Since then, they have been hearing sounds with no source, and seeing things that aren't there. Perhaps some rest and an animancer can help?
Walkthrough

and
the Black Hound Inn
. After resting in a room, you will be presented with a special scripted interaction. The hanged dwarven animancer is your next stop.

Caldara de Berranzi
.

Wasting Minds
[FS]

A Rising Star
Technical
Quest ID
lax03_tsk_02_celestial_magic_01
Guid
d6ed06f8-2a0b-4b78-bd4e-8eac68d6ca67
Wasting Minds
is a
task
in
The Forgotten Sanctum
.
Synopsis
Bekarna
has been imprisoned by the Hand Occult and stripped of her mind. To save her, the Watcher needs to find some thing that can help her focus, something related to her lifelong fascination with stargazing...
Walkthrough
Bekarna is of little help in figuring out what to find, but that doesn't matter. What you need to grab is
Advanced Wistful Stargazing
, located in the
Hall of Reconfiguration
, in the Alcove of Incision to the northwest. Returning it to Bekarna will help set her mind straight and initiate the next quest, where she'll ask for her grimoire.
Be sure to return her book before engaging the
Memory Hoarder
inside
The Assembly of Echoes
. Upon starting the interaction with it, the door back up will be barriered off.
If you use Llengrath's scroll instead of Thayn's on the Memory Hoarder's Organ, without restoring her mind first, Bekarna will be among those euthanized by it.

Outcomes and rewards
Finish the quest
Shame or Glory
Crucible Knight Officer Armor
Second Skin Talent
Adra
Related quests
Previous:
Built to Last
Next:
-
Concurrent:
The Hermit of Hadret House
Technical
Quest ID
02_qst_winds_of_steel
Winds of Steel
is a
side quest
in
Pillars of Eternity
.
Warning:
Accepting this quest means choosing the Knights of the Crucible as your faction in
Defiance Bay
, meaning you can no longer ally with nor perform quests for
House Doemenel
or the
The Dozens
.
Synopsis
Commander Clyver
's
forge knights
seem to have been a success. In fact, the
High Justice
wants to expand by having his own forge masters build more forge knights at
Fleetbreaker Castle
. Commander Clyver asks the Watcher to deliver the research to one of the High Justice's commanders at
Anslog's Compass
.
Walkthrough
You will receive this quest from Commander Clyver at
Crucible Keep
after completing
Built to Last
.
Meet
Aranroed
by the docks in
Anslög's Compass
. She's located to the southeast, near the Dial. Defeat the mercenaries who show up under
Padebald
's command.
Go back to Crucible Keep and defeat the rogue forge knights.
Report back to Commander Clyver. You can choose to encourage him to continue his research or convince him otherwise.
If
The Blade of the Endless Paths
is not yet complete, DO NOT talk to Dunstan at this point. You must exit the keep, then re-enter in order for the quest to complete properly. If you give him the blade parts while he is in the war room, the quest may bug and you will never receive the completed blade.
Notes
Accepting this quest bars you from taking
The Changing of the Guard
(for House Doemenel) or
The Bronze Beneath the Lake
(for The Dozens).
Completing this quest allows you to get an invitation to the
Animancy hearings
from the
Knights of the Crucible
.
If any knights die from the forge knights, they may drop
Crucible plate armor
.
Your choice to encourage Clyver to continue his Forge Knight research or not affects the
end-game slides
for Defiance Bay.
Journal
quests\02_defiance_bay_first_fires\02_qst_winds_of_steel

Anslög's Compass is a lagoon just north of Defiance Bay. Commander Clyver told me that the High Justice's courier, a commander named Aranroed, will be waiting there. Sending a ship directly to Defiance Bay would likely attract the attention of the Dozens.
